# Tekla.Structures.Model

Auto-generated from vendor docs for tekla 2026.0. 304 types in this namespace.

## Assembly (class)

The Assembly class defines a single manufacturing unit: objects that are bolted or welded together in the workshop. An assembly has a main part and secondary assemblables attached to it. The number of secondaries is limited to 2048. Hierarchical assemblies can also have subassemblies attached to them and they can have a parent assembly instance.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/05895e96-9746-fce3-380b-7892400ca1b8)

### Constructors
- `public Assembly()` — Creates a new assembly instance.

### Methods
#### `public bool Add(ArrayList Assemblables)`

Adds an array list of given assemblable instances to the assembly. For assemblies use Add(Assembly Assembly). Notice that adding other than assembly instances will actually assume that you are adding objects to a cast unit assembly.

**Parameters:**
- `Assemblables` (System.Collections.ArrayList) — A list of assemblables to add to the assembly.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True on success, otherwise false.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/14444602-aeba-c719-f883-59fc8432805f)

#### `public bool Add(Assembly Assembly)`

Adds a new subassembly for the assembly instance.

**Parameters:**
- `Assembly` (Tekla.Structures.Model.Assembly) — The assembly instance to be added.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if the subassembly was successfully added.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/07ace982-ded4-7674-5bbc-b6e3f5823e0e)

#### `public bool Add(IAssemblable Object)`

Adds the given assemblable instance to the assembly. Notice that adding other than assembly instances will assume that you are adding objects to a cast unit assembly.

**Parameters:**
- `Object` (Tekla.Structures.Model.IAssemblable) — The part instance to add to the assembly.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True on success, otherwise false.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/2a844691-427f-aded-4ec7-ae07ae95db63)

#### `public bool CompareTo(Assembly AssemblyToCompare)`

Compares the instantiated assembly with another one.

**Remarks:** If the numbering hasn't been performed the function will also return false.

**Parameters:**
- `AssemblyToCompare` (Tekla.Structures.Model.Assembly) — The assembly to compare with.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if there are no differences, false otherwise.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/22871c43-1a15-bb3b-862c-d8a2e5e5f7bd)

#### `public int CompareTo(Object obj)`

Compares Identifiers of model objects.

**Parameters:**
- `obj` (System.Object) — The model object to be used in comparison.

**Returns:** `Int32` — 0 if object Identifiers are the same, 1 if given object Identifier is bigger, -1 otherwise.

#### `public bool HasUserDefinedCoordSys()`

Returns whether the assembly has a user defined coordinate system.

**Remarks:** Currently only supported with rebar assemblies.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if the assembly has a user defined coordinate system.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/e6a5faaa-31e1-6978-4d97-e1d53ddb47ea)

#### `public override bool Insert()`

Inserts the Assembly instance in the model. At the moment only rebar assemblies can be inserted. The main object of the assembly must be a Reinforcement or RebarSet. If the main rebar is not set (null), the main rebar will be automatically set to the secondary rebar with the biggest weight.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if successful; otherwise false.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/267fc42c-acb4-f8ab-686a-da9747e9e12f)

#### `public override bool Modify()`

Modifies the assembly instance in the model.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True on success.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/a1dcbb64-dc3b-ad05-9541-ad0f9d4055bf)

#### `public bool Remove(ModelObject Object)`

Removes the given instance from the assembly. Before removing steel parts from an assembly all connecting workshop welds and bolts must be deleted.

**Parameters:**
- `Object` (Tekla.Structures.Model.ModelObject) — The model object to remove from the assembly.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if successfully removed.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/f2b5be55-4710-68f1-81d2-cc2651a52c26)

#### `public override bool Select()`

Selects the assembly instance from the model. The identifier of the instance must be set.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True on success.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/3127bb46-b578-c2b8-14aa-cd30a76a1c6e)

### Properties
- `AssemblyNumber` (NumberingSeries, get/set) — Defines the numbering in the assembly sense. If the members are empty and zero, the main part attributes are used.
- `Identifier` (Identifier, get/set) — The identifier of the object.
- `IsUpToDate` (object, get) — Gets if the object does not have a modification which is not shared.
- `ModificationTime` (object, get) — Gets latest time of the object was modified or created.
- `Name` (String, get/set) — The name of the assembly.

## Assembly.AssemblyTypeEnum (enum)

The assembly types.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/b5783436-5017-c594-0f5f-3c437e0e1108)

### Values
- `STEEL_ASSEMBLY` = `0` — The steel assembly type.
- `PRECAST_ASSEMBLY` = `1` — The precast assembly type.
- `IN_SITU_ASSEMBLY` = `2` — The in-situ assembly type.
- `TIMBER_ASSEMBLY` = `3` — The timber assembly type.
- `UNKNOWN_ASSEMBLY` = `4` — The unknown assembly type.
- `REBAR_ASSEMBLY` = `5` — The rebar assembly type.

## BaseComponent (class)

The BaseComponent class is an abstract base class that represents generic components. The generic components derived from the base component are the component, the connection, the detail and the seam.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/3f5c4af6-b37c-5f30-9ce4-ea5f2bf028fe)

### Constructors
- `public BaseComponent()` — Creates a base component instance with default attributes.

- `values` (System.Collections.Hashtable.) — The return value, a hash table list that contains all the values that were retrieved successfully.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if at least one single property was retrieved successfully.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/0708f404-ce77-28e1-4ebe-fa906f68bff8)

#### `public bool LoadAttributesFromFile(string Filename)`

Loads the attributes for the component from the given file. These attributes will be loaded before all the attributes that have been set with the SetAttribute methods, so any attributes that are set with SetAttribute will override those loaded from the given standard file.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if a file name is specified.

### Properties
- `Identifier` (Identifier, get/set) — The identifier of the object.
- `IsUpToDate` (object, get) — Gets if the object does not have a modification which is not shared.
- `ModificationTime` (object, get) — Gets latest time of the object was modified or created.
- `Name` (String, get/set) — The name of the component. The name identifies custom components or plug-ins.
- `Number` (Int32, get/set) — The number of the component. A number greater than zero identifies system components, for custom components the number is CUSTOM_OBJECT_NUMBER, and for plug-ins the number is PLUGIN_OBJECT_NUMBER.

## BasePoint (class)

The BasePoint class provides base point related functionalities. Base points can be retrieved using ProjectInfo class.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/5c1b45f5-9a0b-cd81-ec51-dd4e21ff20ce)

### Constructors
- `public BasePoint(string name)` — Initializes a new instance of the BasePoint class.
- `public BasePoint(int id, Guid guid, string initialGuid, string name, string description, string coordinateSystem, double northSouth, double eastWest, double elevation, double latitude, double longitude, double locationInModelX, double locationInModelY, double locationInModelZ, double angleToNorth, bool isProjectBasePoint, bool isCurrentBasePoint)` — Initializes a new instance of the BasePoint class.

### Methods
#### `public static Point ConvertFromBasePoint(BasePoint basePoint, Point point)`

Converts the given base point point to local point.

**Parameters:**
- `basePoint` (Tekla.Structures.Model.BasePoint) — The base point, from which the point is converted from.
- `point` (Tekla.Structures.Geometry3d.Point) — The point to be converted.

**Returns:** `Point` — On success, the converted value. Otherwise the original value.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/405c560b-8772-065c-7a63-e474d21592c2)

#### `public Point ConvertFromBasePoint(Point point)`

Converts the given point from this base point to local point.

**Parameters:**
- `point` (Tekla.Structures.Geometry3d.Point) — The point to be converted.

**Returns:** `Point` — On success, the converted value. Otherwise the original value.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/69941ebe-3291-9807-b9ea-24339052ba16)

#### `public static Point ConvertToBasePoint(BasePoint basePoint, Point point)`

Converts the given local point to given base point.

**Parameters:**
- `basePoint` (Tekla.Structures.Model.BasePoint) — The base point, to which the point is converted to.
- `point` (Tekla.Structures.Geometry3d.Point) — The point to be converted.

**Returns:** `Point` — On success, the converted value. Otherwise the original value.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/1ff21f4d-0da5-7345-9494-dd1bddcd9816)

#### `public Point ConvertToBasePoint(Point point)`

Converts the given local point to this base point.

**Parameters:**
- `point` (Tekla.Structures.Geometry3d.Point) — The point to be converted.

**Returns:** `Point` — On success, the converted value. Otherwise the original value.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/07421afb-80e7-8279-e0b8-aa31206d6ff9)

#### `public bool Delete()`

Deletes the base point from the model database.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True on success, otherwise false.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/59b13ff7-fc18-dfed-6c0d-34efd5302110)

#### `public Tuple<bool, int, int, int> GetCompoundPlaneAngleLatitude()`

Gets the Latitude as a compound plane angle.

**Returns:** `Tuple<Boolean,Int32,Int32,Int32>` — compound plane angle integers. The first param is true, if the value successfully calculated.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/fb80f506-4793-911c-ed0f-32e7ddf5da12)

#### `public Tuple<bool, int, int, int> GetCompoundPlaneAngleLongitude()`

Gets the Longitude as a compound plane angle.

**Returns:** `Tuple<Boolean,Int32,Int32,Int32>` — compound plane angle integers. The first param is true, if the value successfully calculated.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/303a3cf2-8ce2-2cb7-84c4-77cdce557ca8)

#### `public CoordinateSystem GetCoordinateSystem(BasePoint.CoordinateSystemType CoordsysType = BasePoint.CoordinateSystemType.GLOBAL)`

Gets the coordinate system of the base point.

**Parameters:**
- `CoordsysType` (Tekla.Structures.Model.BasePoint.CoordinateSystemType) — Optional: Type of the coordinate system.

**Returns:** `CoordinateSystem` — The coordinate system of the base point.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/230d2dc0-2288-59b4-4175-93eae8271817)

#### `public bool Insert()`

Inserts the base point into the model database.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True on success, otherwise false.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/74097064-a8ad-b2bc-4fd1-7ed932b55ba3)

#### `public bool Modify()`

Modifies the base point in the model database.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True on success, otherwise false.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/a90914f1-1429-f8e1-bd91-94506e890222)

#### `public IDisposable SetAsCurrent()`

Sets this base point as current base point point until the token is disposed of. Can be used for example when retrieving report values according to this base point.

**Returns:** `IDisposable` — The disposable token that removes this base points current base point status.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/35ba04bd-51ac-d3ed-cd44-0c518bbb97a4)

### Properties
- `AngleToNorth` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the angle to north in radians.
- `CoordinateSystem` (String, get/set) — Gets or sets the coordinate system.
- `Description` (String, get/set) — Gets or sets the description.
- `EastWest` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the east-west.
- `Elevation` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the elevation.
- `Guid` (Guid, get/set) — Gets or sets the guid.
- `Id` (Int32, get/set) — Gets or sets the id.
- `InitialGuid` (String, get/set) — Gets or sets the initial guid. A metadata field to store for example some external applications base point guid. Not used in itself in any functionality.
- `IsCurrentBasePoint` (Boolean, get/set) — Gets or sets the value indicating if this base point is the current base point.
- `IsLocked` (Boolean, get/set) — Gets or sets the value indicating if this base point is locked.
- `IsProjectBasePoint` (Boolean, get/set) — Gets or sets the value indicating if this base point is the project base point.
- `IsScopedCurrentBasePoint` (Boolean, get) — Gets the value indicating whether this base point is the scoped current base point. That means, a temporary base point set with the SetAsCurrent method, and changed back when returned token is disposed of.
- `Latitude` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the latitude.
- `LocationInModelX` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the location in model x.
- `LocationInModelY` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the location in model y.
- `LocationInModelZ` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the location in model z.
- `Longitude` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the longitude.
- `Name` (String, get/set) — Gets or sets the name.
- `NorthSouth` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the north-south.

## BasePoint.CoordinateSystemType (enum)

The type of the base points coordinate system.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/f91ad4ef-a27d-ecfe-2d60-5cc8fcdfbdd3)

### Values
- `GLOBAL` = `1` — Coordinate system in global.
- `WORKPLANE` = `2` — Coordinate system in work plane.

## BaseRebarGroup (class)

The BaseRebarGroup class is an abstract base class that represents reinforcing bar group classes. The reinforcing bar groups derived from the base group are the rebar group, the circled rebar group and the curved rebar group.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/2102980d-739e-ab9a-1311-c4c494dc6d42)

### Methods
#### `public int CompareTo(Object obj)`

Compares Identifiers of model objects.

**Parameters:**
- `obj` (System.Object) — The model object to be used in comparison.

**Returns:** `Int32` — 0 if object Identifiers are the same, 1 if given object Identifier is bigger, -1 otherwise.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/79cca356-0c8a-e8fb-463a-34ad4141bec7)

#### `public abstract bool Delete()`

Deletes the instance from the model database.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True on success.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/0b922776-ccee-4b5e-4a12-4e5306802e90)

#### `public bool Equals(ModelObject other)`

Check if Identifiers of model objects are same.

**Parameters:**
- `other` (Tekla.Structures.Model.ModelObject) — The model object to be used in comparison.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if object Identifiers are the same, false otherwise,

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/e4324265-2490-00de-4139-63a8f7acb492)

#### `public bool GetAllReportProperties(ArrayList stringNames, ArrayList doubleNames, ArrayList integerNames, ref Hashtable values)`

Retrieves all properties for the model object in one hashtable. Type for the returned value must be checked using type casting.

**Parameters:**
- `stringNames` (System.Collections.ArrayList) — An array list containing the names of the string properties.
- `doubleNames` (System.Collections.ArrayList) — An array list containing the names of the double properties.
- `integerNames` (System.Collections.ArrayList) — An array list containing the names of the integer properties.
- `values` (System.Collections.Hashtable.) — The return value, a hash table list that contains all the values that were retrieved successfully.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if at least one single property was retrieved successfully.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/0708f404-ce77-28e1-4ebe-fa906f68bff8)

#### `public List<RebarComplexGeometry> GetRebarComplexGeometries(bool withHooks, bool withoutClashes, bool lengthAdjustments)`

Retrieves a list of physical reinforcing bars (of type RebarComplexGeometry). These objects contain physical curves in the 3D space of each reinforcing bar as shown in model view. RebarComplexGeometry only differs from RebarGeometry by containing a polycurve rather than a polyline.

**Parameters:**
- `withHooks` (System.Boolean) — A parameter to determine whether the hook points should be included in the rebar geometry or not.
- `withoutClashes` (System.Boolean) — A parameter to determine whether self-clash avoidance should be included in the rebar geometry or not.
- `lengthAdjustments` (System.Boolean) — A parameter to determine whether rounding and step tapering should be included in the rebar geometry or not.

**Returns:** `List<RebarComplexGeometry>` — A list of RebarComplexGeometrys.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/c796aafb-3520-fe06-12e3-a07a87c8f2f9)

#### `public List<RebarComplexGeometry> GetRebarComplexGeometries(bool withHooks, bool withoutClashes, bool lengthAdjustments, Reinforcement.RebarGeometrySimplificationTypeEnum simplified)`

Retrieves a list of physical reinforcing bars (of type RebarComplexGeometry). These objects contain physical curves in the 3D space of each reinforcing bar as shown in model view. RebarComplexGeometry only differs from RebarGeometry by containing a polycurve (of Arc and LineSegment objects) rather than a polyline. The polycurve only contains arcs if the "simplified" parameter is RATIONALIZED or FABRICATION. If "simplified" is NONE, any arcs will be returned as a series of line segments.

**Parameters:**
- `withHooks` (System.Boolean) — A parameter to determine whether the hook points should be included in the rebar geometry or not.
- `withoutClashes` (System.Boolean) — A parameter to determine whether self-clash avoidance should be included in the rebar geometry or not.
- `lengthAdjustments` (System.Boolean) — A parameter to determine whether rounding and step tapering should be included in the rebar geometry or not.
- `simplified` (Tekla.Structures.Model.Reinforcement.RebarGeometrySimplificationTypeEnum) — A parameter to determine whether the "simplified" rebar shape recognition geometry should be returned.

**Returns:** `List<RebarComplexGeometry>` — A list of RebarComplexGeometrys.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/84a3a38b-d7eb-97df-7fba-a34d13de6610)

#### `public ArrayList GetRebarGeometries(bool withHooks)`

Retrieves a list of physical reinforcing bars (of type RebarGeometry). These objects contain physical points in the 3D space of each reinforcing bar.

**Parameters:**
- `withHooks` (System.Boolean) — A parameter to determine whether the hook points should be included in the rebar geometry or not.

**Returns:** `ArrayList` — An array list that contains the RebarGeometry objects.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/75649a4c-6252-09a1-b509-cd815e718c0f)

#### `public ArrayList GetRebarGeometriesWithoutClashes(bool withHooks)`

Retrieves a list of physical reinforcing bars (of type RebarGeometry). These objects contain physical points in the 3D space of each reinforcing bar as shown in model view. In case rebar polygon clashes with itself, physical points are moved to avoid clashing.

**Parameters:**
- `withHooks` (System.Boolean) — A parameter to determine whether the hook points should be included in the rebar geometry or not.

**Returns:** `ArrayList` — An array list that contains the RebarGeometry objects.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/470acf1f-1beb-f30a-a1c2-2fcab066ab4a)

#### `public RebarGeometry GetSingleRebar(int index, bool withHooks)`

Returns a single rebar inside the rebar group located by the given index. The indexing starts from the start point. The rebar represents a physical reinforcing bar and contains physical points in the 3D space of the bar. The method returns null on error, for example if given an erroneous index. The number of rebars in the group can be requested with GetNumberOfRebars().

**Parameters:**
- `index` (System.Int32) — The index of the rebar in the group.
- `withHooks` (System.Boolean) — A parameter to determine whether the hook points should be included in the rebar geometry or not.

**Returns:** `RebarGeometry` — The instance of a RebarGeometry object that contains the point list of the single physical reinforcing bar. Null if the given index was not valid.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/41181569-65f8-f4ba-7722-3cd890c7a3d4)

#### `public RebarGeometry GetSingleRebarWithoutClash(int index, bool withHooks)`

Returns a single rebar inside the rebar group located by the given index. The indexing starts from the start point. The rebar represents a physical reinforcing bar and contains physical, non-clashing points in the 3D space of the bar. The method returns null on error, for example if given an erroneous index. The number of rebars in the group can be requested with GetNumberOfRebars().

**Parameters:**
- `index` (System.Int32) — The index of the rebar in the group.
- `withHooks` (System.Boolean) — A parameter to determine whether the hook points should be included in the rebar geometry or not.

**Returns:** `RebarGeometry` — The instance of a RebarGeometry object that contains the point list of the single physical reinforcing bar. Null if the given index was not valid.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/8fd2e21a-3d2f-6211-92ba-9d6e652ac100)

### Properties
- `Class` (Int32, get/set) — Gets or sets the class of the reinforcement. The class is used to group reinforcements.
- `EndFromPlaneOffset` (Double, get/set) — The end offset value from the part surface.
- `EndHook` (RebarHookData, get/set) — The hook at the end of the reinforcing bar.
- `EndPoint` (Point, get/set) — The end point of the direction in which the bars are distributed.
- `EndPointOffsetType` (Reinforcement.RebarOffsetTypeEnum, get/set) — Gets or sets the type of the end point offset. The options are: OFFSET_TYPE_LEG_LENGTHOFFSET_TYPE_COVER_THICKNESS
- `EndPointOffsetValue` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the concrete cover thickness or leg length at the second end of the bar.
- `ExcludeType` (BaseRebarGroup.ExcludeTypeEnum, get/set) — Defines which bars to omit from the group. The options are: EXCLUDE_TYPE_NONEEXCLUDE_TYPE_FIRSTEXCLUDE_TYPE_LASTEXCLUDE_TYPE_BOTH
- `Father` (ModelObject, get/set) — Gets or sets the father object of the reinforcement; the model object instance to operate on.
- `FromPlaneOffset` (Double, get/set) — The offset value from the part surface applied in both sides.
- `Grade` (String, get/set) — Gets or sets the steel grade of the reinforcing bar. The grade indicates the strength of the steel used in reinforcing bars. It can also indicate other factors, such as the weldability or surface deformations of the bar.
- `Identifier` (Identifier, get/set) — The identifier of the object.
- `InputPointDeformingState` (DeformingType, get/set) — Gets or sets the reinforcement input point deforming state.
- `IsUpToDate` (object, get) — Gets if the object does not have a modification which is not shared.
- `ModificationTime` (object, get) — Gets latest time of the object was modified or created.
- `Name` (String, get/set) — Gets or sets the name of the reinforcement.
- `NumberingSeries` (NumberingSeries, get/set) — Gets or sets the numbering series of the reinforcement.
- `OnPlaneOffsets` (ArrayList, get/set) — Gets or sets the double offset value for each leg on the same plane as the bar.
- `RadiusValues` (ArrayList, get/set) — Gets or sets the radius value(s) of the bends in the bar.
- `Size` (String, get/set) — The size of the reinforcement.
- `Spacings` (ArrayList, get/set) — The spacing value(s). If the type of the spacing is SPACING_TYPE_EXACT_NUMBER Spacings has only one value that defines the number of the reinforcing bars.
- `SpacingType` (BaseRebarGroup.RebarGroupSpacingTypeEnum, get/set) — The type of spacing. The options are (BaseRebarGroup.RebarGroupSpacingTypeEnum.): SPACING_TYPE_UNDEFINEDSPACING_TYPE_EXACT_SPACINGSSPACING_TYPE_EXACT_NUMBERSPACING_TYPE_TARGET_SPACESPACING_TYPE_EXACT_SPACE_FLEX_AT_STARTSPACING_TYPE_EXACT_SPACE_FLEX_AT_ENDSPACING_TYPE_EXACT_SPACE_FLEX_AT_BOTHSPACING_TYPE_EXACT_SPACE_FLEX_AT_MIDDLE
- `StartFromPlaneOffset` (Double, get/set) — The start offset value from the part surface.
- `StartHook` (RebarHookData, get/set) — The hook at the beginning of the reinforcing bar.
- `StartPoint` (Point, get/set) — The start point of the direction in which the bars are distributed.
- `StartPointOffsetType` (Reinforcement.RebarOffsetTypeEnum, get/set) — Gets or sets the type of the start point offset is either OFFSET_TYPE_LEG_LENGTH or OFFSET_TYPE_COVER_THICKNESS.
- `StartPointOffsetValue` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the concrete cover thickness or leg length at the first end of the bar.

## BaseRebarGroup.ExcludeTypeEnum (enum)

The different ways to exclude reinforcing bars from the group.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/46465095-11b7-c672-27b6-5cf5578ea1fc)

### Values
- `EXCLUDE_TYPE_NONE` = `1` — All the reinforcing bars are included.
- `EXCLUDE_TYPE_FIRST` = `2` — The first reinforcing bar is not to be created to the group.
- `EXCLUDE_TYPE_LAST` = `3` — The last reinforcing bar is not to be created to the group.
- `EXCLUDE_TYPE_BOTH` = `4` — The first and last reinforcing bars are not to be created to the group.

## BaseRebarGroup.RebarGroupSpacingTypeEnum (enum)

The ways to distribute the reinforcing bars with different spacings.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/30e7f555-8816-41d5-f79f-e902c423bc81)

### Values
- `SPACING_TYPE_UNDEFINED` = `0` — The undefined spacing type.
- `SPACING_TYPE_EXACT_SPACINGS` = `1` — The bars are distributed using the spacing values.
- `SPACING_TYPE_EXACT_NUMBER` = `2` — Equal distribution by the number of reinforcing bars.
- `SPACING_TYPE_TARGET_SPACE` = `3` — Equal distribution by the target spacing value.
- `SPACING_TYPE_EXACT_SPACE_FLEX_AT_START` = `4` — Distribution by the exact spacing value with a flexible first space.
- `SPACING_TYPE_EXACT_SPACE_FLEX_AT_END` = `5` — Distribution by the exact spacing value with a flexible last space.
- `SPACING_TYPE_EXACT_SPACE_FLEX_AT_BOTH` = `6` — Distribution by the exact spacing value with flexible first and last spaces.
- `SPACING_TYPE_EXACT_SPACE_FLEX_AT_MIDDLE` = `7` — Distribution by the exact spacing value with a flexible middle space.

## BaseRebarModifier (class)

Represents a modifier that can modify the characteristics of RebarSet reinforcing bars that pass through it.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/f2d8320f-58b5-3669-bde6-a6bdf71d6062)

#### `public int CompareTo(Object obj)`

Compares Identifiers of model objects.

**Parameters:**
- `obj` (System.Object) — The model object to be used in comparison.

**Returns:** `Int32` — 0 if object Identifiers are the same, 1 if given object Identifier is bigger, -1 otherwise.

### Properties
- `BarsAffected` (BaseRebarModifier.BarsAffectedEnum, get/set) — Gets or sets the reinforcing bars affected.
- `Curve` (Contour, get/set) — Gets or sets the geometric Contour of the rebar modifier.
- `Father` (RebarSet, get/set) — Gets or sets the RebarSet to which the modifier belongs. Only reinforcing bars generated by this RebarSet can be modified by this rebar modifier.
- `FirstAffectedBar` (Int32, get/set) — Gets or sets the first affected bar. If set to zero, the first affected bar will be chosen automatically.
- `FollowEdges` (Boolean, get/set) — Gets or sets a flag that indicates whether the modifier should attempt to follow the leg face edges located between its end points.
- `Identifier` (Identifier, get/set) — The identifier of the object.
- `IsUpToDate` (object, get) — Gets if the object does not have a modification which is not shared.
- `ModificationTime` (object, get) — Gets latest time of the object was modified or created.

## BaseRebarModifier.AffectedRebarEnum (enum)

This enum allows the caller to specify which end of the bar is of interest when calling the function GetAffectedBars.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/240f68e0-8f7f-6483-bfc6-d46137879691)

### Values
- `START` = `0` — Gets bars that are affected at their start.
- `END` = `1` — Gets bars that affected at their end.
- `EITHER` = `2` — Gets all bars affected by the modifier.

## BaseRebarModifier.BarsAffectedEnum (enum)

The reinforcing bars affected.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/68f81d64-7286-6118-c2e4-f659b15a0c06)

### Values
- `ALL_BARS` = `0` — All reinforcing bars.
- `EVERY_SECOND_BAR` = `1` — Every second reinforcing bar.
- `EVERY_THIRD_BAR` = `2` — Every third reinforcing bar.
- `EVERY_FOURTH_BAR` = `3` — Every fourth reinforcing bar.

### Properties
- `AdditionalSizeAbove` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the additional size above for combination welds.
- `AdditionalSizeBelow` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the additional size below for combination welds.
- `AngleAbove` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the angle above.
- `AngleBelow` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the angle below.
- `AroundWeld` (Boolean, get/set) — Gets or sets a value indicating whether the weld is an around weld (true) or an edge weld (false).
- `ConnectAssemblies` (Boolean, get/set) — Gets or sets a value indicating whether to connect a part or an assembly as a secondary part (false) or as a sub-assembly (true).
- `ContourAbove` (BaseWeld.WeldContourEnum, get/set) — Gets or sets the contour above.
- `ContourBelow` (BaseWeld.WeldContourEnum, get/set) — Gets or sets the contour below.
- `EffectiveThroatAbove` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the effective throat above.
- `EffectiveThroatBelow` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the effective throat below.
- `ElectrodeClassification` (BaseWeld.WeldElectrodeClassificationEnum, get/set) — Gets or sets the weld electrode classification.
- `ElectrodeCoefficient` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the electrode strength coefficient.
- `ElectrodeStrength` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the electrode strength.
- `FinishAbove` (BaseWeld.WeldFinishEnum, get/set) — Gets or sets the finish above.
- `FinishBelow` (BaseWeld.WeldFinishEnum, get/set) — Gets or sets the finish below.
- `Identifier` (Identifier, get/set) — The identifier of the object.
- `IncrementAmountAbove` (Int32, get/set) — Gets or sets the increment amount above.
- `IncrementAmountBelow` (Int32, get/set) — Gets or sets the increment amount below.
- `IntermittentType` (BaseWeld.WeldIntermittentTypeEnum, get/set) — Gets or sets the weld intermittent type.
- `IsUpToDate` (object, get) — Gets if the object does not have a modification which is not shared.
- `LengthAbove` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the length above.
- `LengthBelow` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the length below.
- `MainObject` (ModelObject, get/set) — Gets or sets the main part of the weld.
- `ModificationTime` (object, get) — Gets latest time of the object was modified or created.
- `NDTInspection` (BaseWeld.WeldNDTInspectionEnum, get/set) — Gets or sets the NDT inspection level.
- `PitchAbove` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the pitch above.
- `PitchBelow` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the pitch below.
- `Placement` (BaseWeld.WeldPlacementTypeEnum, get/set) — Gets or sets the weld placement.
- `PrefixAboveLine` (String, get/set) — Gets or sets the size prefix above the line.
- `PrefixBelowLine` (String, get/set) — Gets or sets the size prefix below the line.
- `Preparation` (BaseWeld.WeldPreparationTypeEnum, get/set) — Gets or sets the weld preparation.
- `ProcessType` (BaseWeld.WeldProcessTypeEnum, get/set) — Gets or sets the process type.
- `ReferenceText` (String, get/set) — Gets or sets the reference text.
- `RootFaceAbove` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the root face above.
- `RootFaceBelow` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the root face below.
- `RootOpeningAbove` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the root opening above.
- `RootOpeningBelow` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the root opening below.
- `SecondaryObject` (ModelObject, get/set) — Gets or sets the secondary part of the weld.
- `ShopWeld` (Boolean, get/set) — Gets or sets a value indicating whether the weld is a shop weld (true) or a site weld (false).
- `SizeAbove` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the size above.
- `SizeBelow` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the size below.
- `Standard` (String, get/set) — Gets or sets the weld detail/standard.
- `StitchWeld` (Boolean, get/set) — Gets or sets a value indicating whether the weld is stitched (true) or not stitched (false).
- `TypeAbove` (BaseWeld.WeldTypeEnum, get/set) — Gets or sets the type above.
- `TypeBelow` (BaseWeld.WeldTypeEnum, get/set) — Gets or sets the type below.
- `WeldNumber` (Int32, get) — Gets the weld number.
- `WeldNumberPrefix` (String, get/set) — Gets or sets the weld number prefix.

## BaseWeld.WeldContourEnum (enum)

The weld contour.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/45f9adf5-15ab-ec1a-ee0f-d2630e1b191c)

### Values
- `WELD_CONTOUR_NONE` = `0` — No contour.
- `WELD_CONTOUR_FLUSH` = `1` — The flush contour.
- `WELD_CONTOUR_CONVEX` = `2` — The convex contour.
- `WELD_CONTOUR_CONCAVE` = `3` — The concave contour.

## BaseWeld.WeldElectrodeClassificationEnum (enum)

- `WELD_ELECTRODE_CLASSIFICATION_42` = `2` — The electrode classification 42.

- `WELD_ELECTRODE_CLASSIFICATION_E60XX` = `4` — The electrode classification E60XX.
- `WELD_ELECTRODE_CLASSIFICATION_E70XX` = `5` — The electrode classification E70XX.
- `WELD_ELECTRODE_CLASSIFICATION_E80XX` = `6` — The electrode classification E80XX.
- `WELD_ELECTRODE_CLASSIFICATION_E90XX` = `7` — The electrode classification E90XX.

- `WELD_FINISH_FINISHED_WELD` = `4` — The normally treated finish.

### Values
- `WELD_PROCESS_NONE` = `0` — No process type.
- `WELD_PROCESS_SMAW` = `1` — The shielded metal arc (SMAW) process type.
- `WELD_PROCESS_SAW` = `2` — The submerged arc (SAW) process type.
- `WELD_PROCESS_GMAW` = `3` — The gas metal arc (GMAW) process type.
- `WELD_PROCESS_FCAW` = `4` — The flux cored arc (FCAW) process type.
- `WELD_PROCESS_ESW` = `5` — The electroslag (ESW) process type.
- `WELD_PROCESS_EGW` = `6` — The electrogas (EGW) process type.

## BaseWeld.WeldTypeEnum (enum)

The weld types.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/4371747e-9838-b687-e75b-06d27cbffb1e)

### Values
- `WELD_TYPE_NONE` = `0` — No weld type.
- `WELD_TYPE_EDGE_FLANGE` = `1` — The edge-flange weld.
- `WELD_TYPE_SQUARE_GROOVE_SQUARE_BUTT` = `2` — The square-groove (square butt) weld.
- `WELD_TYPE_BEVEL_GROOVE_SINGLE_V_BUTT` = `3` — The bevel-groove (single-V butt) weld.
- `WELD_TYPE_BEVEL_GROOVE_SINGLE_BEVEL_BUTT` = `4` — The bevel-groove (single-bevel butt) weld.
- `WELD_TYPE_SINGLE_V_BUTT_WITH_BROAD_ROOT_FACE` = `5` — The single-V butt weld with broad root face.
- `WELD_TYPE_SINGLE_BEVEL_BUTT_WITH_BROAD_ROOT_FACE` = `6` — The single-bevel butt weld with broad root face.
- `WELD_TYPE_U_GROOVE_SINGLE_U_BUTT` = `7` — The U-groove (single U-butt) weld.
- `WELD_TYPE_J_GROOVE_J_BUTT` = `8` — The J-groove (single J-butt) weld.
- `WELD_TYPE_BEVEL_BACKING` = `9` — The bevel backing weld.
- `WELD_TYPE_FILLET` = `10` — The fillet weld.
- `WELD_TYPE_PLUG` = `11` — The plug weld.
- `WELD_TYPE_SPOT` = `12` — The spot weld.
- `WELD_TYPE_SEAM` = `13` — The seam weld.
- `WELD_TYPE_SLOT` = `14` — The slot weld.
- `WELD_TYPE_FLARE_BEVEL_GROOVE` = `15` — The flare-bevel-groove weld.
- `WELD_TYPE_FLARE_V_GROOVE` = `16` — The flare V-groove weld.
- `WELD_TYPE_CORNER_FLANGE` = `17` — The corner-flange weld.
- `WELD_TYPE_PARTIAL_PENETRATION_SINGLE_BEVEL_BUTT_PLUS_FILLET` = `18` — The partial penetration (single-bevel butt + fillet) weld.
- `WELD_TYPE_PARTIAL_PENETRATION_SQUARE_GROOVE_PLUS_FILLET` = `19` — The partial penetration (square groove + fillet) weld.
- `WELD_TYPE_MELT_THROUGH` = `20` — The melt-through weld.
- `STEEP_FLANKED_BEVEL_GROOVE_SINGLE_V_BUTT` = `21` — The steep flanked bevel groove single-V butt weld.
- `STEEP_FLANKED_BEVEL_GROOVE_SINGLE_BEVEL_BUTT` = `22` — The steep flanked bevel groove single-bevel butt weld.
- `WELD_TYPE_EDGE` = `23` — The edge weld.
- `WELD_TYPE_ISO_SURFACING` = `24` — The ISO surfacing weld.
- `WELD_TYPE_FOLD` = `25` — The fold weld.
- `WELD_TYPE_INCLINED` = `26` — The inclined weld.

## Beam (class)

The Beam class represents a single beam in the model. A beam has a single start and end point.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/8621e658-a496-c1b9-49d0-4076c20779f7)

### Constructors
- `public Beam()` — Initializes a new instance of the Beam class. The start and end points are in the origin.
- `public Beam(Beam.BeamTypeEnum beamType)` — Initializes a new instance of the Beam class by using the defined type.
- `public Beam(Point startPoint, Point endPoint)` — Initializes a new instance of the Beam class with the given start and end points.

### Methods
#### `public int CompareTo(Object obj)`

Compares Identifiers of model objects.

**Parameters:**
- `obj` (System.Object) — The model object to be used in comparison.

**Returns:** `Int32` — 0 if object Identifiers are the same, 1 if given object Identifier is bigger, -1 otherwise.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/79cca356-0c8a-e8fb-463a-34ad4141bec7)

Compares the instantiated part with another one.

**Remarks:** If the numbering hasn't been performed the function will also return false.

**Parameters:**
- `partToCompare` (Tekla.Structures.Model.Part) — The part to compare with.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if there are no differences, false otherwise.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/e8632c0d-89cf-9aab-6532-f62f9873067b)

### Properties
- `AssemblyNumber` (NumberingSeries, get/set) — Gets or sets the assembly number. Defines the numbering in the assembly sense.
- `CastUnitType` (Part.CastUnitTypeEnum, get/set) — Gets or sets the cast unit type of the part.
- `Class` (String, get/set) — Gets or sets the class of the part.
- `DeformingData` (DeformingData, get/set) — Gets or sets the deforming data of the part.
- `EndLevelName` (String, get/set) — Gets or sets the end level name.
- `EndLevelOffset` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the end level offset.
- `EndPoint` (Point, get/set) — Gets or sets the end point of the beam.
- `EndPointOffset` (Offset, get/set) — Gets or sets the beam's end point offset.
- `Finish` (String, get/set) — Gets or sets the finish of the part.
- `Identifier` (Identifier, get/set) — The identifier of the object.
- `IsUpToDate` (object, get) — Gets if the object does not have a modification which is not shared.
- `Material` (Material, get/set) — Gets or sets the material the part is made of.
- `ModificationTime` (object, get) — Gets latest time of the object was modified or created.
- `Name` (String, get/set) — Gets or sets the name of the part.
- `PartNumber` (NumberingSeries, get/set) — Gets or sets the part number. Defines the numbering in the part sense.
- `Position` (Position, get/set) — Gets or sets the part position. Defines the way the part is positioned in the model.
- `PourPhase` (Int32, get/set) — Gets or sets the pour phase of the part.
- `Profile` (Profile, get/set) — Gets or sets the profile of the part.
- `StartLevelName` (String, get/set) — Gets or sets the start level name.
- `StartLevelOffset` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the start level offset.
- `StartPoint` (Point, get/set) — Gets or sets the start point of the beam.
- `StartPointOffset` (Offset, get/set) — Gets or sets the beam's start point offset.
- `Type` (Beam.BeamTypeEnum, get) — Gets the read-only type of the beam. The default type for a new beam instance is beam.

## Beam.BeamTypeEnum (enum)

The beam types. The strip and pad footings are valid only if the material type of the beam is concrete.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/6151749e-0ba3-8d75-6127-b84f300dd30f)

### Values
- `BEAM` = `0` — The beam.
- `PANEL` = `1` — The panel.
- `STRIP_FOOTING` = `2` — The concrete strip footing.
- `PAD_FOOTING` = `3` — The concrete pad footing.
- `COLUMN` = `4` — The column.

## BendSurface (class)

The BendSurface class defines a surface contour that can be used as a bend between two plates

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/4b8c0f69-2f54-749d-5dcc-753991f7481f)

### Constructors
- `public BendSurface(Arc baseArc, double height, double radiusAtHeight)` — Initializes a new instance of the BendSurface class from a base arc, a height and a radius at the top. This is the recommended constructor if no intermediate points are desired along the lateral boundaries of the bend surface.
- `public BendSurface(List<Point> lateralBoundary1, List<Point> lateralBoundary2, Line centerLine, Vector rotationAxis)` — Initializes a new instance of the BendSurface class from the cone lateral boundaries and the center line. This is the recommended constructor if fine grained control is desired over the lateral boundaries of the surface
- `public BendSurface(Vector endFaceNormal1, Vector endFaceNormal2, List<Point> lateralBoundary1, List<Point> lateralBoundary2)` — Initializes a new instance of the BendSurface class with given parameters. The boundaries are defined by the lateral boundaries (i.e. the points on the sides of the curved part of the surface). The side boundaries are defined by the two first points and the two last points of the lateral boundaries.
- `public BendSurface(Vector endFaceNormal1, Vector endFaceNormal2, LineSegment sideBoundary1, LineSegment sideBoundary2)` — Initializes a new instance of the BendSurface class with given parameters. The boundaries are defined by the side boundaries (i.e. generator lines of the surface).

### Properties
- `CenterLine` (Line, get) — Gets the center line of the bend surface (i.e. the line that crosses every circular cross section of the bend at their center point)
- `EndFaceNormal1` (Vector, get) — Gets the first end face normal.
- `EndFaceNormal2` (Vector, get) — Gets the second end face normal.
- `IntersectionLine` (Line, get) — Gets intersection line.
- `InwardCurved` (Boolean, get) — Gets the direction of the curve - true if the curve is oriented towards the intersection line, false otherwise.
- `LateralBoundary1` (List<Point>, get) — Gets or sets the first lateral boundary
- `LateralBoundary2` (List<Point>, get) — Gets or sets the second lateral boundary
- `RotationAxis` (Vector, get) — Gets the unit vector that defines the rotation axis (for counter clockwise rotations) of the lateral boundaries of the surface. This axis is always parallel to the direction of the center line.
- `SideBoundary1` (LineSegment, get/set) — Gets or sets the first side boundary.
- `SideBoundary2` (LineSegment, get/set) — Gets or sets the second side boundary.

## BendSurfaceNode (class)

The BendSurfaceNode class represents a bend surface geometry tree node.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/587d9a1d-e196-e65c-eaf5-130c137c8b60)

### Constructors
- `public BendSurfaceNode(BendSurface surface)` — Initializes a new instance of the BendSurfaceNode class.

### Methods

Entry method for the visitor pattern

**Parameters:**
- `visitor` (Tekla.Structures.Model.IGeometryNodeVisitor)

Creates a new object that is a copy of the current instance.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/5cb016ba-46b5-d41d-8e6a-760ba327a50f)

- `Surface` (BendSurface, get) — Gets the bend surface geometry.

#### `public override bool Insert()`

Inserts the bent plate into the model database. All the attributes must be set and geometry should have at least one bend.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True on success.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/cadf8e15-919e-7684-c67f-b1ced59d4695)

### Properties
- `AssemblyNumber` (NumberingSeries, get/set) — Gets or sets the assembly number. Defines the numbering in the assembly sense.
- `CastUnitType` (Part.CastUnitTypeEnum, get/set) — Gets or sets the cast unit type of the part.
- `Class` (String, get/set) — Gets or sets the class of the part.
- `DeformingData` (DeformingData, get/set) — Gets or sets the deforming data of the part.
- `Finish` (String, get/set) — Gets or sets the finish of the part.
- `Geometry` (ConnectiveGeometry, get/set) — Gets or sets the geometry of the bent plate.
- `Identifier` (Identifier, get/set) — The identifier of the object.
- `IsUpToDate` (object, get) — Gets if the object does not have a modification which is not shared.
- `Material` (Material, get/set) — Gets or sets the material the part is made of.
- `ModificationTime` (object, get) — Gets latest time of the object was modified or created.
- `Name` (String, get/set) — Gets or sets the name of the part.
- `PartNumber` (NumberingSeries, get/set) — Gets or sets the part number. Defines the numbering in the part sense.
- `Position` (Position, get/set) — Gets or sets the part position. Defines the way the part is positioned in the model.
- `PourPhase` (Int32, get/set) — Gets or sets the pour phase of the part.
- `Profile` (Profile, get/set) — Gets or sets the profile of the part.
- `Thickness` (Double, get) — Gets thickness of the bent plate.

## BentPlate.BendShape (enum)

Enumerator to define different possibilities for bend shapes.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/b5343f3c-931a-194b-ccc5-e1c29895b276)

### Values
- `Cylindrical` = `0` — Cylindrical bend.
- `Conical` = `1` — Conical bend.

## BentPlateGeometrySolver (class)

BentPlateGeometrySolver is a solver class to handle ConnectiveGeometry modification related computations.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/6868795d-e6b6-4c94-a705-5e4972e535c9)

### Constructors
- `public BentPlateGeometrySolver()` — Initializes a new instance of the BentPlateGeometrySolver class.

### Methods
#### `public ConnectiveGeometry AddLeg(ConnectiveGeometry geometry, Contour polygon)`

Appends a polygon section to ConnectiveGeometry, using the maximal radius for the cylindrical connection.

**Parameters:**
- `geometry` (Tekla.Structures.Model.ConnectiveGeometry) — ConnectiveGeometry used to create a connection with.
- `polygon` (Tekla.Structures.Model.Contour) — The polygonal contour to add to geometry.

**Returns:** `ConnectiveGeometry` — Modified ConnectiveGeometry if command succeeded or geometry otherwise.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/a2759dfe-8879-387a-43c8-a30e76609c81)

#### `public ConnectiveGeometry AddLeg(ConnectiveGeometry geometry, Contour polygon, BentPlate.BendShape bendShape)`

Appends a polygon section to ConnectiveGeometry, using the maximal radius for the cylindrical connection, or the maximum cone aperture for conical connection.

**Parameters:**
- `geometry` (Tekla.Structures.Model.ConnectiveGeometry) — ConnectiveGeometry used to create a connection with.
- `polygon` (Tekla.Structures.Model.Contour) — The polygonal contour to add to geometry.
- `bendShape` (Tekla.Structures.Model.BentPlate.BendShape) — Shape of the bend that connects the leg

**Returns:** `ConnectiveGeometry` — Modified ConnectiveGeometry if command succeeded or geometry otherwise.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/01139736-16e5-b503-b77c-2a989302924e)

#### `public ConnectiveGeometry AddLeg(ConnectiveGeometry geometry, Contour polygon, double radius)`

Appends a polygon section to this ConnectiveGeometry, using the specified radius for the cylindrical connection.

**Parameters:**
- `geometry` (Tekla.Structures.Model.ConnectiveGeometry) — ConnectiveGeometry used to create a connection with.
- `polygon` (Tekla.Structures.Model.Contour) — the polygonal contour to add to this geometry
- `radius` (System.Double) — the specified radius for the connecting cylindrical section

**Returns:** `ConnectiveGeometry` — Modified ConnectiveGeometry if command succeeded or geometry otherwise.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/be684308-bd46-4b43-ab67-885df4db987f)

#### `public ConnectiveGeometry AddLeg(ConnectiveGeometry geometry, Contour polygon, double largestRadius, double halfAperture)`

Joins a polygon section to this ConnectiveGeometry, using the specified radius and aperture for the conical connection.

**Parameters:**
- `geometry` (Tekla.Structures.Model.ConnectiveGeometry) — ConnectiveGeometry used to create a connection with.
- `polygon` (Tekla.Structures.Model.Contour) — the polygonal contour to add to this geometry
- `largestRadius` (System.Double) — The specified largest radius for the connecting conical section.
- `halfAperture` (System.Double) — The angle between the cone generatrix and any of its generator lines.

**Returns:** `ConnectiveGeometry` — Modified ConnectiveGeometry if command succeeded or geometry otherwise.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/005a09fb-64f4-0150-fdee-40ba1d2e009e)

#### `public ConnectiveGeometry AddLeg(ConnectiveGeometry geometry, LineSegment segment1, Contour polygon, LineSegment segment2)`

Appends a polygon section to this ConnectiveGeometry, using the specified connection points and maximal radius for the cylindrical connection.

**Parameters:**
- `geometry` (Tekla.Structures.Model.ConnectiveGeometry) — ConnectiveGeometry used to create a connection with.
- `segment1` (Tekla.Structures.Geometry3d.LineSegment) — the two connection points on this instance geometry
- `polygon` (Tekla.Structures.Model.Contour) — the polygonal contour to add to this geometry
- `segment2` (Tekla.Structures.Geometry3d.LineSegment) — the two connection points on polygon geometry

**Returns:** `ConnectiveGeometry` — Modified ConnectiveGeometry if command succeeded or geometry otherwise.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/fe3ef7c1-95d0-b903-029f-3327db769d09)

#### `public ConnectiveGeometry AddLeg(ConnectiveGeometry geometry, LineSegment segment1, Contour polygon, LineSegment segment2, BentPlate.BendShape bendShape)`

Appends a polygon section to this ConnectiveGeometry, using the specified connection points and maximal radius for the cylindrical connection, or maximum aperture for conical connection.

**Parameters:**
- `geometry` (Tekla.Structures.Model.ConnectiveGeometry) — ConnectiveGeometry used to create a connection with.
- `segment1` (Tekla.Structures.Geometry3d.LineSegment) — the two connection points on this instance geometry
- `polygon` (Tekla.Structures.Model.Contour) — the polygonal contour to add to this geometry
- `segment2` (Tekla.Structures.Geometry3d.LineSegment) — the two connection points on polygon geometry
- `bendShape` (Tekla.Structures.Model.BentPlate.BendShape) — Shape of the bend that connects the leg

**Returns:** `ConnectiveGeometry` — Modified ConnectiveGeometry if command succeeded or geometry otherwise.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/2ae3af3f-6bf8-1f24-e666-c55aca86f5c4)

#### `public ConnectiveGeometry AddLeg(ConnectiveGeometry geometry, LineSegment segment1, Contour polygon, LineSegment segment2, double radius)`

Appends a polygon section to this ConnectiveGeometry, using the specified connection points and radius for the cylindrical connection.

**Parameters:**
- `geometry` (Tekla.Structures.Model.ConnectiveGeometry) — ConnectiveGeometry used to create a connection with.
- `segment1` (Tekla.Structures.Geometry3d.LineSegment) — the two connection points on this instance geometry
- `polygon` (Tekla.Structures.Model.Contour) — the polygonal contour to add to this geometry
- `segment2` (Tekla.Structures.Geometry3d.LineSegment) — the two connection points on polygon geometry
- `radius` (System.Double) — the specified radius for the connecting cylindrical section

**Returns:** `ConnectiveGeometry` — Modified ConnectiveGeometry if command succeeded or geometry otherwise.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/3381e0db-8efa-f82b-8322-f4a48ed1a265)

#### `public ConnectiveGeometry AddLeg(ConnectiveGeometry geometry, LineSegment segment1, Contour polygon, LineSegment segment2, double largestRadius, double halfAperture)`

Joins a polygon section to this ConnectiveGeometry, using the specified connection points and radius and aperture for the conical connection.

**Parameters:**
- `geometry` (Tekla.Structures.Model.ConnectiveGeometry) — ConnectiveGeometry used to create a connection with.
- `segment1` (Tekla.Structures.Geometry3d.LineSegment) — The two connection points on this instance geometry.
- `polygon` (Tekla.Structures.Model.Contour) — the polygonal contour to add to this geometry
- `segment2` (Tekla.Structures.Geometry3d.LineSegment) — The two connection points on polygon geometry.
- `largestRadius` (System.Double) — The specified largest radius for the connecting conical section.
- `halfAperture` (System.Double) — The angle between the cone generatrix and any of its generator lines.

**Returns:** `ConnectiveGeometry` — Modified ConnectiveGeometry if command succeeded or geometry otherwise.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/253cf055-768d-0624-e258-2039cd78494d)

#### `public ConnectiveGeometry ModifyBendSurface(ConnectiveGeometry geometry, GeometrySection bendSection, BendSurface surface)`

Modifies the shape of a bend surface section, and updates related polygon sections accordingly.

**Parameters:**
- `geometry` (Tekla.Structures.Model.ConnectiveGeometry) — Reference geometry to modify which contains cylindricalSection.
- `bendSection` (Tekla.Structures.Model.GeometrySection) — Geometry section to modify.
- `surface` (Tekla.Structures.Model.BendSurface) — The modified bend surface for the section.

**Returns:** `ConnectiveGeometry` — Modified ConnectiveGeometry if command succeeded or geometry otherwise.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/2d82eb90-c4a4-d473-b872-08e8522fa827)

#### `public ConnectiveGeometry ModifyConicalRadiuses(ConnectiveGeometry geometry, GeometrySection conicalSection, double radius1, double radius2)`

Modify the radiuses of a conical section, preserving the length of the side boundaries. The order of the provided radiuses is not relevant, as the cone vertex will be located on the same side of the plates as the original cone section.

**Parameters:**
- `geometry` (Tekla.Structures.Model.ConnectiveGeometry) — Reference geometry to modify which contains conicalSection.
- `conicalSection` (Tekla.Structures.Model.GeometrySection) — The conical geometry section to modify.
- `radius1` (System.Double) — New radius one of the cross sections of the cone.
- `radius2` (System.Double) — New radius of the other cross section of the cone.

**Returns:** `ConnectiveGeometry` — Modified ConnectiveGeometry if command succeeded or geometry otherwise.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/e2a50415-93e4-cc52-e702-0bab551f0a2d)

#### `public ConnectiveGeometry ModifyCylindricalSurface(ConnectiveGeometry geometry, GeometrySection cylindricalSection, CylindricalSurface surface)`

Modifies the shape of a cylindrical surface section, and updates related polygon sections accordingly.

**Remarks:** Identical to ModifyBendSurface(ConnectiveGeometry, GeometrySection, BendSurface)

**Parameters:**
- `geometry` (Tekla.Structures.Model.ConnectiveGeometry)
- `cylindricalSection` (Tekla.Structures.Model.GeometrySection)
- `surface` (Tekla.Structures.Model.CylindricalSurface)

**Returns:** `ConnectiveGeometry` — 

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/9b20a6c3-4976-912f-a940-660056f80e3d)

#### `public ConnectiveGeometry ModifyPolygon(ConnectiveGeometry geometry, GeometrySection polygonSection, Contour points)`

Modify the shape of a polygon section, and updates related cylindrical sections accordingly.

**Parameters:**
- `geometry` (Tekla.Structures.Model.ConnectiveGeometry) — Reference geometry to modify which contains polygonSection.
- `polygonSection` (Tekla.Structures.Model.GeometrySection) — A polygon section from geometry to modify.
- `points` (Tekla.Structures.Model.Contour) — The modified contour for the section.

**Returns:** `ConnectiveGeometry` — Modified ConnectiveGeometry if command succeeded or geometry otherwise.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/124f6069-da5d-82e5-0762-9266e5b30aed)

#### `public ConnectiveGeometry ModifyRadius(ConnectiveGeometry geometry, GeometrySection cylindricalSection, double radius)`

Modify the radius of a cylindrical section.

**Parameters:**
- `geometry` (Tekla.Structures.Model.ConnectiveGeometry) — Reference geometry to modify which contains cylindricalSection.
- `cylindricalSection` (Tekla.Structures.Model.GeometrySection) — The cylindrical geometry section to modify.
- `radius` (System.Double) — The new radius for the cylindrical section.

**Returns:** `ConnectiveGeometry` — Modified ConnectiveGeometry if command succeeded or geometry otherwise.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/332d9017-9164-8a8b-5a1e-dc772f98674f)

#### `public ConnectiveGeometry RemoveLeg(ConnectiveGeometry geometry, GeometrySection legSection)`

Removes a polygon section which is the end section, from this ConnectiveGeometry including extensions and cylindrical surface.

**Parameters:**
- `geometry` (Tekla.Structures.Model.ConnectiveGeometry) — ConnectiveGeometry used to remove a connection.
- `legSection` (Tekla.Structures.Model.GeometrySection) — Leg GeometrySection to remove.

**Returns:** `ConnectiveGeometry` — Modified ConnectiveGeometry if command succeeded or geometry otherwise.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/5426185f-4351-d659-7842-8a1e6256eb25)

#### `public ConnectiveGeometry ScaleConeSection(ConnectiveGeometry geometry, GeometrySection conicalSection, double scale)`

Scale the shape of a conical section.

**Parameters:**
- `geometry` (Tekla.Structures.Model.ConnectiveGeometry) — Reference geometry to modify which contains conicalSection.
- `conicalSection` (Tekla.Structures.Model.GeometrySection) — The conical geometry section to modify.
- `scale` (System.Double) — Scale to apply to the conical section (greater than 0).

**Returns:** `ConnectiveGeometry` — Modified ConnectiveGeometry if command succeeded or geometry otherwise.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/ca3720e0-be3e-6960-ca2f-151a15227447)

#### `public ConnectiveGeometry SetBendAngle(double angle, GeometrySection sectionToSetAngle, GeometrySection sectionToMove, ConnectiveGeometry geometry)`

Sets angle as the angle of curved section sectionToSetAngle and modifies geometry starting from sectionToMove.

**Parameters:**
- `angle` (System.Double) — Angle in degrees to set.
- `sectionToSetAngle` (Tekla.Structures.Model.GeometrySection) — Curved section to set angle value.
- `sectionToMove` (Tekla.Structures.Model.GeometrySection) — Could be curved or plate section, used to know which side of chain to modify.
- `geometry` (Tekla.Structures.Model.ConnectiveGeometry) — Geometry of the bent plate to modify.

**Returns:** `ConnectiveGeometry` — Returns modified geometry with on success or initial geometry on fail.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/6480f6a8-c58d-c973-1e50-e90c49132b08)

#### `public ConnectiveGeometry SetMainSection(GeometrySection newMainSection, ConnectiveGeometry geometry)`

Sets newMainSection as a main section of geometry.

**Parameters:**
- `newMainSection` (Tekla.Structures.Model.GeometrySection) — Geometry section to set as main section.
- `geometry` (Tekla.Structures.Model.ConnectiveGeometry) — Connective geometry to modify.

**Returns:** `ConnectiveGeometry` — Connective geometry with newMainSection as main section.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/a9b78007-72d0-cdc5-7461-ffd5fc97c0d5)

#### `public IList<ConnectiveGeometry> Split(ConnectiveGeometry geometry, GeometrySection geometrySection)`

Returns the resulting geometry or geometries when a section and the related automatically created sections are removed from this geometry.

**Parameters:**
- `geometry` (Tekla.Structures.Model.ConnectiveGeometry) — ConnectiveGeometry used for split operation.
- `geometrySection` (Tekla.Structures.Model.GeometrySection) — GeometrySection to remove.

**Returns:** `IList<ConnectiveGeometry>` — The resulting geometries once the section and related automatic sections are removed.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/5cac6160-6cf1-1bfb-b4da-d9c72fff026a)

## BentPlateGeometrySolver.OperationStatus (enum)

Define possible statuses for geometry creation/modification commands.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/6e3965fc-cced-c0c2-3025-63df350c92bd)

### Values
- `Success` = `0` — Status on success creation.
- `Failure` = `1` — General failure mode.
- `FacePerpendicularToIntersectionLine` = `2` — Face perpendicular to intersection line.
- `PlateIntersectsWithIntersectionLine` = `3` — Plate polygon extends beyond intersection line.
- `ExtensionIntersectsWithPlate` = `4` — Extension plate clashes with plates.
- `FacesTooNearEachOther` = `5` — Faces to be connected overlap.
- `FacesAtAnObtuseAngle` = `6` — Faces to be connected are too steep of an angle.
- `UndefinedCurveDirection` = `7` — Can't make inward/outward curve check.
- `UnsupportedChamfer` = `8` — Plates containing unsupported chamfer information.
- `InvalidRadius` = `9` — Cannot connect parts with a cylindrical section having the provided radius.
- `InvalidFacePoints` = `10` — Cannot create geometry from requested face points.

## BoltArray (class)

The BoltArray class defines a bolt group with an array shape.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/caa3aee3-d7ae-6c96-bce9-eaab89a1cad5)

### Constructors
- `public BoltArray()` — Creates a new bolt array instance.

### Methods
#### `public bool AddBoltDistX(double DistX)`

Adds an X distance to the bolt array.

**Parameters:**
- `DistX` (System.Double) — The X distance to be added.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if the distance was added. Throws an OverflowException if there was no more space for the distance.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/d42a61ed-0cfb-094d-c7af-dc55b8a32373)

Adds a Y distance to the bolt array.

Adds the given part to the list of the other parts that should be bolted.

- `M` (Tekla.Structures.Model.Part) — The part to add.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True on success.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/342a0099-f3a9-d0f2-c02f-9df1901682e3)

Compares Identifiers of model objects.

### Properties
- `BlindHoleDepth` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the blind hole depth.
- `Bolt` (Boolean, get/set) — Indicates whether the instance is a bolt or just a hole.
- `BoltHolesAttributes` (IList<BoltHoleAttributes>, get/set) — The bolt holes attributes.
- `BoltPositions` (ArrayList, get) — Gets the single bolt positions. All the bolt positions are in the XY-plane defined by the bolt group coordinate system. The given positions are in relation to the transformation plane in which the bolt group was selected.
- `BoltSize` (Double, get/set) — The size of the bolts in the group.
- `BoltStandard` (String, get/set) — The standard of the bolt group.
- `BoltType` (BoltGroup.BoltTypeEnum, get/set) — The type of the bolt group.
- `ConnectAssemblies` (Boolean, get/set) — Defines whether to connect a part or an assembly as a secondary part (false) or as a sub-assembly (true).
- `CutLength` (Double, get/set) — The cut length.
- `EndPointOffset` (Offset, get/set) — The end point offset values of the bolt group.
- `ExtraLength` (Double, get/set) — The extra length for the bolts.
- `FirstPosition` (Point, get/set) — The first position point.
- `Hole1` (Boolean, get/set) — Indicates whether the hole 1 is used.
- `Hole2` (Boolean, get/set) — Indicates whether the hole 2 is used.
- `Hole3` (Boolean, get/set) — Indicates whether the hole 3 is used.
- `Hole4` (Boolean, get/set) — Indicates whether the hole 4 is used.
- `Hole5` (Boolean, get/set) — Indicates whether the hole 5 is used.
- `HoleType` (BoltGroup.BoltHoleTypeEnum, get/set) — The special hole type: oversized, slotted, blind or no hole.
- `Identifier` (Identifier, get/set) — The identifier of the object.
- `IsUpToDate` (object, get) — Gets if the object does not have a modification which is not shared.
- `Length` (Double, get/set) — The length. An extra variable that is only set when the bolt group is made of studs instead of bolts.
- `ModificationTime` (object, get) — Gets latest time of the object was modified or created.
- `Nut1` (Boolean, get/set) — Indicates whether the nut 1 is used in the assembly.
- `Nut2` (Boolean, get/set) — Indicates whether the nut 2 is used in the assembly.
- `OtherPartsToBolt` (ArrayList, get) — The other parts to be bolted (read-only).
- `PartToBeBolted` (Part, get/set) — The part to be bolted.
- `PartToBoltTo` (Part, get/set) — The part to bolt to.
- `PlainHoleType` (BoltGroup.BoltPlainHoleTypeEnum, get/set) — Gets or sets the plain hole type.
- `Position` (Position, get/set) — The position attributes.
- `RotateSlots` (BoltGroup.BoltRotateSlotsEnum, get/set) — Gets or sets the rotation of the slots: odd, even or parallel.
- `SecondPosition` (Point, get/set) — The second position point.
- `SlotOffsetX` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the slotted hole offset X.
- `SlotOffsetY` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the slotted hole offset Y.
- `SlottedHoleX` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the X allowance of the slotted hole.
- `SlottedHoleY` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the Y allowance of the slotted hole.
- `StartPointOffset` (Offset, get/set) — The start point offset values of the bolt group.
- `ThreadInMaterial` (BoltGroup.BoltThreadInMaterialEnum, get/set) — Whether the thread goes inside the materal or not.
- `Tolerance` (Double, get/set) — The hole tolerance.
- `Washer1` (Boolean, get/set) — Indicates whether the washer 1 is used in the assembly.
- `Washer2` (Boolean, get/set) — Indicates whether the washer 2 is used in the assembly.
- `Washer3` (Boolean, get/set) — Indicates whether the washer 3 is used in the assembly.

## BoltCircle (class)

The BoltCircle class defines a bolt group with a circle shape.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/344a2e05-14de-2b76-e846-28e68b0c68ae)

### Constructors
- `public BoltCircle()` — Creates a new bolt circle instance.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/80bf0348-b651-3d4a-862b-49c85bc924ab)

### Properties
- `BlindHoleDepth` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the blind hole depth.
- `Bolt` (Boolean, get/set) — Indicates whether the instance is a bolt or just a hole.
- `BoltHolesAttributes` (IList<BoltHoleAttributes>, get/set) — The bolt holes attributes.
- `BoltPositions` (ArrayList, get) — Gets the single bolt positions. All the bolt positions are in the XY-plane defined by the bolt group coordinate system. The given positions are in relation to the transformation plane in which the bolt group was selected.
- `BoltSize` (Double, get/set) — The size of the bolts in the group.
- `BoltStandard` (String, get/set) — The standard of the bolt group.
- `BoltType` (BoltGroup.BoltTypeEnum, get/set) — The type of the bolt group.
- `ConnectAssemblies` (Boolean, get/set) — Defines whether to connect a part or an assembly as a secondary part (false) or as a sub-assembly (true).
- `CutLength` (Double, get/set) — The cut length.
- `Diameter` (Double, get/set) — The diameter of the circle.
- `EndPointOffset` (Offset, get/set) — The end point offset values of the bolt group.
- `ExtraLength` (Double, get/set) — The extra length for the bolts.
- `FirstPosition` (Point, get/set) — The first position point.
- `Hole1` (Boolean, get/set) — Indicates whether the hole 1 is used.
- `Hole2` (Boolean, get/set) — Indicates whether the hole 2 is used.
- `Hole3` (Boolean, get/set) — Indicates whether the hole 3 is used.
- `Hole4` (Boolean, get/set) — Indicates whether the hole 4 is used.
- `Hole5` (Boolean, get/set) — Indicates whether the hole 5 is used.
- `HoleType` (BoltGroup.BoltHoleTypeEnum, get/set) — The special hole type: oversized, slotted, blind or no hole.
- `Identifier` (Identifier, get/set) — The identifier of the object.
- `IsUpToDate` (object, get) — Gets if the object does not have a modification which is not shared.
- `Length` (Double, get/set) — The length. An extra variable that is only set when the bolt group is made of studs instead of bolts.
- `ModificationTime` (object, get) — Gets latest time of the object was modified or created.
- `NumberOfBolts` (Double, get/set) — The number of bolts in the circle.
- `Nut1` (Boolean, get/set) — Indicates whether the nut 1 is used in the assembly.
- `Nut2` (Boolean, get/set) — Indicates whether the nut 2 is used in the assembly.
- `OtherPartsToBolt` (ArrayList, get) — The other parts to be bolted (read-only).
- `PartToBeBolted` (Part, get/set) — The part to be bolted.
- `PartToBoltTo` (Part, get/set) — The part to bolt to.
- `PlainHoleType` (BoltGroup.BoltPlainHoleTypeEnum, get/set) — Gets or sets the plain hole type.
- `Position` (Position, get/set) — The position attributes.
- `RotateSlots` (BoltGroup.BoltRotateSlotsEnum, get/set) — Gets or sets the rotation of the slots: odd, even or parallel.
- `SecondPosition` (Point, get/set) — The second position point.
- `SlotOffsetX` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the slotted hole offset X.
- `SlotOffsetY` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the slotted hole offset Y.
- `SlottedHoleX` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the X allowance of the slotted hole.
- `SlottedHoleY` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the Y allowance of the slotted hole.
- `StartPointOffset` (Offset, get/set) — The start point offset values of the bolt group.
- `ThreadInMaterial` (BoltGroup.BoltThreadInMaterialEnum, get/set) — Whether the thread goes inside the materal or not.
- `Tolerance` (Double, get/set) — The hole tolerance.
- `Washer1` (Boolean, get/set) — Indicates whether the washer 1 is used in the assembly.
- `Washer2` (Boolean, get/set) — Indicates whether the washer 2 is used in the assembly.
- `Washer3` (Boolean, get/set) — Indicates whether the washer 3 is used in the assembly.

## BoltGroup (class)

The BoltGroup class is an abstract base class for all bolt shapes, such as array, circle and XY list. See the Tekla Structures help file for further information about the attributes.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/3647beae-3e71-72e2-03ee-df67381da5db)

### Constructors
- `public BoltGroup()` — Creates a new bolt group instance with the given shape.

## BoltGroup.BoltHoleTypeEnum (enum)

The special hole type.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/4875b00c-24a3-bd15-5b05-76b3c541f18c)

### Values
- `HOLE_TYPE_OVERSIZED` = `0` — The oversized hole type.
- `HOLE_TYPE_SLOTTED` = `1` — The slotted hole type.
- `HOLE_TYPE_NO_HOLE` = `2` — The no hole type.
- `HOLE_TYPE_TAPPED` = `3` — The tapped hole type.

## BoltGroup.BoltPlainHoleTypeEnum (enum)

The plain hole type.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/d1716b64-f135-3612-eaca-36a14f2341ec)

### Values
- `HOLE_TYPE_THROUGH` = `0` — The through hole type.
- `HOLE_TYPE_BLIND` = `1` — The blind hole type.

## BoltGroup.BoltRotateSlotsEnum (enum)

The rotation of the slots (the holes).

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/b1a3f6e5-d6af-9968-310d-4de51286c668)

### Values
- `ROTATE_SLOTS_ODD` = `0` — The slots are crossed to odd parts.
- `ROTATE_SLOTS_EVEN` = `1` — The slots are crossed to even parts.
- `ROTATE_SLOTS_PARALLEL` = `2` — The slots are rotated to be parallel.

## BoltGroup.BoltThreadInMaterialEnum (enum)

The thread in the material.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/3db6886a-d653-ddc4-6543-8e645a77f6da)

### Values
- `THREAD_IN_MATERIAL_NO` = `0` — No thread inside the material (the part).
- `THREAD_IN_MATERIAL_YES` = `1` — Thread inside the material (the part).

## BoltGroup.BoltTypeEnum (enum)

The type of the bolt.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/6983381d-847d-9348-a456-338ed39eeb56)

### Values
- `BOLT_TYPE_SITE` = `0` — The site bolt type.
- `BOLT_TYPE_WORKSHOP` = `1` — The workshop bolt type.

## BoltGroup.ErrorStatus (enum)

Define possible statuses for geometry creation/modification commands.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/2c68335c-fae5-a806-5e6b-40bbb9a29738)

### Values
- `CommonSettingFromDiffPropertiesException` = `0` — Exception thrown when the common setting cannot be computed from the individual settings.
- `SlottedHoleOffsetsTooBigException` = `1` — Exception thrown when the slotted hole offsets move hole too far from bolt origin.

## BoltHoleAttributes (class)

The bolt hole attributes.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/422ddb23-4ec9-3422-853d-aac6c307683d)

### Constructors
- `public BoltHoleAttributes()` — Creates a new bolt hole attributes instance.

### Properties
- `HoleType` (BoltGroup.BoltHoleTypeEnum, get/set) — The special hole type: oversized, tapped, slotted, blind or no hole.
- `LongHoleX` (Double, get/set) — The long hole X value.
- `LongHoleY` (Double, get/set) — The long hole Y value.
- `SlotOffsetX` (Double, get/set) — The slot offset X value that must be between +-0.5 x LongHoleX.
- `SlotOffsetY` (Double, get/set) — The slot offset Y value that must be between +-0.5 x LongHoleY.

## BoltXYList (class)

The BoltXYList class defines a bolt group with an XY list shape. The bolt group is the most generic one of the bolt groups.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/46d4ad9b-0db9-51b1-caa4-74a439cd54fc)

### Constructors
- `public BoltXYList()` — Creates a new bolt XY list instance.

### Methods
#### `public bool AddBoltDistX(double DistX)`

Adds an X distance to the bolt XY list.

**Parameters:**
- `DistX` (System.Double) — The X distance to be added.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if the distance was added. Throws an OverflowException if there was no more space for the distance.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/ca8277c3-163b-fa35-0b6d-4f596d04e468)

### Properties
- `BlindHoleDepth` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the blind hole depth.
- `Bolt` (Boolean, get/set) — Indicates whether the instance is a bolt or just a hole.
- `BoltHolesAttributes` (IList<BoltHoleAttributes>, get/set) — The bolt holes attributes.
- `BoltPositions` (ArrayList, get) — Gets the single bolt positions. All the bolt positions are in the XY-plane defined by the bolt group coordinate system. The given positions are in relation to the transformation plane in which the bolt group was selected.
- `BoltSize` (Double, get/set) — The size of the bolts in the group.
- `BoltStandard` (String, get/set) — The standard of the bolt group.
- `BoltType` (BoltGroup.BoltTypeEnum, get/set) — The type of the bolt group.
- `ConnectAssemblies` (Boolean, get/set) — Defines whether to connect a part or an assembly as a secondary part (false) or as a sub-assembly (true).
- `CutLength` (Double, get/set) — The cut length.
- `EndPointOffset` (Offset, get/set) — The end point offset values of the bolt group.
- `ExtraLength` (Double, get/set) — The extra length for the bolts.
- `FirstPosition` (Point, get/set) — The first position point.
- `Hole1` (Boolean, get/set) — Indicates whether the hole 1 is used.
- `Hole2` (Boolean, get/set) — Indicates whether the hole 2 is used.
- `Hole3` (Boolean, get/set) — Indicates whether the hole 3 is used.
- `Hole4` (Boolean, get/set) — Indicates whether the hole 4 is used.
- `Hole5` (Boolean, get/set) — Indicates whether the hole 5 is used.
- `HoleType` (BoltGroup.BoltHoleTypeEnum, get/set) — The special hole type: oversized, slotted, blind or no hole.
- `Identifier` (Identifier, get/set) — The identifier of the object.
- `IsUpToDate` (object, get) — Gets if the object does not have a modification which is not shared.
- `Length` (Double, get/set) — The length. An extra variable that is only set when the bolt group is made of studs instead of bolts.
- `ModificationTime` (object, get) — Gets latest time of the object was modified or created.
- `Nut1` (Boolean, get/set) — Indicates whether the nut 1 is used in the assembly.
- `Nut2` (Boolean, get/set) — Indicates whether the nut 2 is used in the assembly.
- `OtherPartsToBolt` (ArrayList, get) — The other parts to be bolted (read-only).
- `PartToBeBolted` (Part, get/set) — The part to be bolted.
- `PartToBoltTo` (Part, get/set) — The part to bolt to.
- `PlainHoleType` (BoltGroup.BoltPlainHoleTypeEnum, get/set) — Gets or sets the plain hole type.
- `Position` (Position, get/set) — The position attributes.
- `RotateSlots` (BoltGroup.BoltRotateSlotsEnum, get/set) — Gets or sets the rotation of the slots: odd, even or parallel.
- `SecondPosition` (Point, get/set) — The second position point.
- `SlotOffsetX` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the slotted hole offset X.
- `SlotOffsetY` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the slotted hole offset Y.
- `SlottedHoleX` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the X allowance of the slotted hole.
- `SlottedHoleY` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the Y allowance of the slotted hole.
- `StartPointOffset` (Offset, get/set) — The start point offset values of the bolt group.
- `ThreadInMaterial` (BoltGroup.BoltThreadInMaterialEnum, get/set) — Whether the thread goes inside the materal or not.
- `Tolerance` (Double, get/set) — The hole tolerance.
- `Washer1` (Boolean, get/set) — Indicates whether the washer 1 is used in the assembly.
- `Washer2` (Boolean, get/set) — Indicates whether the washer 2 is used in the assembly.
- `Washer3` (Boolean, get/set) — Indicates whether the washer 3 is used in the assembly.

## Boolean (class)

The Boolean class is an abstract base class for boolean operations such as a part cut, an add, a fitting or a cut plane.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/49be33d1-5e49-b9cf-a30f-6292868e03a5)

- `public Boolean()` — Constructs an empty boolean.

### Properties
- `Father` (ModelObject, get/set) — The father object of the boolean operation; the model object instance to operate on.
- `Identifier` (Identifier, get/set) — The identifier of the object.
- `IsUpToDate` (object, get) — Gets if the object does not have a modification which is not shared.
- `ModificationTime` (object, get) — Gets latest time of the object was modified or created.

## BooleanPart (class)

The BooleanPart class represents a part cut or an add. This means that a model object is, for example, cut with a part instance to create a void to the father part. Typically the operative part is deleted after the boolean cut operation. In case of boolean add, operative part is deleted automatically.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/2fbf5949-ba94-3e0d-a738-1ed7ba1bec1d)
