#### `public override bool Insert()`

Inserts the boolean part into the model database. All the attributes must be set. The operative part is overwritten by a copy of the original part.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True on success.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/429e4a44-dd59-97ae-3b93-b5921e272d3a)

#### `public override bool Modify()`

Currently it is not possible to modify the boolean part as there is nothing that can be modified for it. Calling this function will actually call modify for the operative part member.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True on success.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/e257d9f9-d22b-9189-ece5-3a4d1e6bf35f)

#### `public override bool Select()`

Selects a boolean part from the model database. The part ID must be set.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True on success.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/6fef0b3f-6159-9689-c0ee-9c90a7b4f170)

#### `public bool SetCustomObjectType(string type)`

Sets a custom object type name for an existing object.

**Parameters:**
- `type` (System.String) — The custom obect type name to set to the model object.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True on success.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/3d6fb91b-48a9-35ca-d498-526514344089)

#### `public bool SetDynamicStringProperty(string name, string value)`

Sets a dynamic string property for the model object.

**Parameters:**
- `name` (System.String) — The name of the property to be set.
- `value` (System.String) — The new value for the property.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if the property was set successfully.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/4dab0254-09e5-5e61-c28b-1c12afab71a0)

#### `public bool SetLabel(string label)`

Sets a label for an object when a new instance is created, this method must be called before Insert. The label is used in plug-ins for identifying the changed object in modification.

**Parameters:**
- `label` (System.String) — The label to set to the model object.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True on success.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/3efcfc44-613a-e66b-9ebd-9f86df4f4036)

#### `public bool SetOperativePart(Part Part)`

Sets the part to operate with. The operative part's class member must be defined to be BooleanOperativeClass.

**Parameters:**
- `Part` (Tekla.Structures.Model.Part) — The operative part for the boolean.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True on success.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/c70ac6e9-d127-bbbc-28c0-0d22545aa923)

#### `public bool SetPhase(Phase phase)`

Sets the phase of the model object.

**Parameters:**
- `phase` (Tekla.Structures.Model.Phase) — The new phase. A phase with this phase's number must exist in the database.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True on success.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/ed80abcc-0ff7-d081-2229-e651f858a727)

### Properties
- `Father` (ModelObject, get/set) — The father object of the boolean operation; the model object instance to operate on.
- `Identifier` (Identifier, get/set) — The identifier of the object.
- `IsUpToDate` (object, get) — Gets if the object does not have a modification which is not shared.
- `ModificationTime` (object, get) — Gets latest time of the object was modified or created.
- `OperativePart` (Part, get/set) — The part that does the boolean operation. When Insert() is called, this field is overwritten with a new part instance which is a copy of the original operative part. This part has the same identifier as the boolean part. Modifications (such as profile change) have to be made through the part instance and not the boolean part instance. The operative part's class must be BooleanOperativeClass.
- `Type` (BooleanPart.BooleanTypeEnum, get/set) — Defines the boolean operation: an add or a cut.

## BooleanPart.BooleanTypeEnum (enum)

Defines the boolean operation: an add or a cut.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/a103fb75-b005-bb97-51cc-c4f5ff5763af)

### Values
- `BOOLEAN_ADD` = `1` — Defines the add part. Typically the add part type is not used.
- `BOOLEAN_CUT` = `2` — Defines the cut part.
- `BOOLEAN_WELDPREP` = `3` — Defines the weld prep part.

## Brep (class)

The Brep class represents a single brep in the model. A brep has a single start and end point.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/77e7ce8e-76cd-c1d8-1814-1b02750fa9bc)

### Constructors
- `public Brep()` — Initializes a new instance of the Brep class. The start and end points are in the origin.
- `public Brep(Point startPoint, Point endPoint)` — Initializes a new instance of the Brep class with the given start and end points.

### Methods
#### `public int CompareTo(Object obj)`

Compares Identifiers of model objects.

**Parameters:**
- `obj` (System.Object) — The model object to be used in comparison.

**Returns:** `Int32` — 0 if object Identifiers are the same, 1 if given object Identifier is bigger, -1 otherwise.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/79cca356-0c8a-e8fb-463a-34ad4141bec7)

#### `public bool CompareTo(Part partToCompare)`

Compares the instantiated part with another one.

**Remarks:** If the numbering hasn't been performed the function will also return false.

**Parameters:**
- `partToCompare` (Tekla.Structures.Model.Part) — The part to compare with.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if there are no differences, false otherwise.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/e8632c0d-89cf-9aab-6532-f62f9873067b)

Deletes the brep instance with the given identifier from the model database.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True on success.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/171e636e-f6b0-3f4e-7014-63ebef8a99a6)

#### `public bool Equals(ModelObject other)`

Check if Identifiers of model objects are same.

**Parameters:**
- `other` (Tekla.Structures.Model.ModelObject) — The model object to be used in comparison.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if object Identifiers are the same, false otherwise,

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/e4324265-2490-00de-4139-63a8f7acb492)

Inserts the brep into the model database. All the attributes must be set.

### Properties
- `AssemblyNumber` (NumberingSeries, get/set) — Gets or sets the assembly number. Defines the numbering in the assembly sense.
- `CastUnitType` (Part.CastUnitTypeEnum, get/set) — Gets or sets the cast unit type of the part.
- `Class` (String, get/set) — Gets or sets the class of the part.
- `DeformingData` (DeformingData, get/set) — Gets or sets the deforming data of the part.
- `EndPoint` (Point, get/set) — Gets or sets the end point of the brep.
- `EndPointOffset` (Offset, get/set) — Gets or sets the beam's end point offset.
- `Finish` (String, get/set) — Gets or sets the finish of the part.
- `Identifier` (Identifier, get/set) — The identifier of the object.
- `IsUpToDate` (object, get) — Gets if the object does not have a modification which is not shared.
- `Material` (Material, get/set) — Gets or sets the material the part is made of.
- `ModificationTime` (object, get) — Gets latest time of the object was modified or created.
- `Name` (String, get/set) — Gets or sets the name of the part.
- `PartNumber` (NumberingSeries, get/set) — Gets or sets the part number. Defines the numbering in the part sense.
- `Position` (Position, get/set) — Gets or sets the part position. Defines the way the part is positioned in the model.
- `PourPhase` (Int32, get/set) — Gets or sets the pour phase of the part.
- `Profile` (Profile, get/set) — Gets or sets the profile of the part.
- `StartPoint` (Point, get/set) — Gets or sets the start point of the brep.
- `StartPointOffset` (Offset, get/set) — Gets or sets the beam's start point offset.

## Chamfer (class)

The Chamfer class defines how the corners of a polybeam or a contour plate are rounded.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/3064a4cf-a8fc-6b76-b9e1-01173be467df)

### Constructors
- `public Chamfer()` — Creates a chamfer instance with the basic shape type CHAMFER_NONE.
- `public Chamfer(double X, double Y, Chamfer.ChamferTypeEnum Type)` — Instantiates a chamfer with the given values.

### Properties
- `DZ1` (Double, get/set) — The thickness above the offset. Note that this does nothing when used with the PolyBeam.
- `DZ2` (Double, get/set) — The thickness below the offset. Note that this does nothing when used with the PolyBeam.
- `Type` (Chamfer.ChamferTypeEnum, get/set) — The basic shape type of the chamfer.
- `X` (Double, get/set) — The dimension of the chamfer.
- `Y` (Double, get/set) — The second dimension of straight chamfers.

## Chamfer.ChamferTypeEnum (enum)

The basic shape type of the chamfer.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/e17f4130-3566-bee7-b126-0ddfa6a77eea)

### Values
- `CHAMFER_NONE` = `0` — No chamfer type.
- `CHAMFER_LINE` = `1` — The line type chamfer.
- `CHAMFER_ROUNDING` = `2` — The rounding type chamfer.
- `CHAMFER_ARC` = `3` — The arc type chamfer.
- `CHAMFER_ARC_POINT` = `4` — The arc point type chamfer.
- `CHAMFER_SQUARE` = `5` — The square type chamfer.
- `CHAMFER_SQUARE_PARALLEL` = `6` — The square parallel type chamfer.
- `CHAMFER_LINE_AND_ARC` = `7` — The line and arc type chamfer.

## ChangeData (class)

The ChangeData class presents information about the detected change of the modelobject in the model. It is instantiated inside the connected method.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/71408208-1126-6f3d-f4a1-8e524f7875da)

### Properties
- `Object` (ModelObject, get) — Gets the changed object.
- `Source` (ChangeData.ChangeSourceTypeEnum, get) — Gets the change source type.
- `Type` (ChangeData.ChangeTypeEnum, get) — Gets the change type.

## ChangeData.ChangeSourceTypeEnum (enum)

The source types of changed event.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/fe62fb09-f708-2f31-f3d4-57c7300fe40e)

### Values
- `COMMIT` = `0` — Normal commit
- `UNDO_REDO` = `1` — Undo or redo pressed
- `ROLLBACK` = `2` — Rollback of database

## ChangeData.ChangeTypeEnum (enum)

The types of changed event.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/64a75766-987a-6ed2-0a2d-553e8507a5fc)

### Values
- `OBJECT_INSERT` = `0` — Object created
- `OBJECT_MODIFY` = `1` — Object modified
- `OBJECT_DELETE` = `2` — Object deleted
- `USERPROPERTY_CHANGED` = `3` — User property of object changed

## CircleRebarGroup (class)

The CircleRebarGroup class represents a group of reinforcing bars which have a circular shape.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/8b1ad479-c856-307c-b1c3-87b6ad423bec)

### Constructors
- `public CircleRebarGroup()` — Initializes a new circle rebar group instance with empty attributes.

### Methods

Compares Identifiers of model objects.

**Parameters:**
- `obj` (System.Object) — The model object to be used in comparison.

**Returns:** `Int32` — 0 if object Identifiers are the same, 1 if given object Identifier is bigger, -1 otherwise.

#### `public List<RebarComplexGeometry> GetRebarComplexGeometries(bool withHooks, bool withoutClashes, bool lengthAdjustments)`

Retrieves a list of physical reinforcing bars (of type RebarComplexGeometry). These objects contain physical curves in the 3D space of each reinforcing bar as shown in model view. RebarComplexGeometry only differs from RebarGeometry by containing a polycurve rather than a polyline.

**Parameters:**
- `withHooks` (System.Boolean) — A parameter to determine whether the hook points should be included in the rebar geometry or not.
- `withoutClashes` (System.Boolean) — A parameter to determine whether self-clash avoidance should be included in the rebar geometry or not.
- `lengthAdjustments` (System.Boolean) — A parameter to determine whether rounding and step tapering should be included in the rebar geometry or not.

**Returns:** `List<RebarComplexGeometry>` — A list of RebarComplexGeometrys.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/c796aafb-3520-fe06-12e3-a07a87c8f2f9)

#### `public List<RebarComplexGeometry> GetRebarComplexGeometries(bool withHooks, bool withoutClashes, bool lengthAdjustments, Reinforcement.RebarGeometrySimplificationTypeEnum simplified)`

Retrieves a list of physical reinforcing bars (of type RebarComplexGeometry). These objects contain physical curves in the 3D space of each reinforcing bar as shown in model view. RebarComplexGeometry only differs from RebarGeometry by containing a polycurve (of Arc and LineSegment objects) rather than a polyline. The polycurve only contains arcs if the "simplified" parameter is RATIONALIZED or FABRICATION. If "simplified" is NONE, any arcs will be returned as a series of line segments.

**Parameters:**
- `withHooks` (System.Boolean) — A parameter to determine whether the hook points should be included in the rebar geometry or not.
- `withoutClashes` (System.Boolean) — A parameter to determine whether self-clash avoidance should be included in the rebar geometry or not.
- `lengthAdjustments` (System.Boolean) — A parameter to determine whether rounding and step tapering should be included in the rebar geometry or not.
- `simplified` (Tekla.Structures.Model.Reinforcement.RebarGeometrySimplificationTypeEnum) — A parameter to determine whether the "simplified" rebar shape recognition geometry should be returned.

**Returns:** `List<RebarComplexGeometry>` — A list of RebarComplexGeometrys.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/84a3a38b-d7eb-97df-7fba-a34d13de6610)

#### `public ArrayList GetRebarGeometries(bool withHooks)`

Retrieves a list of physical reinforcing bars (of type RebarGeometry). These objects contain physical points in the 3D space of each reinforcing bar.

**Parameters:**
- `withHooks` (System.Boolean) — A parameter to determine whether the hook points should be included in the rebar geometry or not.

**Returns:** `ArrayList` — An array list that contains the RebarGeometry objects.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/75649a4c-6252-09a1-b509-cd815e718c0f)

#### `public ArrayList GetRebarGeometries(Reinforcement.RebarGeometryOptionEnum options)`

Retrieves a list of physical reinforcing bars (of type RebarGeometry). These objects contain physical points in the 3D space of each reinforcing bar.

**Parameters:**
- `options` (Tekla.Structures.Model.Reinforcement.RebarGeometryOptionEnum) — The options.

**Returns:** `ArrayList` — An array list that contains the RebarGeometry objects.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/bc45309d-fc4b-9bf9-c640-e71bb1ade771)

#### `public ArrayList GetRebarGeometriesWithoutClashes(bool withHooks)`

Retrieves a list of physical reinforcing bars (of type RebarGeometry). These objects contain physical points in the 3D space of each reinforcing bar as shown in model view. In case rebar polygon clashes with itself, physical points are moved to avoid clashing.

**Parameters:**
- `withHooks` (System.Boolean) — A parameter to determine whether the hook points should be included in the rebar geometry or not.

**Returns:** `ArrayList` — An array list that contains the RebarGeometry objects.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/470acf1f-1beb-f30a-a1c2-2fcab066ab4a)

#### `public RebarGeometry GetSingleRebar(int index, bool withHooks)`

Returns a single rebar inside the rebar group located by the given index. The indexing starts from the start point. The rebar represents a physical reinforcing bar and contains physical points in the 3D space of the bar. The method returns null on error, for example if given an erroneous index. The number of rebars in the group can be requested with GetNumberOfRebars().

**Parameters:**
- `index` (System.Int32) — The index of the rebar in the group.
- `withHooks` (System.Boolean) — A parameter to determine whether the hook points should be included in the rebar geometry or not.

**Returns:** `RebarGeometry` — The instance of a RebarGeometry object that contains the point list of the single physical reinforcing bar. Null if the given index was not valid.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/41181569-65f8-f4ba-7722-3cd890c7a3d4)

#### `public RebarGeometry GetSingleRebarWithoutClash(int index, bool withHooks)`

Returns a single rebar inside the rebar group located by the given index. The indexing starts from the start point. The rebar represents a physical reinforcing bar and contains physical, non-clashing points in the 3D space of the bar. The method returns null on error, for example if given an erroneous index. The number of rebars in the group can be requested with GetNumberOfRebars().

**Parameters:**
- `index` (System.Int32) — The index of the rebar in the group.
- `withHooks` (System.Boolean) — A parameter to determine whether the hook points should be included in the rebar geometry or not.

**Returns:** `RebarGeometry` — The instance of a RebarGeometry object that contains the point list of the single physical reinforcing bar. Null if the given index was not valid.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/8fd2e21a-3d2f-6211-92ba-9d6e652ac100)

#### `public override bool Insert()`

Inserts the circle rebar group into the model database. All the attributes must be set.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True on success.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/bbc15f10-8acb-3a04-ace8-a9b2042141f1)

#### `public bool IsGeometryValid()`

Tells whether the geometry of a reinforcement object is valid or not.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if the geometry of the reinforcement is valid, false otherwise.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/5699f6a6-4e39-b784-d84f-45eb7b1d0e37)

#### `public override bool Modify()`

Modifies the existing circle rebar group in the model database to match the current one.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True on success.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/48bf80ea-6ea1-6f7e-4bcb-f4cd9ffbed44)

#### `public override bool Select()`

Selects a circle rebar group from the model database. The identifier of the circle rebar group must be set.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True on success.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/978e4b4d-3dc8-2230-03c8-e801ff97f464)

#### `public bool SetCustomObjectType(string type)`

Sets a custom object type name for an existing object.

**Parameters:**
- `type` (System.String) — The custom obect type name to set to the model object.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True on success.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/3d6fb91b-48a9-35ca-d498-526514344089)

#### `public bool SetDynamicStringProperty(string name, string value)`

Sets a dynamic string property for the model object.

**Parameters:**
- `name` (System.String) — The name of the property to be set.
- `value` (System.String) — The new value for the property.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if the property was set successfully.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/4dab0254-09e5-5e61-c28b-1c12afab71a0)

#### `public bool SetLabel(string label)`

Sets a label for an object when a new instance is created, this method must be called before Insert. The label is used in plug-ins for identifying the changed object in modification.

**Parameters:**
- `label` (System.String) — The label to set to the model object.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True on success.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/3efcfc44-613a-e66b-9ebd-9f86df4f4036)

#### `public bool SetPhase(Phase phase)`

Sets the phase of the model object.

**Parameters:**
- `phase` (Tekla.Structures.Model.Phase) — The new phase. A phase with this phase's number must exist in the database.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True on success.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/ed80abcc-0ff7-d081-2229-e651f858a727)

### Properties
- `Class` (Int32, get/set) — Gets or sets the class of the reinforcement. The class is used to group reinforcements.
- `EndFromPlaneOffset` (Double, get/set) — The end offset value from the part surface.
- `EndHook` (RebarHookData, get/set) — The hook at the end of the reinforcing bar.
- `EndPoint` (Point, get/set) — The end point of the direction in which the bars are distributed.
- `EndPointOffsetType` (Reinforcement.RebarOffsetTypeEnum, get/set) — Gets or sets the type of the end point offset. The options are: OFFSET_TYPE_LEG_LENGTHOFFSET_TYPE_COVER_THICKNESS
- `EndPointOffsetValue` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the concrete cover thickness or leg length at the second end of the bar.
- `ExcludeType` (BaseRebarGroup.ExcludeTypeEnum, get/set) — Defines which bars to omit from the group. The options are: EXCLUDE_TYPE_NONEEXCLUDE_TYPE_FIRSTEXCLUDE_TYPE_LASTEXCLUDE_TYPE_BOTH
- `Father` (ModelObject, get/set) — Gets or sets the father object of the reinforcement; the model object instance to operate on.
- `FromPlaneOffset` (Double, get/set) — The offset value from the part surface applied in both sides.
- `Grade` (String, get/set) — Gets or sets the steel grade of the reinforcing bar. The grade indicates the strength of the steel used in reinforcing bars. It can also indicate other factors, such as the weldability or surface deformations of the bar.
- `Identifier` (Identifier, get/set) — The identifier of the object.
- `InputPointDeformingState` (DeformingType, get/set) — Gets or sets the reinforcement input point deforming state.
- `IsUpToDate` (object, get) — Gets if the object does not have a modification which is not shared.
- `ModificationTime` (object, get) — Gets latest time of the object was modified or created.
- `Name` (String, get/set) — Gets or sets the name of the reinforcement.
- `NumberingSeries` (NumberingSeries, get/set) — Gets or sets the numbering series of the reinforcement.
- `OnPlaneOffsets` (ArrayList, get/set) — Gets or sets the double offset value for each leg on the same plane as the bar.
- `Polygon` (Polygon, get/set) — A polygon definition for the circle reinforcing bar group shape. The polygon must have 3 points.
- `RadiusValues` (ArrayList, get/set) — Gets or sets the radius value(s) of the bends in the bar.
- `Size` (String, get/set) — The size of the reinforcement.
- `Spacings` (ArrayList, get/set) — The spacing value(s). If the type of the spacing is SPACING_TYPE_EXACT_NUMBER Spacings has only one value that defines the number of the reinforcing bars.
- `SpacingType` (BaseRebarGroup.RebarGroupSpacingTypeEnum, get/set) — The type of spacing. The options are (BaseRebarGroup.RebarGroupSpacingTypeEnum.): SPACING_TYPE_UNDEFINEDSPACING_TYPE_EXACT_SPACINGSSPACING_TYPE_EXACT_NUMBERSPACING_TYPE_TARGET_SPACESPACING_TYPE_EXACT_SPACE_FLEX_AT_STARTSPACING_TYPE_EXACT_SPACE_FLEX_AT_ENDSPACING_TYPE_EXACT_SPACE_FLEX_AT_BOTHSPACING_TYPE_EXACT_SPACE_FLEX_AT_MIDDLE
- `StartFromPlaneOffset` (Double, get/set) — The start offset value from the part surface.
- `StartHook` (RebarHookData, get/set) — The hook at the beginning of the reinforcing bar.
- `StartPoint` (Point, get/set) — The start point of the direction in which the bars are distributed.
- `StartPointOffsetType` (Reinforcement.RebarOffsetTypeEnum, get/set) — Gets or sets the type of the start point offset is either OFFSET_TYPE_LEG_LENGTH or OFFSET_TYPE_COVER_THICKNESS.
- `StartPointOffsetValue` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the concrete cover thickness or leg length at the first end of the bar.
- `StirrupType` (CircleRebarGroup.CircleRebarGroupStirrupTypeEnum, get/set) — The type of the stirrup. The options are: STIRRUP_TYPE_CIRCLESTIRRUP_TYPE_SPIRAL

## CircleRebarGroup.CircleRebarGroupStirrupTypeEnum (enum)

The different stirrup types.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/1053de24-3e25-9b11-8b1c-30adc1a537b7)

### Values
- `STIRRUP_TYPE_CIRCLE` = `0` — The circle type of stirrup.
- `STIRRUP_TYPE_SPIRAL` = `1` — The spiral type of stirrup.

## ClashCheckData (class)

The ClashCheckData class presents information about the detected clash. It is instantiated inside the onClashDetected method. The objects store the identifiers and type of the clash to be passed on to event subscribers.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/2e113655-9825-9801-b873-2dc75b1549f1)

### Constructors
- `public ClashCheckData()` — Initializes a new instance of the ClashCheckData class

### Properties
- `Object1` (ModelObject, get) — Gets the first clashing object.
- `Object2` (ModelObject, get) — Gets the second clashing object.
- `Overlap` (Double, get) — Gets the overlap of the clash. Only applies to clashes of type CLASH_TYPE_CLASH.
- `Type` (ClashCheckData.ClashTypeEnum, get) — Gets the type of the clash.

## ClashCheckData.ClashTypeEnum (enum)

An enumeration for the different clash types.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/ef3402da-08ed-9713-6f0e-fcd6e9bfe78f)

### Values
- `CLASH_TYPE_INVALID` = `0` — The invalid clash type.
- `CLASH_TYPE_ISINSIDE` = `1` — One object is inside the other object.
- `CLASH_TYPE_EXACTMATCH` = `2` — The exact match clash type.
- `CLASH_TYPE_CLASH` = `3` — The unidentified clash type.
- `CLASH_TYPE_MINDISTANCE` = `4` — The minimum distance clash type.
- `CLASH_TYPE_FAILEDSOLID` = `5` — The failed solid clash type.
- `CLASH_TYPE_CUTTHROUGH` = `6` — The cut through clash type.
- `CLASH_TYPE_COMPLEXCLASH` = `7` — The complex clash type.
- `CLASH_TYPE_FAILEDTEST` = `8` — The failed test clash type.

## ClashCheckHandler (class)

The ClashCheckHandler class contains clash check methods.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/788112d7-7e99-4d1c-c631-4c1a23366696)

### Methods
#### `public ArrayList GetIntersectionBoundingBoxes(Identifier ID1, Identifier ID2)`

Get a list of bounding boxes (AABB) of the clashing volumes of two objects.

**Parameters:**
- `ID1` (Tekla.Structures.Identifier) — The identifier of the first object.
- `ID2` (Tekla.Structures.Identifier) — The identifier of the second object.

**Returns:** `ArrayList` — A list of bounding boxes of the clashing volumes.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/73b07f50-4b4c-cfe3-1e89-d723db38ac7e)

#### `public bool RunClashCheck()`

Starts the clash checking. Uses advanced options sa option values.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True on success.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/866f01a1-9f7b-64ff-5131-476f1162d5db)

#### `public bool RunClashCheckWithOptions(bool betweenReferenceModels, bool betweenReferenceModelsAndComponents, bool objectsInsideReferenceModels, double minDistance, bool betweenParts)`

Starts the clash checking with options.

**Parameters:**
- `betweenReferenceModels` (System.Boolean) — True, if clash check should be performed between reference models.
- `betweenReferenceModelsAndComponents` (System.Boolean) — True, if clash cehck should be performed between reference models and components.
- `objectsInsideReferenceModels` (System.Boolean) — True, if clash check should be performed to objects inside reference models.
- `minDistance` (System.Double) — The minimum distance value.
- `betweenParts` (System.Boolean) — True, if clash check should be performed between native parts.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True on success.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/69286754-a3b8-f783-0f47-ba35a9d72ff9)

#### `public bool RunClashCheckWithOptions(bool betweenReferenceModels, bool objectsInsideReferenceModels, double minDistance, bool betweenParts)`

Starts the clash checking with options.

**Parameters:**
- `betweenReferenceModels` (System.Boolean) — True, if clash check should be performed between reference models.
- `objectsInsideReferenceModels` (System.Boolean) — True, if clash check should be performed to objects inside reference models.
- `minDistance` (System.Double) — The minimum distance value.
- `betweenParts` (System.Boolean) — True, if clash check should be performed between native parts.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True on success.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/efdedd8f-706d-e77e-0f49-3647b6a78ebe)

#### `public bool StopClashCheck()`

Stops the clash checking.

**Remarks:** Stops the clash check only when the Advanced Options' XS_USE_NEW_CLASH_CHECK is set.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True on success.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/a392ba3f-5f87-3d4d-e65e-15c1960e947a)

## Component (class)

The Component class represents a component. A component is a modelling tool that typically assembles multiple parts to build some type of a structure, for example a hall macro or at a smaller scale a reinforcement component (a macro). Also, a custom part is a component.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/63586110-d60f-f5a1-2bce-c5138003cbad)

### Constructors
- `public Component()` — Creates a new component instance.
- `public Component(ComponentInput I)` — Creates a new component instance with the given component input.

### Methods
#### `public int CompareTo(Object obj)`

Compares Identifiers of model objects.

**Parameters:**
- `obj` (System.Object) — The model object to be used in comparison.

**Returns:** `Int32` — 0 if object Identifiers are the same, 1 if given object Identifier is bigger, -1 otherwise.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/79cca356-0c8a-e8fb-463a-34ad4141bec7)

#### `public override bool Delete()`

Deletes the component instance with the given ID from the model database.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True on success.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/d72b85cf-584f-15a4-063d-c20f2d4772fa)

#### `public bool Equals(ModelObject other)`

Check if Identifiers of model objects are same.

**Parameters:**
- `other` (Tekla.Structures.Model.ModelObject) — The model object to be used in comparison.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if object Identifiers are the same, false otherwise,

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/e4324265-2490-00de-4139-63a8f7acb492)

#### `public bool GetAllReportProperties(ArrayList stringNames, ArrayList doubleNames, ArrayList integerNames, ref Hashtable values)`

Retrieves all properties for the model object in one hashtable. Type for the returned value must be checked using type casting.

**Parameters:**
- `stringNames` (System.Collections.ArrayList) — An array list containing the names of the string properties.
- `doubleNames` (System.Collections.ArrayList) — An array list containing the names of the double properties.
- `integerNames` (System.Collections.ArrayList) — An array list containing the names of the integer properties.
- `values` (System.Collections.Hashtable.) — The return value, a hash table list that contains all the values that were retrieved successfully.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if at least one single property was retrieved successfully.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/0708f404-ce77-28e1-4ebe-fa906f68bff8)

#### `public bool GetAllUserProperties(ref Hashtable values)`

Retrieves all properties for the model object in one hashtable. Type for the returned value must be checked using type casting.

**Parameters:**
- `values` (System.Collections.Hashtable.) — The return value, a hash table that contains all the values that were retrieved successfully.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if at least one single property was retrieved successfully.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/63b853a0-0af5-af9c-3ce7-05299664d7f8)

### Properties
- `Identifier` (Identifier, get/set) — The identifier of the object.
- `IsUpToDate` (object, get) — Gets if the object does not have a modification which is not shared.
- `ModificationTime` (object, get) — Gets latest time of the object was modified or created.
- `Name` (String, get/set) — The name of the component. The name identifies custom components or plug-ins.
- `Number` (Int32, get/set) — The number of the component. A number greater than zero identifies system components, for custom components the number is CUSTOM_OBJECT_NUMBER, and for plug-ins the number is PLUGIN_OBJECT_NUMBER.

## ComponentInput (class)

The ComponentInput class handles the input of component objects and positions. A component has one component input. The component input must include all the needed input objects and points in the correct order. For example, an input with a point and a part is different from an input of a part and a point. The maximum number of different input items in the collection is 10.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/2e678a2a-118b-ca18-2776-91cd29d4540c)

### Constructors
- `public ComponentInput()` — Creates a new component input object.

### Methods
#### `public bool AddInputObject(ModelObject M)`

Adds a model object to the component input.

**Parameters:**
- `M` (Tekla.Structures.Model.ModelObject) — The model object to be added.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True on success.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/a5d879e1-4952-2600-40db-fdab2637adae)

Adds an array list of model objects to the component input.

**Parameters:**
- `Objects` (System.Collections.ArrayList) — A list of model objects in the correct order.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True on success.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/f7711bd2-4bf6-9b33-dd62-dc5702e62a2b)

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/d50f8295-7d7b-90ce-e1a2-4a38b0a05826)

- `P` (Tekla.Structures.Geometry3d.Point) — The position to be added.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True on success.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/8aca8df7-49a3-0a9c-b4cd-66ba8b611345)

Adds two input positions to the component input.

**Parameters:**
- `Position1` (Tekla.Structures.Geometry3d.Point) — The first position to be added.
- `Position2` (Tekla.Structures.Geometry3d.Point) — The second position to be added.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True on success.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/63f0c2d2-025a-9c81-915f-fd8df69f744a)

Copies the elements of the ICollection to an Array, starting at a particular Array index.

**Parameters:**
- `array` (System.Array) — The one-dimensional Array that is the destination of the elements copied from the ICollection. The Array must have zero-based indexing.
- `index` (System.Int32) — The zero-based index in the array at which copying begins.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/42454497-9e17-cd6b-cda6-b7ec6decbe1f)

Returns an enumerator that iterates through a collection.

**Returns:** `IEnumerator` — An IEnumerator object that can be used to iterate through the collection.

### Properties
- `Count` (Int32, get) — Gets the number of elements contained in the ICollection.
- `IsSynchronized` (Boolean, get) — Gets a value indicating whether access to the ICollection is synchronized (thread safe).
- `SyncRoot` (Object, get) — Gets an object that can be used to synchronize access to the ICollection.

## ConicalSurface (class)

The ConicalSurface class defines a conical surface contour.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/782c39eb-4018-ceec-cbf2-c8502d926da0)

### Constructors
- `public ConicalSurface(Arc baseArc, double height, double radiusAtHeight)` — Initializes a new instance of the ConicalSurface class from a base arc, a height and a radius at the top. This is the recommended constructor if no intermediate points are desired along the lateral boundaries of the bend surface.
- `public ConicalSurface(List<Point> lateralBoundary1, List<Point> lateralBoundary2, Line centerLine, Vector rotationAxis)` — Initializes a new instance of the ConicalSurface class from the cone lateral boundaries and the center line. This is the recommended constructor if fine grained control is desired over the lateral boundaries of the surface.
- `public ConicalSurface(Vector endFaceNormal1, Vector endFaceNormal2, List<Point> lateralBoundary1, List<Point> lateralBoundary2)` — Initializes a new instance of the ConicalSurface class with given parameters. The boundaries are defined by the lateral boundaries (i.e. the points on the sides of the curved part of the cone). The side boundaries are defined by the two first points and the two last points of the lateral boundaries.
- `public ConicalSurface(Vector endFaceNormal1, Vector endFaceNormal2, LineSegment sideBoundary1, LineSegment sideBoundary2)` — Initializes a new instance of the ConicalSurface class with given parameters. The boundaries are defined by the side boundaries (i.e. generator lines of the cone).

### Properties
- `Apex` (Point, get) — Gets the apex of the cone. If it does not exist, it returns null
- `CenterLine` (Line, get) — Gets the center line of the bend surface (i.e. the line that crosses every circular cross section of the bend at their center point)
- `EndFaceNormal1` (Vector, get) — Gets the first end face normal.
- `EndFaceNormal2` (Vector, get) — Gets the second end face normal.
- `IntersectionLine` (Line, get) — Gets intersection line.
- `InwardCurved` (Boolean, get) — Gets the direction of the curve - true if the curve is oriented towards the intersection line, false otherwise.
- `LateralBoundary1` (List<Point>, get) — Gets or sets the first lateral boundary
- `LateralBoundary2` (List<Point>, get) — Gets or sets the second lateral boundary
- `Radiuses` (Tuple<Double,Double>, get) — Gets radiuses of the conical surface. The first radius corresponds to the section located in the first point of the side boundary 1, and the second radius corresponds to the section located in the second point of the side boundary 1.
- `RotationAxis` (Vector, get) — Gets the unit vector that defines the rotation axis (for counter clockwise rotations) of the lateral boundaries of the surface. This axis is always parallel to the direction of the center line.
- `SideBoundary1` (LineSegment, get/set) — Gets or sets the first side boundary.
- `SideBoundary2` (LineSegment, get/set) — Gets or sets the second side boundary.

## ConicalSurfaceNode (class)

The ConicalSurfaceNode class represents conical surface geometry tree node.

## Connection (class)

The Connection class represents a connection. A connection is something that connects two or more parts together.

#### `public override bool Insert()`

Inserts the connection into the model database. All the attributes must be set.

#### `public bool LoadAttributesFromFile(string Filename)`

Loads the attributes for the component from the given file. These attributes will be loaded before all the attributes that have been set with the SetAttribute methods, so any attributes that are set with SetAttribute will override those loaded from the given standard file.

**Parameters:**
- `Filename` (System.String) — The name of the file.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if a file name is specified.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/acc9930a-aa6f-667c-0878-7ea80ce416b8)

### Properties
- `AutoDirectionType` (AutoDirectionTypeEnum, get/set) — The auto direction type. Default value is AUTODIR_BASIC. Value should be AUTODIR_NA to set direction with UpVector property. Value should be AUTODIR_FROM_ATTRIBUTE_FILE to set direction with connection attribute file.
- `Class` (Int32, get/set) — The class of the connection.
- `Code` (String, get/set) — The code of the connection. The code can be used to classify the connection. The code of the connection can be reported and shown in drawings. The maximum length of the string is 20 characters.
- `Identifier` (Identifier, get/set) — The identifier of the object.
- `IsUpToDate` (object, get) — Gets if the object does not have a modification which is not shared.
- `ModificationTime` (object, get) — Gets latest time of the object was modified or created.
- `Name` (String, get/set) — The name of the component. The name identifies custom components or plug-ins.
- `Number` (Int32, get/set) — The number of the component. A number greater than zero identifies system components, for custom components the number is CUSTOM_OBJECT_NUMBER, and for plug-ins the number is PLUGIN_OBJECT_NUMBER.
- `PositionType` (PositionTypeEnum, get/set) — The position type. When creating custom connections this attribute is not applied, instead the position type used when creating the custom connection is used.
- `Status` (ConnectionStatusEnum, get) — The read only status of the connection. The status can be reported and shown in drawings. The color of the connection symbol in the model indicates the status of the connection.
- `UpVector` (Vector, get/set) — A vector indicating which direction is considered the up direction for the connection.

## ConnectiveGeometry (class)

A class for geometry formed of several geometry sections. Geometry is initialized with polygon geometry and then can be extended by calling one of AddLeg methods using BentPlateGeometrySolver class. In the case of success, two polygon geometries will be connected by cylindrical surface geometry possibly with help of additional polygon geometries. Resulted geometry, then, can be set to bent plate.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/f376b309-e3fe-c8f6-d8cd-805695550b9d)

### Constructors
- `public ConnectiveGeometry(BendSurface bendSurface)` — Initializes a new instance of the ConnectiveGeometry class for a stand-alone bend creation.
- `public ConnectiveGeometry(Contour contour)` — Initializes a new instance of the ConnectiveGeometry class.

### Methods
#### `public IList<LineSegment> GetConnection(GeometrySection geometrySection1, GeometrySection geometrySection2)`

Returns 2 connecting line segments between geometrySection1 and geometrySection2 sections, if sections are connected, otherwise returns empty list.

**Parameters:**
- `geometrySection1` (Tekla.Structures.Model.GeometrySection) — The first GeometrySection.
- `geometrySection2` (Tekla.Structures.Model.GeometrySection) — The second GeometrySection.

**Returns:** `IList<LineSegment>` — 2 connecting line segments if sections are connected, otherwise returns empty list.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/97fdc8ce-5c44-0d5b-0cf1-0477b2bf7ee7)

#### `public GeometrySectionEnumerator GetGeometryEnumerator()`

Gets a new geometry sections enumerator for enumerating through the geometry sections.

**Returns:** `GeometrySectionEnumerator` — The new geometry sections enumerator.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/4b345f2f-7977-8d1d-bfe8-df07f99c4c91)

#### `public IList<GeometrySection> GetGeometryLegSections()`

Gets geometry end GeometrySections which are possible to remove.

**Returns:** `IList<GeometrySection>` — Returns List of the removable GeometrySections.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/68035fba-42ff-c225-5b85-2fd9b6664c21)

#### `public IList<GeometrySection> GetNeighborSections(GeometrySection geometrySection)`

Gets neighbor sections of the geometrySection inside this ConnectiveGeometry.

**Parameters:**
- `geometrySection` (Tekla.Structures.Model.GeometrySection) — GeometrySection to get neighbors for.

**Returns:** `IList<GeometrySection>` — List of neighbor GeometrySection of the given GeometrySection.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/7ffd60f3-2ecb-c6fe-beba-c9e01964d2db)

#### `public bool IsEmpty()`

Checks whether the geometry is degenerate

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True, if the geometry does not contain any section.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/a5b27561-22c1-e67a-ba34-971391059a30)

## ConnectiveGeometryException (class)

The ConnectiveGeometryException class represents an error that occurred during the ConnectiveGeometry creation or modification.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/bb701f1a-d0bc-4b22-23ab-8484219793af)

### Constructors
- `public ConnectiveGeometryException()` — Initializes a new instance of the ConnectiveGeometryException class with general connective geometry error message.
- `public ConnectiveGeometryException(BentPlateGeometrySolver.OperationStatus status, string errorMessage)` — Initializes a new instance of the ConnectiveGeometryException class with connective geometry error message based on status.

## Contour (class)

The Contour class defines a possibly chamfered contour. Contours must not have loops.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/903abf10-4c92-e708-533a-88c7cebfd501)

### Constructors
- `public Contour()` — Instantiates an empty contour.

### Methods
#### `public void AddContourPoint(ContourPoint Point)`

Adds a new contour point to the contour. Do not add the same contour point twice.

**Parameters:**
- `Point` (Tekla.Structures.Model.ContourPoint) — The contour point to add.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/758831d8-d612-1ccd-aa64-e3c0d07baa1d)

#### `public bool CalculatePolygon(out Polygon polygon)`

Calculate a polygon approximating the contour with chamfers evaluated.

**Parameters:**
- `polygon` (Tekla.Structures.Model.Polygon.) — The calculated Polygon.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if successful, otherwise false.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/f5c142bc-8a2b-dee2-5b19-26863e8410a0)

#### `public Polycurve GetPolycurve()`

Gets the contour geometry as Polycurve

**Returns:** `Polycurve` — Polycurve

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/1a21df7a-cb44-dada-7422-69a6052fad72)

### Properties
- `ContourPoints` (ArrayList, get/set) — The contour points belonging to the contour.

## ContourPlate (class)

The ContourPlate class represents a part made with a contour, such as, a concrete slab.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/ec399c5b-d8e9-a69a-57c8-d85f70ac3c51)

### Constructors
- `public ContourPlate()` — Initializes a new instance of the ContourPlate class.

### Methods
#### `public bool AddContourPoint(ContourPoint contourPoint)`

Adds a contour point to the contour plate.

**Parameters:**
- `contourPoint` (Tekla.Structures.Model.ContourPoint) — The contour point that is to be inserted.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True on success, false if there is no more space for another contour point.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/e76c9697-5ba1-7d4a-69d0-62f148d12938)

Compares Identifiers of model objects.

**Parameters:**

**Returns:** `Int32` — 0 if object Identifiers are the same, 1 if given object Identifier is bigger, -1 otherwise.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/79cca356-0c8a-e8fb-463a-34ad4141bec7)

Compares the instantiated part with another one.

- `partToCompare` (Tekla.Structures.Model.Part) — The part to compare with.

#### `public override bool Insert()`

Inserts the contour plate into the model database. All the attributes must be set.

**Remarks:** Internal code of TeklaStructures can rearrange points of Contour to get a valid coordinate system. Calling method Select after inserting plate is recommended to get the Contour as stored in model database.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True on success.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/c02b023b-a8be-925c-f2bf-7f3cee59c03d)

#### `public override bool Modify()`

Modifies the existing contour plate in the model database to match the current one. The identifier must be set.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True on success.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/15700480-e315-b734-7e0d-129e39990368)

#### `public override bool Select()`

Selects a contour plate from the model database. The identifier of the contour plate must be set.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True on success.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/cc2b528a-c78d-8237-3171-8ae0c6650913)

#### `public bool SetCustomObjectType(string type)`

Sets a custom object type name for an existing object.

**Parameters:**
- `type` (System.String) — The custom obect type name to set to the model object.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True on success.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/3d6fb91b-48a9-35ca-d498-526514344089)

#### `public bool SetDynamicStringProperty(string name, string value)`

Sets a dynamic string property for the model object.

**Parameters:**
- `name` (System.String) — The name of the property to be set.
- `value` (System.String) — The new value for the property.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if the property was set successfully.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/4dab0254-09e5-5e61-c28b-1c12afab71a0)

#### `public bool SetLabel(string label)`

Sets a label for an object when a new instance is created, this method must be called before Insert. The label is used in plug-ins for identifying the changed object in modification.

**Parameters:**
- `label` (System.String) — The label to set to the model object.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True on success.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/3efcfc44-613a-e66b-9ebd-9f86df4f4036)

### Properties
- `AssemblyNumber` (NumberingSeries, get/set) — Gets or sets the assembly number. Defines the numbering in the assembly sense.
- `CastUnitType` (Part.CastUnitTypeEnum, get/set) — Gets or sets the cast unit type of the part.
- `Class` (String, get/set) — Gets or sets the class of the part.
- `Contour` (Contour, get/set) — Gets or sets the contour for the contour plate.
- `DeformingData` (DeformingData, get/set) — Gets or sets the deforming data of the part.
- `Finish` (String, get/set) — Gets or sets the finish of the part.
- `Identifier` (Identifier, get/set) — The identifier of the object.
- `IsUpToDate` (object, get) — Gets if the object does not have a modification which is not shared.
- `LevelName` (String, get/set) — Gets or sets the level name.
- `LevelOffset` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the level offset.
- `Material` (Material, get/set) — Gets or sets the material the part is made of.
- `ModificationTime` (object, get) — Gets latest time of the object was modified or created.
- `Name` (String, get/set) — Gets or sets the name of the part.
- `PartNumber` (NumberingSeries, get/set) — Gets or sets the part number. Defines the numbering in the part sense.
- `Position` (Position, get/set) — Gets or sets the part position. Defines the way the part is positioned in the model.
- `PourPhase` (Int32, get/set) — Gets or sets the pour phase of the part.
- `Profile` (Profile, get/set) — Gets or sets the profile of the part.
- `Type` (ContourPlate.ContourPlateTypeEnum, get) — Gets the read only type of the contour plate. In creation, the type is defined based on the material. If the material is concrete, the type will be slab, otherwise plate.

## ContourPlate.ContourPlateTypeEnum (enum)

The contour plate types.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/22f8144f-78df-e35b-1d18-c9999523d61b)

### Values
- `UNKNOWN` = `0` — The unknown type.
- `PLATE` = `1` — The plate-like contour plate.
- `SLAB` = `2` — The slab-like contour plate.

## ContourPoint (class)

The ContourPoint class defines a point with possible chamfering information, used, for example, to define a contour plate.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/1f158311-3d42-5ee3-4074-7cb13d653826)

### Constructors
- `public ContourPoint()` — Creates a new contour point instance.
- `public ContourPoint(Point P, Chamfer C)` — Creates a new contour point using the given point and chamfer.

### Methods
#### `public int CompareTo(Object obj)`

Compares two points. To use binarysearch somekind of sorting should be used.

**Parameters:**
- `obj` (System.Object) — The point to be compared.

**Returns:** `Int32` — 0 if both are equal, -1 if this point is before, 1 if this point is after.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/2f202828-05fd-220f-92b1-5b64372a4f7e)

#### `public override bool Equals(Object obj)`

Returns true if the current object and the given object are equal.

**Parameters:**
- `obj` (System.Object) — The object we wish to check the equality with.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if the given object equals the current object.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/dbca17f2-6730-e2c4-583c-90c74c72e0b0)

#### `public override int GetHashCode()`

Returns a hash code for the point. Notice, in extremely rare cases, you might not get the same hash code for two points even though they are considered equal! This should, however, happen only in extremely rare cases!

**Returns:** `Int32` — The hash code for the point.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/9f84f024-170b-5181-770d-5ed2f56332cd)

#### `public void SetPoint(Point P)`

Sets the point's coordinates for the contour point.

**Parameters:**
- `P` (Tekla.Structures.Geometry3d.Point) — The point.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/caa63223-3fa6-7f07-5e3f-1d8e7b7386d0)

#### `public string ToString(CultureInfo cultureInfo)`

Formats the point into a string, using the provided culture.

**Parameters:**
- `cultureInfo` (System.Globalization.CultureInfo)

**Returns:** `String` — The string that represents the point.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/176a7423-000a-5fb0-dab9-659b87bf1505)

#### `public override string ToString()`

Formats the point into a string, using the current culture.

**Returns:** `String` — The string that represents the point.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/63db29a6-32f7-6696-31eb-a6084dcb62cd)

#### `public virtual void Translate(double X, double Y, double Z)`

Translates the point using the given vector.

**Parameters:**
- `X` (System.Double) — The X-translation to be used.
- `Y` (System.Double) — The Y-translation to be used.
- `Z` (System.Double) — The Z-translation to be used.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/7ad7ecb9-acf3-7ed2-f2bc-aa52b99ceae6)

#### `public void Zero()`

Zeros all the members of the point.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/9bf63e0c-fe71-40ae-e467-306f0a41f315)

### Properties
- `Chamfer` (Chamfer, get/set) — The chamfer for the contour point.

## ControlArc (class)

The ControlArc class defines a user defined arc helping in modeling work.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/5b51428a-00f9-155b-333c-83bd261a0b88)

### Constructors
- `public ControlArc()` — Creates a new control arc instance. The default values are as follows: CenterPoint = Point(0.0, 0.0, 0.0);StartPoint = Point(0.0, 1000.0, 0.0);EndPoint = Point(1000.0, 0.0, 0.0);Color = ControlObjectColorEnum.YELLOW;
- `public ControlArc(Point startPoint, Point endPoint, Point pointOnArc)` — Creates a new control arc instance taking the 3 points.

### Methods
#### `public int CompareTo(Object obj)`

Compares Identifiers of model objects.

**Parameters:**
- `obj` (System.Object) — The model object to be used in comparison.

**Returns:** `Int32` — 0 if object Identifiers are the same, 1 if given object Identifier is bigger, -1 otherwise.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/79cca356-0c8a-e8fb-463a-34ad4141bec7)

### Properties
- `Color` (ControlObjectColorEnum, get/set) — Gets or sets color of the control arc.
- `Geometry` (Arc, get/set) — Gets or sets the arc geometry. The arc must not represent a complete circle (i.e. the arc angle must be less than 360 degrees).
- `Identifier` (Identifier, get/set) — The identifier of the object.
- `IsUpToDate` (object, get) — Gets if the object does not have a modification which is not shared.
- `LineType` (ControlObjectLineType, get/set) — The line type of the control arc.
- `ModificationTime` (object, get) — Gets latest time of the object was modified or created.

## ControlCircle (class)

The ControlCircle class defines a user defined (not magnetic) circle helping in modeling work. If there are duplicated input points or 3 input points are in a line, ControlCircle.Insert() will return false. ControlCircle.Select() will return the center point of the circle to Point1.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/9763b8ff-1f52-2d69-7549-a6d8733e945c)

### Constructors
- `public ControlCircle()` — Initializes a new instance of the ControlCircle class. The default values are as follows: Color = ControlCircleColorEnum.YELLOW; Extension = 0.0;
- `public ControlCircle(Point point1, Point point2, Point point3)` — Initializes a new instance of the ControlCircle class. The control circle is not magnetic.

Compares Identifiers of model objects.

**Parameters:**
- `obj` (System.Object) — The model object to be used in comparison.

**Returns:** `Int32` — 0 if object Identifiers are the same, 1 if given object Identifier is bigger, -1 otherwise.

- `value` (System.String) — The new value for the property.

### Properties
- `Color` (ControlCircle.ControlCircleColorEnum, get/set) — Gets or sets the color of the control circle.
- `Extension` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the extension of the control circle.
- `Identifier` (Identifier, get/set) — The identifier of the object.
- `IsUpToDate` (object, get) — Gets if the object does not have a modification which is not shared.
- `LineType` (ControlObjectLineType, get/set) — The line type of the control circle.
- `ModificationTime` (object, get) — Gets latest time of the object was modified or created.
- `Point1` (Point, get/set) — Gets or sets the first point. Point1 becomes the center point of the circle after selecting the control circle.
- `Point2` (Point, get/set) — Gets or sets the second point. Point2 contains the original input Point1 after selecting the control circle.
- `Point3` (Point, get/set) — Gets or sets the third point. Point3 contains the calculated result from the orthogonal point and the center point after selecting the control circle.

## ControlCircle.ControlCircleColorEnum (enum)

The different circle colors.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/20466415-4abb-34c8-b445-f7a9e03810da)

### Values
- `YELLOW_RED` = `-1` — The yellow/red color.
- `BLACK` = `0` — The black color.
- `WHITE` = `1` — The white color.
- `RED` = `2` — The red color.
- `GREEN` = `3` — The green color.
- `BLUE` = `4` — The blue color.
- `CYAN` = `5` — The cyan color.
- `YELLOW` = `6` — The yellow color.
- `MAGENTA` = `7` — The magenta color.

## ControlLine (class)

The ControlLine class defines a user defined (possibly magnetic) line helping in modeling work.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/8f08f0af-9bc7-d718-4bba-cd6f23c4b0e1)

### Constructors
- `public ControlLine()` — Creates a new control line instance. The default values are as follows: Line = new LineSegment();IsMagnetic = false;Color = ControlLineColorEnum.YELLOW;Extension = 0.0;
- `public ControlLine(LineSegment Line, bool IsMagnetic)` — Creates a new control line instance taking the line segment and magnetism as input.

### Properties
- `Color` (ControlLine.ControlLineColorEnum, get/set) — The color of the control line.
- `Extension` (Double, get/set) — The extension of the control line.
- `Identifier` (Identifier, get/set) — The identifier of the object.
- `IsMagnetic` (Boolean, get/set) — Determines whether the line is magnetic or not.
- `IsUpToDate` (object, get) — Gets if the object does not have a modification which is not shared.
- `Line` (LineSegment, get/set) — The line segment defining the position of the control line.
- `LineType` (ControlObjectLineType, get/set) — The line type of the control line.
- `ModificationTime` (object, get) — Gets latest time of the object was modified or created.

## ControlLine.ControlLineColorEnum (enum)

The different line colors.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/5be6efb4-cfe5-6f0b-1ebc-be8609d0bc05)

### Values
- `YELLOW_RED` = `-1` — The yellow/red color.
- `BLACK` = `0` — The black color.
- `WHITE` = `1` — The white color.
- `RED` = `2` — The red color.
- `GREEN` = `3` — The green color.
- `BLUE` = `4` — The blue color.
- `CYAN` = `5` — The cyan color.
- `YELLOW` = `6` — The yellow color.
- `MAGENTA` = `7` — The magenta color.

## ControlObjectColorEnum (enum)

The different colors used for control objects (ControlLine and ControlCircle have their own).

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/de19d580-60b4-961b-dfb1-107560ed1ed2)

### Values
- `YELLOW_RED` = `-1` — The yellow/red color.
- `BLACK` = `0` — The black color.
- `WHITE` = `1` — The white color.
- `RED` = `2` — The red color.
- `GREEN` = `3` — The green color.
- `BLUE` = `4` — The blue color.
- `CYAN` = `5` — The cyan color.
- `YELLOW` = `6` — The yellow color.
- `MAGENTA` = `7` — The magenta color.

## ControlObjectLineType (enum)

The line types (the presentation of the line).

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/1e738aca-83ee-237a-9285-eceb80bc6c51)

### Values
- `SolidLine` = `1` — The solid line (default).
- `DashedLine` = `2` — The dashed line (-----).
- `SlashedLine` = `3` — The slashed line (- - -).
- `DashDot` = `4` — The dash dotted line (--.--.).
- `DottedLine` = `5` — The dotted line (.....).

## ControlPlane (class)

The ControlPlane class defines a user defined (possibly magnetic) plane helping in modeling work.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/561ea178-df9a-d196-a846-e3223a8b202f)

### Constructors
- `public ControlPlane()` — Creates a new control plane instance. The default values are as follows: Plane = new Plane();IsMagnetic = false;Name = "Plane";
- `public ControlPlane(Plane P, bool IsMagnetic)` — Creates a new control plane instance taking the plane and magnetism as input.

### Properties
- `Identifier` (Identifier, get/set) — The identifier of the object.
- `IsMagnetic` (Boolean, get/set) — Determines whether the plane is magnetic or not.
- `IsUpToDate` (object, get) — Gets if the object does not have a modification which is not shared.
- `ModificationTime` (object, get) — Gets latest time of the object was modified or created.
- `Name` (String, get/set) — The name of the control plane.
- `Plane` (Plane, get/set) — The plane defining the position of the control plane.

## ControlPoint (class)

The ControlPoint class defines a user defined point helping in modeling work.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/38b3f184-d532-5d3e-d59e-2d16a7e0f2d9)

### Constructors
- `public ControlPoint()` — Initializes a new instance of the ControlPoint class with zero members.
- `public ControlPoint(Point existPoint)` — Initializes a new instance of the ControlPoint class with an existing point.

### Methods
#### `public int CompareTo(Object obj)`

Compares Identifiers of model objects.

**Parameters:**
- `obj` (System.Object) — The model object to be used in comparison.

**Returns:** `Int32` — 0 if object Identifiers are the same, 1 if given object Identifier is bigger, -1 otherwise.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/79cca356-0c8a-e8fb-463a-34ad4141bec7)

Deletes the control point with the given Identifier.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/dd5b828d-3a87-bb58-5ac9-e1c3b4c2fe4b)

#### `public bool SetUserProperty(string name, string value)`

Sets a string property for the model object.

**Parameters:**
- `name` (System.String) — The name of the property to be set.
- `value` (System.String) — The new value for the property.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if the property was set successfully.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/80bf0348-b651-3d4a-862b-49c85bc924ab)

### Properties
- `Identifier` (Identifier, get/set) — The identifier of the object.
- `IsUpToDate` (object, get) — Gets if the object does not have a modification which is not shared.
- `ModificationTime` (object, get) — Gets latest time of the object was modified or created.
- `Point` (Point, get/set) — Gets or sets the position of the control point.

## ControlPolycurve (class)

The ControlPolycurve class defines a user defined polycurve object which contains a chain of line and arc geometries.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/cd91f00f-1681-cfc5-3616-ff8e229a29bc)

### Constructors
- `public ControlPolycurve()` — Initializes a new instance of the ControlPolycurve class. The default values are as follows: Color = ControlObjectColorEnum.BLUE;LineType = LineType.DashDot;
- `public ControlPolycurve(Polycurve geometry)` — Initializes a new instance of the ControlPolycurve class.

### Properties
- `Color` (ControlObjectColorEnum, get/set) — Gets or sets color of the Polycurve.
- `Geometry` (Polycurve, get/set) — Gets or sets geometry list that defines geometry of the polycurve.
- `Identifier` (Identifier, get/set) — The identifier of the object.
- `IsUpToDate` (object, get) — Gets if the object does not have a modification which is not shared.
- `LineType` (ControlObjectLineType, get/set) — Gets or sets the line type of the polycurve.
- `ModificationTime` (object, get) — Gets latest time of the object was modified or created.

## CurvedRebarGroup (class)

The CurvedRebarGroup class represents a group of reinforcing bars which have a curved shape.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/9875d65f-111f-f5bb-6ba0-e8cb263099ab)

#### `public List<RebarComplexGeometry> GetRebarComplexGeometries(bool withHooks, bool withoutClashes, bool lengthAdjustments)`

Retrieves a list of physical reinforcing bars (of type RebarComplexGeometry). These objects contain physical curves in the 3D space of each reinforcing bar as shown in model view. RebarComplexGeometry only differs from RebarGeometry by containing a polycurve rather than a polyline.

**Parameters:**
- `withHooks` (System.Boolean) — A parameter to determine whether the hook points should be included in the rebar geometry or not.
- `withoutClashes` (System.Boolean) — A parameter to determine whether self-clash avoidance should be included in the rebar geometry or not.
- `lengthAdjustments` (System.Boolean) — A parameter to determine whether rounding and step tapering should be included in the rebar geometry or not.

**Returns:** `List<RebarComplexGeometry>` — A list of RebarComplexGeometrys.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/c796aafb-3520-fe06-12e3-a07a87c8f2f9)

#### `public List<RebarComplexGeometry> GetRebarComplexGeometries(bool withHooks, bool withoutClashes, bool lengthAdjustments, Reinforcement.RebarGeometrySimplificationTypeEnum simplified)`

Retrieves a list of physical reinforcing bars (of type RebarComplexGeometry). These objects contain physical curves in the 3D space of each reinforcing bar as shown in model view. RebarComplexGeometry only differs from RebarGeometry by containing a polycurve (of Arc and LineSegment objects) rather than a polyline. The polycurve only contains arcs if the "simplified" parameter is RATIONALIZED or FABRICATION. If "simplified" is NONE, any arcs will be returned as a series of line segments.

**Parameters:**
- `withHooks` (System.Boolean) — A parameter to determine whether the hook points should be included in the rebar geometry or not.
- `withoutClashes` (System.Boolean) — A parameter to determine whether self-clash avoidance should be included in the rebar geometry or not.
- `lengthAdjustments` (System.Boolean) — A parameter to determine whether rounding and step tapering should be included in the rebar geometry or not.
- `simplified` (Tekla.Structures.Model.Reinforcement.RebarGeometrySimplificationTypeEnum) — A parameter to determine whether the "simplified" rebar shape recognition geometry should be returned.

**Returns:** `List<RebarComplexGeometry>` — A list of RebarComplexGeometrys.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/84a3a38b-d7eb-97df-7fba-a34d13de6610)

#### `public ArrayList GetRebarGeometries(bool withHooks)`

Retrieves a list of physical reinforcing bars (of type RebarGeometry). These objects contain physical points in the 3D space of each reinforcing bar.

**Parameters:**
- `withHooks` (System.Boolean) — A parameter to determine whether the hook points should be included in the rebar geometry or not.

**Returns:** `ArrayList` — An array list that contains the RebarGeometry objects.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/75649a4c-6252-09a1-b509-cd815e718c0f)

#### `public ArrayList GetRebarGeometries(Reinforcement.RebarGeometryOptionEnum options)`

Retrieves a list of physical reinforcing bars (of type RebarGeometry). These objects contain physical points in the 3D space of each reinforcing bar.

**Parameters:**
- `options` (Tekla.Structures.Model.Reinforcement.RebarGeometryOptionEnum) — The options.

**Returns:** `ArrayList` — An array list that contains the RebarGeometry objects.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/bc45309d-fc4b-9bf9-c640-e71bb1ade771)

#### `public ArrayList GetRebarGeometriesWithoutClashes(bool withHooks)`

Retrieves a list of physical reinforcing bars (of type RebarGeometry). These objects contain physical points in the 3D space of each reinforcing bar as shown in model view. In case rebar polygon clashes with itself, physical points are moved to avoid clashing.

**Parameters:**
- `withHooks` (System.Boolean) — A parameter to determine whether the hook points should be included in the rebar geometry or not.

**Returns:** `ArrayList` — An array list that contains the RebarGeometry objects.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/470acf1f-1beb-f30a-a1c2-2fcab066ab4a)

#### `public RebarGeometry GetSingleRebar(int index, bool withHooks)`

Returns a single rebar inside the rebar group located by the given index. The indexing starts from the start point. The rebar represents a physical reinforcing bar and contains physical points in the 3D space of the bar. The method returns null on error, for example if given an erroneous index. The number of rebars in the group can be requested with GetNumberOfRebars().

**Parameters:**
- `index` (System.Int32) — The index of the rebar in the group.
- `withHooks` (System.Boolean) — A parameter to determine whether the hook points should be included in the rebar geometry or not.

**Returns:** `RebarGeometry` — The instance of a RebarGeometry object that contains the point list of the single physical reinforcing bar. Null if the given index was not valid.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/41181569-65f8-f4ba-7722-3cd890c7a3d4)

#### `public RebarGeometry GetSingleRebarWithoutClash(int index, bool withHooks)`

Returns a single rebar inside the rebar group located by the given index. The indexing starts from the start point. The rebar represents a physical reinforcing bar and contains physical, non-clashing points in the 3D space of the bar. The method returns null on error, for example if given an erroneous index. The number of rebars in the group can be requested with GetNumberOfRebars().

**Parameters:**
- `index` (System.Int32) — The index of the rebar in the group.
- `withHooks` (System.Boolean) — A parameter to determine whether the hook points should be included in the rebar geometry or not.

**Returns:** `RebarGeometry` — The instance of a RebarGeometry object that contains the point list of the single physical reinforcing bar. Null if the given index was not valid.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/8fd2e21a-3d2f-6211-92ba-9d6e652ac100)

### Properties
- `Class` (Int32, get/set) — Gets or sets the class of the reinforcement. The class is used to group reinforcements.
- `EndFromPlaneOffset` (Double, get/set) — The end offset value from the part surface.
- `EndHook` (RebarHookData, get/set) — The hook at the end of the reinforcing bar.
- `EndPoint` (Point, get/set) — The end point of the direction in which the bars are distributed.
- `EndPointOffsetType` (Reinforcement.RebarOffsetTypeEnum, get/set) — Gets or sets the type of the end point offset. The options are: OFFSET_TYPE_LEG_LENGTHOFFSET_TYPE_COVER_THICKNESS
- `EndPointOffsetValue` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the concrete cover thickness or leg length at the second end of the bar.
- `ExcludeType` (BaseRebarGroup.ExcludeTypeEnum, get/set) — Defines which bars to omit from the group. The options are: EXCLUDE_TYPE_NONEEXCLUDE_TYPE_FIRSTEXCLUDE_TYPE_LASTEXCLUDE_TYPE_BOTH
- `Father` (ModelObject, get/set) — Gets or sets the father object of the reinforcement; the model object instance to operate on.
- `FromPlaneOffset` (Double, get/set) — The offset value from the part surface applied in both sides.
- `Grade` (String, get/set) — Gets or sets the steel grade of the reinforcing bar. The grade indicates the strength of the steel used in reinforcing bars. It can also indicate other factors, such as the weldability or surface deformations of the bar.
- `Identifier` (Identifier, get/set) — The identifier of the object.
- `InputPointDeformingState` (DeformingType, get/set) — Gets or sets the reinforcement input point deforming state.
- `IsUpToDate` (object, get) — Gets if the object does not have a modification which is not shared.
- `ModificationTime` (object, get) — Gets latest time of the object was modified or created.
- `Name` (String, get/set) — Gets or sets the name of the reinforcement.
- `NumberingSeries` (NumberingSeries, get/set) — Gets or sets the numbering series of the reinforcement.
- `OnPlaneOffsets` (ArrayList, get/set) — Gets or sets the double offset value for each leg on the same plane as the bar.
- `Polygon` (Polygon, get/set) — A polygon definition for the curved reinforcing bar group shape. The polygon must have 3 points.
- `RadiusValues` (ArrayList, get/set) — Gets or sets the radius value(s) of the bends in the bar.
- `Size` (String, get/set) — The size of the reinforcement.
- `Spacings` (ArrayList, get/set) — The spacing value(s). If the type of the spacing is SPACING_TYPE_EXACT_NUMBER Spacings has only one value that defines the number of the reinforcing bars.
- `SpacingType` (BaseRebarGroup.RebarGroupSpacingTypeEnum, get/set) — The type of spacing. The options are (BaseRebarGroup.RebarGroupSpacingTypeEnum.): SPACING_TYPE_UNDEFINEDSPACING_TYPE_EXACT_SPACINGSSPACING_TYPE_EXACT_NUMBERSPACING_TYPE_TARGET_SPACESPACING_TYPE_EXACT_SPACE_FLEX_AT_STARTSPACING_TYPE_EXACT_SPACE_FLEX_AT_ENDSPACING_TYPE_EXACT_SPACE_FLEX_AT_BOTHSPACING_TYPE_EXACT_SPACE_FLEX_AT_MIDDLE
- `StartFromPlaneOffset` (Double, get/set) — The start offset value from the part surface.
- `StartHook` (RebarHookData, get/set) — The hook at the beginning of the reinforcing bar.
- `StartPoint` (Point, get/set) — The start point of the direction in which the bars are distributed.
- `StartPointOffsetType` (Reinforcement.RebarOffsetTypeEnum, get/set) — Gets or sets the type of the start point offset is either OFFSET_TYPE_LEG_LENGTH or OFFSET_TYPE_COVER_THICKNESS.
- `StartPointOffsetValue` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the concrete cover thickness or leg length at the first end of the bar.

## CustomPart (class)

The CustomPart class represents a custom part object in the Tekla Structures model. A custom part is a modeling tool that typically assembles multiple parts to build some type of a beam-like structure, for example a tapered beam or a sandwich panel.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/d49ee520-e614-1f88-32b0-3bd442cfc84c)

### Constructors
- `public CustomPart()` — Creates a new custom part instance.
- `public CustomPart(Point StartPoint, Point EndPoint)` — Creates a new custom part instance with the given input.

### Methods
#### `public int CompareTo(Object obj)`

Compares Identifiers of model objects.

**Parameters:**
- `obj` (System.Object) — The model object to be used in comparison.

**Returns:** `Int32` — 0 if object Identifiers are the same, 1 if given object Identifier is bigger, -1 otherwise.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/79cca356-0c8a-e8fb-463a-34ad4141bec7)

Deletes the custom part instance with the given identifier from the model database.

#### `public bool LoadAttributesFromFile(string Filename)`

Loads the attributes for the component from the given file. These attributes will be loaded before all the attributes that have been set with the SetAttribute methods, so any attributes that are set with SetAttribute will override those loaded from the given standard file.

**Parameters:**

### Properties
- `Identifier` (Identifier, get/set) — The identifier of the object.
- `IsUpToDate` (object, get) — Gets if the object does not have a modification which is not shared.
- `ModificationTime` (object, get) — Gets latest time of the object was modified or created.
- `Name` (String, get/set) — The name of the component. The name identifies custom components or plug-ins.
- `Number` (Int32, get/set) — The number of the component. A number greater than zero identifies system components, for custom components the number is CUSTOM_OBJECT_NUMBER, and for plug-ins the number is PLUGIN_OBJECT_NUMBER.
- `Position` (Position, get/set) — The way the custom part is positioned in the model.

## CutPlane (class)

The CutPlane class defines how the end of a part is cut with a plane. A cut plane differs from a fitting because a cut can never extend the boundaries of the original part.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/9ffbb991-b111-ad28-f0c0-5b9484c1002a)

### Constructors
- `public CutPlane()` — Initializes a new instance of the CutPlane class with empty attributes.

Compares Identifiers of model objects.

**Parameters:**
- `obj` (System.Object) — The model object to be used in comparison.

**Returns:** `Int32` — 0 if object Identifiers are the same, 1 if given object Identifier is bigger, -1 otherwise.
