### Properties
- `Father` (ModelObject, get/set) — The father object of the boolean operation; the model object instance to operate on.
- `Identifier` (Identifier, get/set) — The identifier of the object.
- `IsUpToDate` (object, get) — Gets if the object does not have a modification which is not shared.
- `ModificationTime` (object, get) — Gets latest time of the object was modified or created.
- `Plane` (Plane, get/set) — The plane that cuts the part.

## CylindricalSurface (class)

The CylindricalSurface class defines a cylindrical surface contour.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/79e12062-9cfd-f132-b719-45e61c809615)

### Constructors
- `public CylindricalSurface(Arc baseArc, double height)` — Initializes a new instance of the CylindricalSurface class from a base arc and a height. This is the recommended constructor if no intermediate points are desired along the lateral boundaries of the bend surface.
- `public CylindricalSurface(List<Point> lateralBoundary1, List<Point> lateralBoundary2, Line centerLine, Vector rotationAxis)` — Initializes a new instance of the CylindricalSurface class from the cone lateral boundaries and the center line. This is the recommended constructor if fine grained control is desired over the lateral boundaries of the surface.
- `public CylindricalSurface(Vector endFaceNormal1, Vector endFaceNormal2, List<Point> lateralBoundary1, List<Point> lateralBoundary2)` — Initializes a new instance of the CylindricalSurface class with given parameters. The boundaries are defined by the lateral boundaries (i.e. the points on the sides of the curved part of the cylinder). The side boundaries are defined by the two first points and the two last points of the lateral boundaries.
- `public CylindricalSurface(Vector endFaceNormal1, Vector endFaceNormal2, LineSegment sideBoundary1, LineSegment sideBoundary2)` — Initializes a new instance of the CylindricalSurface class with given parameters. The boundaries are defined by the side boundaries (i.e. generator lines of the cylinder).

### Properties
- `CenterLine` (Line, get) — Gets the center line of the bend surface (i.e. the line that crosses every circular cross section of the bend at their center point)
- `EndFaceNormal1` (Vector, get) — Gets the first end face normal.
- `EndFaceNormal2` (Vector, get) — Gets the second end face normal.
- `IntersectionLine` (Line, get) — Gets intersection line.
- `InwardCurved` (Boolean, get) — Gets the direction of the curve - true if the curve is oriented towards the intersection line, false otherwise.
- `LateralBoundary1` (List<Point>, get) — Gets or sets the first lateral boundary
- `LateralBoundary2` (List<Point>, get) — Gets or sets the second lateral boundary
- `Radius` (Double, get) — Gets radius of the cylindrical surface.
- `RotationAxis` (Vector, get) — Gets the unit vector that defines the rotation axis (for counter clockwise rotations) of the lateral boundaries of the surface. This axis is always parallel to the direction of the center line.
- `SideBoundary1` (LineSegment, get/set) — Gets or sets the first side boundary.
- `SideBoundary2` (LineSegment, get/set) — Gets or sets the second side boundary.

## CylindricalSurfaceNode (class)

The CylindricalSurfaceNode class represents cylindrical surface geometry tree node.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/647c9194-6cfd-7361-719f-9107ecae25a6)

Creates a new object that is a copy of the current instance.

- `Surface` (CylindricalSurface, get) — Gets the cylindrical surface geometry.

## DeformingData (class)

The DeformingData class holds information about the deforming of parts. Deformed parts are parts that have been warped, cambered or shortened.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/d344d31d-6c1a-e537-ddf0-57d0a2c0a48f)

### Constructors
- `public DeformingData()` — Instantiates a deforming data instance.

### Properties
- `Angle` (Double, get/set) — The angle of the part at its start point, relative to the part reference line. For example, if the beam is warped 45 degrees at the end point, in the Angle field the value is 0, and the value is 45 in the Angle2 field.
- `Angle2` (Double, get/set) — The angle of the part at its end point, relative to the part reference line. For example, if the beam is warped 45 degrees at the end point, in the Angle field the value is 0, and the value is 45 in the Angle2 field.
- `Cambering` (Double, get/set) — The degree of cambering.
- `Shortening` (Double, get/set) — The degree of shortening.

## Detail (class)

The Detail class represents a detail. A detail is different from a connection since the detail only connects to one part.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/5e5bcfd3-7bef-21c3-0c09-059814ebdebb)

### Constructors
- `public Detail()` — Creates a new detail instance.

### Methods

Compares Identifiers of model objects.

**Parameters:**
- `obj` (System.Object) — The model object to be used in comparison.

**Returns:** `Int32` — 0 if object Identifiers are the same, 1 if given object Identifier is bigger, -1 otherwise.

- `intPropertyNames` (System.Collections.Generic.List<String>) — The names of the int properties to be set.
- `intValues` (System.Collections.Generic.List<Int32>) — The new values for the int properties.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if the property was set successfully.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/8659da37-d571-f03f-c666-5c9fa3b113f5)

Sets a double property for the model object.

- `name` (System.String) — The name of the property to be set.
- `value` (System.Double) — The new value for the property.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if the property was set successfully.

Sets a string property for the model object.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/80bf0348-b651-3d4a-862b-49c85bc924ab)

### Properties
- `AutoDirectionType` (AutoDirectionTypeEnum, get/set) — The auto direction type. Default value is AUTODIR_DETAIL. Value should be AUTODIR_NA to set direction with UpVector property. Value should be AUTODIR_FROM_ATTRIBUTE_FILE to set direction with detail attribute file.
- `Class` (Int32, get/set) — The class of the detail.
- `Code` (String, get/set) — The code of the detail. The code can be used to classify the detail. The code can be reported and shown in drawings. The maximum length of the string is 20 characters.
- `DetailType` (DetailTypeEnum, get/set) — The detail type.
- `Identifier` (Identifier, get/set) — The identifier of the object.
- `IsUpToDate` (object, get) — Gets if the object does not have a modification which is not shared.
- `ModificationTime` (object, get) — Gets latest time of the object was modified or created.
- `Name` (String, get/set) — The name of the component. The name identifies custom components or plug-ins.
- `Number` (Int32, get/set) — The number of the component. A number greater than zero identifies system components, for custom components the number is CUSTOM_OBJECT_NUMBER, and for plug-ins the number is PLUGIN_OBJECT_NUMBER.
- `PositionType` (PositionTypeEnum, get/set) — The position type.
- `Status` (ConnectionStatusEnum, get) — The read only status of the detail. The status can be reported and shown in drawings. The color of the detail symbol in the model indicates the status of the detail.
- `UpVector` (Vector, get/set) — A vector indicating which direction is considered the up direction.

The EdgeChamfer class defines how the part edge is chamfered.

### Properties
- `Chamfer` (Chamfer, get/set) — The chamfer values for the EdgeChamfer.
- `Father` (ModelObject, get/set) — The father object of the boolean operation; the model object instance to operate on.
- `FirstBevelDimension` (Double, get/set) — The bevel dimension if the first end is bevelled.
- `FirstChamferEndType` (EdgeChamfer.ChamferEndTypeEnum, get/set) — The ChamferEndTypeEnum of the first end.
- `FirstEnd` (Point, get/set) — The start point of the EdgeChamfer.
- `Identifier` (Identifier, get/set) — The identifier of the object.
- `IsUpToDate` (object, get) — Gets if the object does not have a modification which is not shared.
- `ModificationTime` (object, get) — Gets latest time of the object was modified or created.
- `Name` (String, get/set) — The name of the EdgeChamfer instance.
- `SecondBevelDimension` (Double, get/set) — The bevel dimension if the second end is bevelled.
- `SecondChamferEndType` (EdgeChamfer.ChamferEndTypeEnum, get/set) — The ChamferEndTypeEnum of the second end.
- `SecondEnd` (Point, get/set) — The end point of the EdgeChamfer.

## EdgeChamfer.ChamferEndTypeEnum (enum)

The end type of the edge chamfer.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/bb703a65-2763-a1f7-5315-8973f2a01dd0)

### Values
- `FULL` = `0` — The chamfer continues to the end of the edge.
- `STRAIGHT` = `1` — The chamfer end is straight at the given point.
- `BEVELLED` = `2` — The chamfer end is bevelled at the given point.

## Events (class)

The Events class allows the user to register event listeners for model events.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/8718989a-ebeb-cbd0-b920-08fb7a8ad3e4)

### Constructors
- `public Events()` — Creates an empty events instance.

### Methods
#### `public void Dispose()`

Disposes the instance.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/98294faa-f7c3-4f2b-fca7-1b1cc6d5e7c3)

#### `public override Object InitializeLifetimeService()`

Initializes the lifetime service.

**Returns:** `Object` — Always null.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/46f8cdad-822e-e7ea-d76a-f495a0c83fb3)

Called when uesr interrupts.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/5228be94-0f54-214b-47eb-60b574f92c9c)

#### `public void OnModelSaveInfo(string eventName, params Object[] parameters)`

Called when model is saved.

**Parameters:**
- `eventName` (System.String) — Name of the save event.
- `parameters` (.System.Object.) — Event parameters. Contains the code which specifies the reason of the save action.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/bed20299-72dc-0d5f-6325-1ba70626082f)

#### `public void OnUndoClicked()`

Called when user clicks undo.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/4f8999bd-b4af-b02b-fd08-250ae8b0a4a7)

#### `public void Register()`

Registers the instance to listen to the specified events. More event delegates should not be added without calling UnRegister first.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/e324b229-c0fc-ee66-a7f9-5277a17c0858)

#### `public void UnRegister()`

Unregisters the instance from listening to events.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/89ad49e2-c123-7b99-5484-b4efe9a1dbde)

### Events
#### `AnnotationSelectionChange` (`Tekla.Structures.Model.Events.SelectionChangeDelegate`)

**Signature:** `public event Events.SelectionChangeDelegate AnnotationSelectionChange`

The SelectionChange event is raised when the user changes the current selection of an annotation inside Tekla Structures.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/f799ee63-e2d3-224e-b824-b2c96c76d405)

#### `ClashCheckDone` (`Tekla.Structures.Model.Events.ClashCheckDoneDelegate`)

**Signature:** `public event Events.ClashCheckDoneDelegate ClashCheckDone`

The ClashCheckDone event is raised just after clash check is completed.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/97cb0c22-0ee7-37d5-7908-c1fc2f036ede)

#### `ClashDetected` (`Tekla.Structures.Model.Events.ClashDetectedDelegate`)

**Signature:** `public event Events.ClashDetectedDelegate ClashDetected`

The ClashDetected event is raised just after the detection of each clash. The clash information is saved before the event is triggered.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/ffc4e371-805a-71c7-2486-4eccb4b2365d)

#### `ClipBoxChanged` (`Tekla.Structures.Model.Events.ClipBoxChangedDelegate`)

**Signature:** `public event Events.ClipBoxChangedDelegate ClipBoxChanged`

Occurs when user deletes a clip box.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/6ceef6b0-799d-3d79-9083-ec99feb19756)

Occurs when user deletes a clip plane.

#### `CommandStatusChange` (`Tekla.Structures.Model.Events.CommandStatusChangeDelegate`)

**Signature:** `public event Events.CommandStatusChangeDelegate CommandStatusChange`

The CommandStatusChange event is raised when a command is started/ended.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/0cd7e991-c25d-a837-a622-e21c45c7708d)

#### `HiddenObjectsChanged` (`Tekla.Structures.Model.Events.HiddenObjectsChangedDelegate`)

**Signature:** `public event Events.HiddenObjectsChangedDelegate HiddenObjectsChanged`

Occurs when user hides an object or view is redrawn

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/c3b53935-2927-bd06-0052-dde7c01c71ea)

#### `Interrupted` (`Tekla.Structures.Model.Events.InterruptedDelegate`)

**Signature:** `public event Events.InterruptedDelegate Interrupted`

Occurs when user interrupts.

#### `ModelUnloadingSync` (`Tekla.Structures.Model.Events.ModelLoadDelegate`)

**Signature:** `public event Events.ModelLoadDelegate ModelUnloadingSync`

The ModelUnloadingSync event is raised synchronously on the main thread just before the model is unloaded. Use this event only when core resources need be cleaned up before the model unloading proceeds. All UI-only related resource cleanup should be done in the async ModelUnloading event. OnModelUnloadingSync(String, .Object.)

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/03f82a07-1d6c-a7df-323c-311d71808b8a)

## ExtensionIntersectsWithPlateException (class)

The ExtensionIntersectsWithPlateException class represents an error when extension plate clashes with plates.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/1e7f5097-9730-c720-48e0-e816e25bef98)

### Constructors
- `public ExtensionIntersectsWithPlateException()` — Initializes a new instance of the ExtensionIntersectsWithPlateException class with "extension plate clashes with plates" error message.

## FacePerpendicularToIntersectionLineException (class)

The FacePerpendicularToIntersectionLineException class represents an error when, face perpendicular to intersection line.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/935fde75-f2c5-1d01-623f-d7a6b4c48c34)

### Constructors
- `public FacePerpendicularToIntersectionLineException()` — Initializes a new instance of the FacePerpendicularToIntersectionLineException class with "face perpendicular to intersection line" error message.

## FacesAtAnObtuseAngleException (class)

The FacesAtAnObtuseAngleException class represents an error when, faces to be connected are too steep of an angle.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/5a2bc09b-f29d-62ef-c502-50bfc1f8aa86)

### Constructors
- `public FacesAtAnObtuseAngleException()` — Initializes a new instance of the FacesAtAnObtuseAngleException class with "Faces to be connected are too steep of an angle" error message.

## FacesTooNearEachOtherException (class)

The FacesTooNearEachOtherException class represents an error when, faces to be connected overlap.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/3e31a78f-75c9-796d-7463-c0d60e28e905)

### Constructors
- `public FacesTooNearEachOtherException()` — Initializes a new instance of the ExtensionIntersectsWithPlateException class with "faces to be connected overlap" error message.

## Fitting (class)

The Fitting class defines how the part end fits to a plane. A fitting can make the part either longer or shorter.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/9bd2b3ed-c087-cb0c-f1ed-2c11a3e4c533)

### Constructors
- `public Fitting()` — Initializes a new instance of the Fitting class with empty attributes.

### Methods
#### `public int CompareTo(Object obj)`

Compares Identifiers of model objects.

**Parameters:**
- `obj` (System.Object) — The model object to be used in comparison.

**Returns:** `Int32` — 0 if object Identifiers are the same, 1 if given object Identifier is bigger, -1 otherwise.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/79cca356-0c8a-e8fb-463a-34ad4141bec7)

#### `public override bool Delete()`

Deletes the fitting instance with the given ID from the model database.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True on success.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/bb537d5c-2a27-22da-ed13-b5287ec63022)

#### `public bool Equals(ModelObject other)`

Check if Identifiers of model objects are same.

**Parameters:**
- `other` (Tekla.Structures.Model.ModelObject) — The model object to be used in comparison.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if object Identifiers are the same, false otherwise,

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/e4324265-2490-00de-4139-63a8f7acb492)

#### `public bool GetAllReportProperties(ArrayList stringNames, ArrayList doubleNames, ArrayList integerNames, ref Hashtable values)`

Retrieves all properties for the model object in one hashtable. Type for the returned value must be checked using type casting.

**Parameters:**
- `stringNames` (System.Collections.ArrayList) — An array list containing the names of the string properties.
- `doubleNames` (System.Collections.ArrayList) — An array list containing the names of the double properties.
- `integerNames` (System.Collections.ArrayList) — An array list containing the names of the integer properties.
- `values` (System.Collections.Hashtable.) — The return value, a hash table list that contains all the values that were retrieved successfully.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if at least one single property was retrieved successfully.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/0708f404-ce77-28e1-4ebe-fa906f68bff8)

#### `public bool GetAllUserProperties(ref Hashtable values)`

Retrieves all properties for the model object in one hashtable. Type for the returned value must be checked using type casting.

**Parameters:**
- `values` (System.Collections.Hashtable.) — The return value, a hash table that contains all the values that were retrieved successfully.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if at least one single property was retrieved successfully.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/63b853a0-0af5-af9c-3ce7-05299664d7f8)

#### `public virtual ModelObjectEnumerator GetChildren()`

Returns an enumerator of all the children model objects.

**Returns:** `ModelObjectEnumerator` — A model object enumerator on success, otherwise null.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/ce51ec40-310c-6067-a89f-f8d029aab747)

#### `public CoordinateSystem GetCoordinateSystem()`

Returns the coordinate system for the given model object.

**Returns:** `CoordinateSystem` — The coordinate system for the given model object.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/8adfe9cd-f6e5-6474-1297-33c6270a7b0a)

#### `public string GetCustomObjectType()`

Gets custom object type name from an existing object.

**Returns:** `String` — Type name string, empty string if type is not set.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/d3be2ba3-8533-7165-f2d3-a5aa9df556b8)

#### `public bool GetDoubleReportProperties(ArrayList names, ref Hashtable values)`

Retrieves multiple double report properties for the model object. It is faster to fetch multiple properties at once.

**Parameters:**
- `names` (System.Collections.ArrayList) — An array list containing the names of the properties.
- `values` (System.Collections.Hashtable.) — The return value, a hash table that contains all the values that were retrieved successfully.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if at least one single property was retrieved successfully.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/f86f6c96-f738-49ca-16ba-f5634e862ba2)

#### `public bool GetDoubleUserProperties(ref Hashtable values)`

Retrieves all double properties for the model object.

**Parameters:**
- `values` (System.Collections.Hashtable.) — The return value, a hash table that contains all the values that were retrieved successfully.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if at least one single property was retrieved successfully.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/6ead7a63-5f76-0631-d480-d46c27e882aa)

#### `public bool GetDynamicStringProperty(string name, ref string value)`

Gets a dynamic string property from the model object.

**Parameters:**
- `name` (System.String) — The name of the property to be set.
- `value` (System.String.) — The new value for the property.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if the property was set successfully.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/415e86d9-7de6-ea53-ce68-20b3e67b8655)

#### `public BaseComponent GetFatherComponent()`

Returns the father component of the model object.

**Returns:** `BaseComponent` — A base component on success, otherwise null.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/a2c28f3f-52ff-50a8-1b32-10d18db5c31d)

#### `public ModelObjectEnumerator GetHierarchicObjects()`

Returns an enumerator of all the connected hierarchic objects.

**Returns:** `ModelObjectEnumerator` — A model object enumerator on success, otherwise null.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/cc228ea0-267b-7d90-7441-c9efc1779b08)

#### `public bool GetIntegerReportProperties(ArrayList names, ref Hashtable values)`

Retrieves multiple integer report properties for the model object. It is faster to fetch multiple properties at once.

**Parameters:**
- `names` (System.Collections.ArrayList) — An array list containing the names of the properties.
- `values` (System.Collections.Hashtable.) — The return value, a hash table that contains all the values that were retrieved successfully.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if at least one single property was retrieved successfully.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/2623a9c0-2a9c-e233-9daa-59f92ed51445)

#### `public bool GetIntegerUserProperties(ref Hashtable values)`

Retrieves all integer properties for the model object.

**Parameters:**
- `values` (System.Collections.Hashtable.) — The return value, a hash table that contains all the values that were retrieved successfully.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if at least one single property was retrieved successfully.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/1b3c6f06-bc25-a54e-2a88-b60dc4a3c85e)

#### `public bool GetPhase(out Phase phase)`

Retrieves the phase of the model object (the phase number, the phase name, the phase comment and whether the phase is the current one or not).

**Parameters:**
- `phase` (Tekla.Structures.Model.Phase.) — The returned phase.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True on success.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/310f8b7a-c120-753a-35c9-f7c73a9806c9)

#### `public bool GetReportProperty(string name, ref double value)`

Retrieves a double property of the report for the model object.

**Parameters:**
- `name` (System.String) — The name of the property to be retrieved.
- `value` (System.Double.) — The return value.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if the value was returned successfully.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/1c05aa56-5f42-be91-a59e-8d59a5549f45)

- `value` (System.Int32.) — The return value.

#### `public bool GetReportProperty(string name, ref string value)`

Retrieves a string property of the report for the model object.

**Parameters:**
- `name` (System.String) — The name of the property to be retrieved.
- `value` (System.String.) — The return value.

### Properties
- `Father` (ModelObject, get/set) — The father object of the boolean operation; the model object instance to operate on.
- `Identifier` (Identifier, get/set) — The identifier of the object.
- `IsUpToDate` (object, get) — Gets if the object does not have a modification which is not shared.
- `ModificationTime` (object, get) — Gets latest time of the object was modified or created.
- `Plane` (Plane, get/set) — The plane that fits the part.

## GeneralConnectiveGeometryException (class)

The GeneralConnectiveGeometryException class represents a general error that occurred during the ConnectiveGeometry creation or modification.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/9a296802-ce5e-ef31-9f8a-37c708592065)

### Constructors
- `public GeneralConnectiveGeometryException()` — Initializes a new instance of the GeneralConnectiveGeometryException class with "Failed to create or modify connective geometry" message.

## GeometrySection (class)

The GeometrySection class represents geometry node with its identifier.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/ebc2368c-cb3d-5533-8e3e-af3d02c7466d)

### Properties
- `GeometryNode` (IGeometryNode, get) — Gets the geometry node
- `Index` (Int32, get) — Gets the index of the node

## GeometrySectionEnumerator (class)

The GeometrySectionEnumerator class is used to enumerate the geometry sections of a bent plate geometry.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/fabaac2a-bb59-e3d1-b60d-38f638e7bf6e)

### Methods
#### `public bool MoveNext()`

Moves to the next item in the enumerator.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — False on failure.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/e3a5139a-f5d1-c283-d58a-21d2403b9624)

#### `public void Reset()`

Resets the enumerator to the beginning.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/2735ca0c-35d3-991b-c53b-d3ea666301b8)

### Properties
- `Current` (GeometrySection, get) — Gets the current geometry section.

## Grid (class)

The Grid class defines a user defined (possibly magnetic) set of planes helping in modeling work. A grid has grid plane instances as children.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/82142833-2bb4-61e8-88d3-e8b43a38ade2)

### Constructors
- `public Grid()` — Creates a new grid instance. The default values are as follows: Name = "Grid";CoordinateX = "0.00 4*3000.00";CoordinateY = "0.00 5*6000.00";CoordinateZ = "0.00 6000.00 8000.00 9000.00";LabelX = "A B C D E";LabelY = "1 2 3 4 5 6";LabelZ = "+0 +6000 +8000 +9000";ExtensionLeftX = 2000.0;ExtensionLeftY = 2000.0;ExtensionLeftZ = 2000.0;ExtensionRightX = 2000.0;ExtensionRightY = 2000.0;ExtensionRightZ = 2000.0;IsMagnetic = false;

### Methods
#### `public int CompareTo(Object obj)`

Compares Identifiers of model objects.

**Parameters:**
- `obj` (System.Object) — The model object to be used in comparison.

**Returns:** `Int32` — 0 if object Identifiers are the same, 1 if given object Identifier is bigger, -1 otherwise.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/79cca356-0c8a-e8fb-463a-34ad4141bec7)

### Properties
- `Color` (Int32, get/set) — Gets or sets the color of the grid.
- `CoordinateX` (String, get/set) — The coordinates for the X-axis.
- `CoordinateY` (String, get/set) — The coordinates for the Y-axis.
- `CoordinateZ` (String, get/set) — The coordinates for the Z-axis.
- `ExtensionForMagneticArea` (Double, get/set) — The extension for the magnetic area.
- `ExtensionLeftX` (Double, get/set) — The extension to the left X-axis.
- `ExtensionLeftY` (Double, get/set) — The extension to the left Y-axis.
- `ExtensionLeftZ` (Double, get/set) — The extension to the left Z-axis.
- `ExtensionRightX` (Double, get/set) — The extension to the right X-axis.
- `ExtensionRightY` (Double, get/set) — The extension to the right Y-axis.
- `ExtensionRightZ` (Double, get/set) — The extension to the right Z-axis.
- `FontColor` (Color, get/set) — Gets or sets font color for the grid labels
- `FontSize` (Int32, get/set) — Gets or sets font size for the grid labels
- `Identifier` (Identifier, get/set) — The identifier of the object.
- `IsMagnetic` (Boolean, get/set) — Gets or sets whether the grid is magnetic or not.
- `IsUpToDate` (object, get) — Gets if the object does not have a modification which is not shared.
- `LabelX` (String, get/set) — The labels for the X-axis.
- `LabelY` (String, get/set) — The labels for the Y-axis.
- `LabelZ` (String, get/set) — The labels for the Z-axis.
- `ModificationTime` (object, get) — Gets latest time of the object was modified or created.
- `Name` (String, get/set) — Gets or sets the name of the grid.
- `Origin` (Point, get/set) — Gets or sets the origin of the grid

## GridBase (class)

The GridBase class defines the base class for grids.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/6cd5d756-5f8e-15d5-37b7-728c940c30ed)

### Constructors
- `public GridBase()` — Creates a new grid instance. The default values are as follows: Name = "Grid";CoordinateX = "0.00 4*3000.00";CoordinateY = "0.00 5*6000.00";CoordinateZ = "0.00 6000.00 8000.00 9000.00";LabelX = "A B C D E";LabelY = "1 2 3 4 5 6";LabelZ = "+0 +6000 +8000 +9000";ExtensionLeftX = 2000.0;ExtensionLeftY = 2000.0;ExtensionLeftZ = 2000.0;ExtensionRightX = 2000.0;ExtensionRightY = 2000.0;ExtensionRightZ = 2000.0;IsMagnetic = false;

### Methods
#### `public int CompareTo(Object obj)`

Compares Identifiers of model objects.

**Parameters:**
- `obj` (System.Object) — The model object to be used in comparison.

**Returns:** `Int32` — 0 if object Identifiers are the same, 1 if given object Identifier is bigger, -1 otherwise.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/79cca356-0c8a-e8fb-463a-34ad4141bec7)

Deletes the grid with the given ID.

### Properties
- `FontColor` (Color, get/set) — Gets or sets font color for the grid labels
- `FontSize` (Int32, get/set) — Gets or sets font size for the grid labels
- `Identifier` (Identifier, get/set) — The identifier of the object.
- `IsMagnetic` (Boolean, get/set) — Gets or sets whether the grid is magnetic or not.
- `IsUpToDate` (object, get) — Gets if the object does not have a modification which is not shared.
- `ModificationTime` (object, get) — Gets latest time of the object was modified or created.
- `Name` (String, get/set) — Gets or sets the name of the grid.
- `Origin` (Point, get/set) — Gets or sets the origin of the grid

## GridCylindricalSurface (class)

The GridCylindricalSurface class defines a user defined (possibly magnetic) cylindrical surface helping in modeling work. A grid cylindrical surface must always belong to a parent grid instance.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/49862649-da7c-2c72-554e-78cc2a2d7f6b)

### Constructors
- `public GridCylindricalSurface()` — Creates a new grid plane instance. The default values are as follows: CylinderBase = new Arc(new Point(0, 0, 0), new Point(6000, 0, 0), new Vector(0, 0, 1), Math.PI);CylinderHeight = 6000.0;Label = "Default";IsMagnetic = false;ExtensionLeft = 2000.0;ExtensionRight = 2000.0;ExtensionBelow = 2000.0;ExtensionAbove = 2000.0;DrawingVisibility = false;
- `public GridCylindricalSurface(Arc cylinderBase, double cylinderHeight, string label)` — Creates a new grid plane instance taking the plane and label as input.

Compares Identifiers of model objects.

### Properties
- `CylinderBase` (Arc, get/set) — The arc that represents the base of the cylindrical surface. The cylinder is then extruded along the normal direction of the arc, with the height given by CylinderHeight"
- `CylinderHeight` (Double, get/set) — Height of the cylinder along the normal direction of the CylinderBase arc.
- `DrawingVisibility` (Boolean, get/set) — The visibility of the grid surface in drawings.
- `ExtensionAbove` (Double, get/set) — The line extension above.
- `ExtensionBelow` (Double, get/set) — The line extension below.
- `ExtensionLeft` (Double, get/set) — The line extension on the left.
- `ExtensionRight` (Double, get/set) — The line extension on the right.
- `Identifier` (Identifier, get/set) — The identifier of the object.
- `IsMagnetic` (Boolean, get/set) — Whether the grid surface is magnetic or not.
- `IsManual` (Boolean, get) — Gets or sets if the line was manually created.
- `IsUpToDate` (object, get) — Gets if the object does not have a modification which is not shared.
- `Label` (String, get/set) — The label for the grid surface.
- `ModificationTime` (object, get) — Gets latest time of the object was modified or created.
- `Parent` (GridBase, get/set) — The parent grid of the grid surface.

## GridPlane (class)

The GridPlane class defines a user defined (possibly magnetic) plane helping in modeling work. A grid plane must always belong to a father grid instance.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/d46eb5c5-4e0e-d205-47a5-61dd7d1bb1f9)

### Constructors
- `public GridPlane()` — Creates a new grid plane instance. The default values are as follows: Plane = new Plane();Plane.Origin = new Point(500.0, 500.0, 0.0);Plane.AxisX = new Vector(2000.0, 0.0, 0.0);Plane.AxisY = new Vector(0.0, 0.0, 2000.0);Label = "Default";IsMagnetic = false;ExtensionLeft = 2000.0;ExtensionRight = 2000.0;ExtensionBelow = 2000.0;ExtensionAbove = 2000.0;DrawingVisibility = false;
- `public GridPlane(Plane Plane, string Label)` — Creates a new grid plane instance taking the plane and label as input.

### Properties
- `Color` (Int32, get/set) — The color of the grid plane. This works only in wireframe views. The color of grid planes in rendered views can be changed using the advanced option XS_GRID_COLOR.
- `DrawingVisibility` (Boolean, get/set) — The visibility of the grid surface in drawings.
- `ExtensionAbove` (Double, get/set) — The line extension above.
- `ExtensionBelow` (Double, get/set) — The line extension below.
- `ExtensionForMagneticArea` (Double, get/set) — The extension for the magnetic area.
- `ExtensionLeft` (Double, get/set) — The line extension on the left.
- `ExtensionRight` (Double, get/set) — The line extension on the right.
- `Father` (Grid, get/set) — The father grid of the grid plane.
- `Identifier` (Identifier, get/set) — The identifier of the object.
- `IsMagnetic` (Boolean, get/set) — Whether the grid surface is magnetic or not.
- `IsManual` (Boolean, get) — Gets or sets if the line was manually created.
- `IsUpToDate` (object, get) — Gets if the object does not have a modification which is not shared.
- `Label` (String, get/set) — The label for the grid surface.
- `ModificationTime` (object, get) — Gets latest time of the object was modified or created.
- `Parent` (GridBase, get/set) — The parent grid of the grid surface.
- `Plane` (Plane, get/set) — The plane defining the position of the grid plane.

## GridSurface (class)

The GridSurface class defines a user defined (possibly magnetic) surface helping in modeling work. A grid surface must always belong to a parent GridBase instance.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/99d2617a-fb93-5d6e-c62d-bc84e0ee76c7)

### Constructors
- `public GridSurface()` — Creates a new grid surface instance. The default values are as follows: Label = "Default";IsMagnetic = false;ExtensionLeft = 2000.0;ExtensionRight = 2000.0;ExtensionBelow = 2000.0;ExtensionAbove = 2000.0;DrawingVisibility = false;
- `public GridSurface(string label)` — Creates a new grid surface instance taking the label as input.

### Methods
#### `public int CompareTo(Object obj)`

Compares Identifiers of model objects.

**Parameters:**
- `obj` (System.Object) — The model object to be used in comparison.

**Returns:** `Int32` — 0 if object Identifiers are the same, 1 if given object Identifier is bigger, -1 otherwise.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/79cca356-0c8a-e8fb-463a-34ad4141bec7)

Deletes the grid surface with the given ID.

### Properties
- `DrawingVisibility` (Boolean, get/set) — The visibility of the grid surface in drawings.
- `ExtensionAbove` (Double, get/set) — The line extension above.
- `ExtensionBelow` (Double, get/set) — The line extension below.
- `ExtensionLeft` (Double, get/set) — The line extension on the left.
- `ExtensionRight` (Double, get/set) — The line extension on the right.
- `Identifier` (Identifier, get/set) — The identifier of the object.
- `IsMagnetic` (Boolean, get/set) — Whether the grid surface is magnetic or not.
- `IsManual` (Boolean, get) — Gets or sets if the line was manually created.
- `IsUpToDate` (object, get) — Gets if the object does not have a modification which is not shared.
- `Label` (String, get/set) — The label for the grid surface.
- `ModificationTime` (object, get) — Gets latest time of the object was modified or created.
- `Parent` (GridBase, get/set) — The parent grid of the grid surface.

## HierarchicDefinition (class)

The HierarchicDefinition class defines a hierarchy structure. This hierarchy structure can be instantiated and extended by hierarchical objects.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/3b2e2665-3864-af76-83c7-ac2221f81a59)

### Constructors
- `public HierarchicDefinition()` — Creates a new hierarchical definition instance.
- `public HierarchicDefinition(Identifier ID)` — Instantiates a hierarchical definition with a known identifier. The instantiation has to be done before the hierarchical definition instance can be selected.

### Methods
#### `public bool AddObjects(ArrayList Objects)`

Associates hierarchical objects with the hierarchical definition. Fails if the hierarchical definition doesn't exist in the model.

**Parameters:**
- `Objects` (System.Collections.ArrayList) — The hierarchical objects.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if successful.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/2f575edf-4a3f-3f2a-cae4-b9ffa5382024)

Compares Identifiers of model objects.

- `obj` (System.Object) — The model object to be used in comparison.

**Returns:** `Int32` — 0 if object Identifiers are the same, 1 if given object Identifier is bigger, -1 otherwise.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/79cca356-0c8a-e8fb-463a-34ad4141bec7)

Deletes the hierarchical definition instance from the model. The ID must be set.

### Properties
- `CustomType` (String, get/set) — The custom type of the hierarchical definition.
- `Drawable` (Boolean, get/set) — Defines whether the hierarchical definition is a drawable area in the model.
- `Father` (HierarchicDefinition, get/set) — The father of the hierarchical definition.
- `HierarchicChildren` (ArrayList, get/set) — An array list that contains the hierarchical definition's children.
- `HierarchyIdentifier` (String, get/set) — The GUID of the hierarchical definition.
- `HierarchyType` (HierarchicDefinitionTypeEnum, get/set) — The type of the hierarchical definition.
- `Identifier` (Identifier, get/set) — The identifier of the object.
- `IsUpToDate` (object, get) — Gets if the object does not have a modification which is not shared.
- `ModificationTime` (object, get) — Gets latest time of the object was modified or created.
- `Name` (String, get/set) — The name of the hierarchical definition.

## HierarchicDefinitionTypeEnum (enum)

Defines the different types of classification hierarchies.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/07fc35c8-a364-8d68-e9cb-4798407bf2b7)

### Values
- `DOT_HIERARCHIC_CUSTOM_TYPE` = `0` — The custom type classification hierarchy.
- `DOT_HIERARCHIC_LOGICAL_BUILDING_AREA` = `1` — The logical building area classification hierarchy.
- `DOT_HIERARCHIC_OBJECT_TYPE` = `2` — The object type classification hierarchy.
- `DOT_HIERARCHIC_TASK_WORK_TYPE` = `3` — The task work type classification hierarchy.
- `DOT_HIERARCHIC_TASK_SCENARIO` = `4` — The task scenario classification hierarchy.

## HierarchicObject (class)

The HierarchicObject class defines an object in a tree-structured hierarchy. The hierarchy structure is defined by hierarchical definitions which the hierarchical objects instantiate. Hierarchical objects may contain other hierarchical objects, thus forming a sub-hierarchy, or model objects.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/c62fa47a-7ee7-9f7c-7151-313d6b9aad25)

### Constructors
- `public HierarchicObject()` — Creates a new hierarchical object instance.
- `public HierarchicObject(Identifier ID)` — Instantiates a hierarchical object with a known identifier. The instantiation has to be done before the hierarchical definition instance can be selected.

### Methods
#### `public bool AddObjects(ArrayList Objects)`

Associates model objects with the hierarchical object. Fails if the hierarchical definition doesn't exist in the model.

**Parameters:**
- `Objects` (System.Collections.ArrayList) — The model objects.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if successful.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/09b467a0-069b-a7d9-2723-fc47c289ad73)

Compares Identifiers of model objects.

**Parameters:**
- `obj` (System.Object) — The model object to be used in comparison.

**Returns:** `Int32` — 0 if object Identifiers are the same, 1 if given object Identifier is bigger, -1 otherwise.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/80bf0348-b651-3d4a-862b-49c85bc924ab)

### Properties
- `Definition` (HierarchicDefinition, get/set) — The hierarchical definition which the hierarchical object is associated with.
- `Father` (HierarchicObject, get/set) — The father of the hierarchical object.
- `HierarchicChildren` (ArrayList, get/set) — An array list that contains the hierarchical object's hierarchical children (of the type hierarchical object).
- `Identifier` (Identifier, get/set) — The identifier of the object.
- `IsUpToDate` (object, get) — Gets if the object does not have a modification which is not shared.
- `ModificationTime` (object, get) — Gets latest time of the object was modified or created.
- `Name` (String, get/set) — The name of the hierarchical object.

## IAssemblable (interface)

The IAssemblable interface dictates which model objects are capabable of belonging to an assembly at the leaf level.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/c937f46b-c437-8d96-0545-6067119dfea2)

### Methods
#### `Assembly GetAssembly()`

Returns the assembly that the assemblable belongs to.

**Returns:** `Assembly` — The assembly that the assemblable belongs to.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/5d79c5f5-a401-ab6b-3566-ae4ae54054a4)

## IEvents (interface)

Interface for the Events class allows the user to register event listeners for Model events. This interface allows the Marshaling of the Events class with Trimble.Remoting

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/54bfb640-73cf-907f-a432-96246eca23ca)

### Methods
#### `void OnClipBoxChanged(string eventName, params Object[] parameters)`

Called when user inserts, updates or deletes a clip box.

**Parameters:**
- `eventName` (System.String)
- `parameters` (.System.Object.)

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/d6829505-52fc-f304-4af8-5c246de2c86b)

#### `void OnClipPlaneChanged(string eventName, params Object[] parameters)`

Called when user inserts, updates or deletes a clip plane.

**Parameters:**
- `eventName` (System.String)
- `parameters` (.System.Object.)

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/c37bfe9c-c675-7019-fce7-92e7e00ac0d3)

#### `void OnHiddenObjectsChanged()`

Called when objects are set to hidden or view is redrawn

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/98996a2a-e743-b61f-672f-24d68eb39832)

#### `void OnInterrupted()`

#### `void OnModelSaveInfo(string eventName, params Object[] parameters)`

Called when model is saved.

**Parameters:**
- `eventName` (System.String) — Name of the save event.
- `parameters` (.System.Object.) — Event parameters. Contains the code which specifies the reason of the save action.

#### `void OnModelViewDrawn(int viewId)`

**Parameters:**
- `viewId` (System.Int32) — The view id.

#### `void OnTsEventOccurred(string eventInfo)`

Called when a TS event occurs.

**Parameters:**
- `eventInfo` (System.String) — The event info.

#### `void OnUndoClicked()`

Called when user clicks undo.

#### `void OnViewClosed(int viewId)`

- `viewId` (System.Int32) — The view id.

#### `void Register()`

Registers the instance to listen to the specified events. More event delegates should not be added without calling UnRegister first.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/d0730bc4-a833-068f-d632-6916bd501832)

#### `void UnRegister()`

Unregisters the instance from listening to events.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/f3c67e22-daa9-f10a-68d7-eeda79d6c10f)

### Events
#### `ClashCheckDone` (`Tekla.Structures.Model.Events.ClashCheckDoneDelegate`)

**Signature:** `event Events.ClashCheckDoneDelegate ClashCheckDone`

The ClashCheckDone event is raised just after clash check is completed.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/e0dd04f8-a536-e2c0-a8d0-e7527e7dd829)

#### `ClashDetected` (`Tekla.Structures.Model.Events.ClashDetectedDelegate`)

**Signature:** `event Events.ClashDetectedDelegate ClashDetected`

The ClashDetected event is raised just after the detection of each clash. The clash information is saved before the event is triggered.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/410049d9-af0c-4049-52e6-f558b577d34b)

#### `ClipPlaneChanged` (`Tekla.Structures.Model.Events.ClipPlaneChangedDelegate`)

**Signature:** `event Events.ClipPlaneChangedDelegate ClipPlaneChanged`

Occurs when clip plane is changed.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/ca0c7199-f018-3c28-5b9f-332caebb9e4b)

#### `CommandStatusChange` (`Tekla.Structures.Model.Events.CommandStatusChangeDelegate`)

**Signature:** `event Events.CommandStatusChangeDelegate CommandStatusChange`

The CommandStatusChange event is raised when a command is started/ended.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/e9291cd7-830b-a215-9520-917294174dc7)

#### `Interrupted` (`Tekla.Structures.Model.Events.InterruptedDelegate`)

**Signature:** `event Events.InterruptedDelegate Interrupted`

Occurs when user interrupts.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/3a0a0ed7-6297-8aba-3edb-6d1e09dfa429)

#### `ModelChanged` (`Tekla.Structures.Model.Events.ModelChangedDelegate`)

**Signature:** `event Events.ModelChangedDelegate ModelChanged`

The ModelChanged event is raised just after some changes have been made to the model. Note, this event is however not triggered when UNDO or REDO are performed.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/00930987-8efe-41ab-e124-6d9d5c00fe12)

#### `ModelLoad` (`Tekla.Structures.Model.Events.ModelLoadDelegate`)

**Signature:** `event Events.ModelLoadDelegate ModelLoad`

The ModelLoad event is raised just after a model has been loaded.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/040b1b98-1abb-f840-d794-a834e9cece9b)

#### `ModelLoadInfo` (`Tekla.Structures.Model.Events.ModelLoadInfoDelegate`)

**Signature:** `event Events.ModelLoadInfoDelegate ModelLoadInfo`

The ModelLoadInfo event is raised just after a model has been loaded with the model load information as a string parameter.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/eeef8696-14ac-ed0f-2f32-f517de721303)

#### `ModelObjectChanged` (`Tekla.Structures.Model.Events.ModelObjectChangedDelegate`)

**Signature:** `event Events.ModelObjectChangedDelegate ModelObjectChanged`

The ModelObjectChanged event is raised just after some changes have been made to the model objects. Note, this event is triggered when UNDO or REDO are performed!!

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/b3deb34d-bf92-ac3c-7ad7-44741c2d565a)

#### `ModelObjectNumbered` (`Tekla.Structures.Model.Events.ModelObjectNumberedDelegate`)

**Signature:** `event Events.ModelObjectNumberedDelegate ModelObjectNumbered`

The ModelObjectNumbered event is raised when model object is numbered.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/14649407-f014-ce82-2c3c-112c81bb0a34)

#### `ModelSave` (`Tekla.Structures.Model.Events.ModelSaveDelegate`)

**Signature:** `event Events.ModelSaveDelegate ModelSave`

The ModelSave event is raised just after a model has been saved.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/8238325b-a4e0-e7df-d735-2c2baf3f94b0)

#### `ModelSaveAs` (`Tekla.Structures.Model.Events.ModelSaveAsDelegate`)

**Signature:** `event Events.ModelSaveAsDelegate ModelSaveAs`

The ModelSaveAs event is raised just after a model has been saved using save as.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/5673f9ff-d163-61b1-6ac9-385d67ab8487)

#### `ModelSaveInfo` (`Tekla.Structures.Model.Events.ModelSaveInfoDelegate`)

**Signature:** `event Events.ModelSaveInfoDelegate ModelSaveInfo`

The ModelSave event is raised just after a model has been saved with the model save information as a string parameter.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/f3aa5f94-d3d4-a4da-b57b-b7d948c0e1a9)

#### `ModelUnloading` (`Tekla.Structures.Model.Events.ModelLoadDelegate`)

**Signature:** `event Events.ModelLoadDelegate ModelUnloading`

The ModelUnloading event is raised just before the model is unloaded.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/b560ae15-4784-0898-3193-d8da77b8e09e)

#### `Numbering` (`Tekla.Structures.Model.Events.NumberingDelegate`)

**Signature:** `event Events.NumberingDelegate Numbering`

The Numbering event is raised just after a model has been numbered.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/eef0a75c-c885-2b43-b979-6eaf5df12a5d)

#### `TeklaStructuresExit` (`Tekla.Structures.Model.Events.TeklaStructuresExitDelegate`)

The TeklaStructuresExit event is raised just before Tekla Structures exits. After this event has been called the user will no longer be able to perform any calls to Tekla Structures.

## IGeometryNode (interface)

The IGeometryNode interface represents geometry tree node. This is implemented by concrete classes for the various types of geometry nodes

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/d79a60dd-f84b-ce24-8bfa-a7a2f1cb3912)

### Methods
#### `void AcceptVisitor(IGeometryNodeVisitor visitor)`

Entry method for the visitor pattern

**Parameters:**
- `visitor` (Tekla.Structures.Model.IGeometryNodeVisitor)

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/e4a23ca4-afba-4ac6-574b-9133549c4515)

#### `IGeometryNode Clone()`

Creates a new object that is a copy of the current instance.

**Returns:** `IGeometryNode` — A new object that is a copy of the current instance.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/e01da20e-a19e-e9c9-dfb4-c1aa981b41b4)

### Properties
- `IsAutomatic` (Boolean, get) — Gets a value indicating whether this geometry node was automatically generated (i.e. false if it was originally a user-defined part)

## IGeometryNodeVisitor (interface)

The IGeometryNodeVisitor interface is implemented by classes visiting geometry nodes

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/526199de-8d7e-02d0-0e3a-2a1a0793cf8d)

### Methods
#### `void Visit(CylindricalSurfaceNode node)`

Visits a node that is an instance of CylindricalSurfaceNode

**Parameters:**
- `node` (Tekla.Structures.Model.CylindricalSurfaceNode)

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/3958d1f8-7497-672d-80c9-0bbf60724b1c)

#### `void Visit(PolygonNode node)`

Visits a node that is an instance of PolygonNode node

**Parameters:**
- `node` (Tekla.Structures.Model.PolygonNode)

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/458e978f-9b0c-1bfd-0807-acbddaa02233)

#### `void Visit(SpiralNode node)`

Visits a node that is an instance of HelixNode

**Parameters:**
- `node` (SpiralNode)

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/7043fa01-8442-e7a4-7c63-b3486b1ff65f)

## ISharedModel (interface)

Represents a shared model in the Tekla Structures environment. Contains metadata such as Id, Name, Code, Description, Owner, creation date, and the current user's sharing role.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/170a1de3-a763-9821-1f25-914f9181c4e7)

### Properties
- `Code` (String, get) — Gets the code associated with the shared model.
- `CreatedAt` (DateTime, get) — Gets the date and time when the shared model was created.
- `CurrentUserRole` (SharingRole, get) — Gets the sharing role of the current user for this model.
- `Description` (String, get) — Gets the description of the shared model.
- `Id` (Guid, get) — Gets the unique identifier of the shared model.
- `Name` (String, get) — Gets the name of the shared model.
- `Owner` (String, get) — Gets the owner of the shared model.

## ISharedModelState (interface)

Represents the shared model state in Tekla Structures.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/2dd7141f-3637-9061-e33c-1c1616b72fab)

### Properties
- `Comment` (String, get) — Gets comment provided at setting state.
- `IsLockOwner` (Boolean, get) — Gets a value indicating whether the current user is the lock owner.
- `LockOwner` (String, get) — Gets display name of the user who owns a lock (in case it is set).
- `PendingVersionsCount` (Nullable<Int32>, get) — Gets number of pending versions or null when it is not relevant (for example model is not shared) or failed to get it.
- `SetBy` (String, get) — Gets display name of the user who has set the state.
- `State` (SharedModelStateEnum, get) — Gets the state of the service.

## ISharingEvents (interface)

Provides events related to the sharing service in a shared model environment.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/3e0df457-57a3-2312-035d-472d7ee71df8)

### Events
#### `SharedModelStateChanged` (`System.EventHandler<ISharedModelState>`)

**Signature:** `event EventHandler<ISharedModelState> SharedModelStateChanged`

Occurs when the sharing service state changes.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/fbd2c8c3-d153-63c7-70b4-42f0066be93b)

## ISharingLocation (interface)

Represents a location used for sharing in the Tekla Structures model.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/5d39a130-6f72-1d9f-f954-0f745a748521)

### Properties
- `DisplayName` (String, get) — Gets the display name of the sharing location. It uses the current TS language when feasible.
- `Id` (Guid, get) — Gets the unique identifier of the sharing location.
- `Name` (String, get) — Gets the name of the sharing location. It does not change based on the TS language.

## ISharingLocationsInformation (interface)

Provides information about the available sharing locations.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/02e6b411-9156-858e-67fd-b7cd736b98ad)

### Properties
- `DefaultLocation` (Guid, get) — Gets the identifier of the default sharing location.
- `Locations` (IReadOnlyList<ISharingLocation>, get) — Gets the collection of available sharing locations.

## ISharingResult (interface)

Represents the result of a sharing operation, including the result code.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/5c029f52-7994-5a25-5ecb-1595e2bdb96c)

### Properties
- `Code` (SharingResultCode, get) — Gets the result code of the sharing operation.
- `ErrorDetails` (String, get) — Gets the error details of the sharing operation.
- `ErrorPath` (String, get) — Gets a path of the file or folder which is relevant to a specific error. SharingResultCode values documentation for a specific cases.
- `Notifications` (IEnumerable<String>, get) — Gets the notifications related to the sharing operation.
- `PathLengthLimit` (Int32, get) — Gets the maximum allowed length for the path which was exceeded.
- `State` (SharingResultState, get) — Gets the state Structures process and current model after the sharing operations completes.

## ISharingResult.T. (interface)

Represents the result of a sharing operation, including the result code and associated data.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/2eb6f0b1-5d74-cc68-e420-709332889cf9)

### Properties
- `Code` (SharingResultCode, get) — Gets the result code of the sharing operation.
- `Data` (T, get) — Gets the data associated with the sharing operation.
- `ErrorDetails` (String, get) — Gets the error details of the sharing operation.
- `ErrorPath` (String, get) — Gets a path of the file or folder which is relevant to a specific error. SharingResultCode values documentation for a specific cases.
- `Notifications` (IEnumerable<String>, get) — Gets the notifications related to the sharing operation.
- `PathLengthLimit` (Int32, get) — Gets the maximum allowed length for the path which was exceeded.
- `State` (SharingResultState, get) — Gets the state Structures process and current model after the sharing operations completes.

## ISharingUpdate (interface)

Represents an update of data used in sharing operations within the Tekla Structures Model.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/8841ee22-b950-8f57-8697-4d654828cee7)

### Properties
- `Code` (String, get) — Gets the code of the sharing update.
- `Comment` (String, get) — Gets the comment of the sharing update.
- `CreatedAt` (DateTime, get) — Gets the date and time when the sharing update was created.
- `CreatedBy` (String, get) — Gets the email of the user who created the sharing update.
- `Id` (Guid, get) — Gets the unique identifier of the sharing update.
- `Number` (Int32, get) — Gets the update's packet number.
- `Type` (SharingUpdateType, get) — Gets the type of the sharing update.

## InputItem (class)

The InputItem class is used to store input objects and positions.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/cc7681b2-4148-059a-f0f0-641b7c2d2769)

### Methods
#### `public Object GetData()`

Returns the data of the input item. See also: InputItem.InputTypeEnum.

**Returns:** `Object` — The input data.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/d588fef0-4022-bf7b-a92f-b3528419fe35)

#### `public InputItem.InputTypeEnum GetInputType()`

Returns the type of the input item.

**Returns:** `InputItem.InputTypeEnum` — The type of the input item.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/9929646f-ef1d-03c3-f4d1-3d123818108d)

## InputItem.InputTypeEnum (enum)

Defines the type of the input.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/a0433c89-2537-7cbc-0224-d7f60ed163d3)

### Values
- `INPUT_1_POINT` = `0` — The input is one point. The data is returned as a point.
- `INPUT_2_POINTS` = `1` — The input is two points. The data is returned as an array list of points.
- `INPUT_POLYGON` = `2` — The input is any number of points (a polygon). The data is returned as an array list of points.
- `INPUT_1_OBJECT` = `3` — The input is one model object. The data is returned as a model object.
- `INPUT_N_OBJECTS` = `4` — The input is any number of model objects. The data is returned as an array list of model objects.

## InvalidCurveCombinationException (class)

This class represent an exception thrown when the combination of base curves is not allowed in a lofted plate operation

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/aee41b14-a9b8-3cd3-84ff-3b08d60f94e6)

### Constructors
- `public InvalidCurveCombinationException()` — Constructs an instance of the class

## InvalidFacePointsException (class)

The InvalidFacePointsException class represents an error when, cannot create geometry from requested face points.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/aec29813-439a-ea77-34ae-d2c1c3db7ef7)

### Constructors
- `public InvalidFacePointsException()` — Initializes a new instance of the InvalidFacePointsException class with "Cannot create geometry from requested face points" error message.

## InvalidRadiusException (class)

The InvalidRadiusException class represents an error when, cannot connect parts with a cylindrical section having the provided radius.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/9f0fd914-78f1-fb5d-af93-9a736d9462d4)

### Constructors
- `public InvalidRadiusException()` — Initializes a new instance of the InvalidRadiusException class with "Cannot connect parts with a cylindrical section having the provided radius" error message.

## Load (class)

The Load class is an abstract base class for all loads.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/d4147471-17dc-0a4b-f579-3f9f50fa93aa)

### Constructors
- `public Load()` — Creates a new load instance.

### Methods
#### `public int CompareTo(Object obj)`

Compares Identifiers of model objects.

**Parameters:**
- `obj` (System.Object) — The model object to be used in comparison.

**Returns:** `Int32` — 0 if object Identifiers are the same, 1 if given object Identifier is bigger, -1 otherwise.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/79cca356-0c8a-e8fb-463a-34ad4141bec7)

#### `public abstract bool Delete()`

Deletes the instance from the model database.

### Properties
- `AutomaticPrimaryAxisWeight` (Boolean, get/set) — The automatic primary axis weight.
- `BoundingBoxDx` (Double, get/set) — The dimension of the bounding box in the X direction.
- `BoundingBoxDy` (Double, get/set) — The dimension of the bounding box in the Y direction.
- `BoundingBoxDz` (Double, get/set) — The dimension of the bounding box in the Z direction.
- `CreateFixedSupportConditionsAutomatically` (Boolean, get/set) — Indicates whether fixed support conditions are created automatically.
- `FatherId` (Identifier, get/set) — The identifier of the father object that the load is attached to.
- `Group` (LoadGroup, get/set) — The load group object for the load.
- `Identifier` (Identifier, get/set) — The identifier of the object.
- `IsUpToDate` (object, get) — Gets if the object does not have a modification which is not shared.
- `LoadAttachment` (Load.LoadAttachmentEnum, get/set) — The load attachment.
- `LoadDispersionAngle` (Double, get/set) — The load dispersion angle.
- `ModificationTime` (object, get) — Gets latest time of the object was modified or created.
- `PartFilter` (String, get/set) — The part filter.
- `PartNames` (Load.LoadPartNamesEnum, get/set) — The part names.
- `PrimaryAxisDirection` (Vector, get/set) — The vector for the primary axis direction.
- `Spanning` (Load.LoadSpanningEnum, get/set) — The load spanning.
- `Weight` (Double, get/set) — The weight.

## Load.LoadAttachmentEnum (enum)

The load attachment.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/a2ef5acf-8fd5-795e-e10a-3158afc3acfa)

### Values
- `LOAD_ATTACHMENT_ATTACH_TO_MEMBER` = `4001` — Attach to member.
- `LOAD_ATTACHMENT_DONT_ATTACH` = `4003` — Don't attach.

## Load.LoadPartNamesEnum (enum)

The load part names.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/1fac12d5-3ea1-bf0c-4173-ad9376ec2d9c)

### Values
- `LOAD_PART_NAMES_EXCLUDE` = `0` — Exclude parts by name.
- `LOAD_PART_NAMES_INCLUDE` = `1` — Include parts by name.

## Load.LoadSpanningEnum (enum)

The load spanning.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/2d4b6439-a7f7-2f14-8a1a-37e5ff6ca95a)

### Values
- `LOAD_SPANNING_SINGLE` = `1` — Single spanning.
- `LOAD_SPANNING_DOUBLE` = `2` — Double spanning.

## LoadArea (class)

The LoadArea class defines a linearly-distributed force bound by a triangle or a quadrangle.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/0c92809d-6aa4-549b-023e-7879db4d7d61)

### Constructors
- `public LoadArea()` — Creates a new load area instance.

Compares Identifiers of model objects.

**Parameters:**
- `obj` (System.Object) — The model object to be used in comparison.

**Returns:** `Int32` — 0 if object Identifiers are the same, 1 if given object Identifier is bigger, -1 otherwise.

### Properties
- `AutomaticPrimaryAxisWeight` (Boolean, get/set) — The automatic primary axis weight.
- `BoundingBoxDx` (Double, get/set) — The dimension of the bounding box in the X direction.
- `BoundingBoxDy` (Double, get/set) — The dimension of the bounding box in the Y direction.
- `BoundingBoxDz` (Double, get/set) — The dimension of the bounding box in the Z direction.
- `CreateFixedSupportConditionsAutomatically` (Boolean, get/set) — Indicates whether fixed support conditions are created automatically.
- `DistanceA` (Double, get/set) — The distance a (for enlarging or reducing the loaded area).
- `FatherId` (Identifier, get/set) — The identifier of the father object that the load is attached to.
- `Group` (LoadGroup, get/set) — The load group object for the load.
- `Identifier` (Identifier, get/set) — The identifier of the object.
- `IsUpToDate` (object, get) — Gets if the object does not have a modification which is not shared.
- `LoadAttachment` (Load.LoadAttachmentEnum, get/set) — The load attachment.
- `LoadDispersionAngle` (Double, get/set) — The load dispersion angle.
- `LoadForm` (LoadArea.AreaLoadFormEnum, get/set) — The load form.
- `ModificationTime` (object, get) — Gets latest time of the object was modified or created.
- `P1` (Vector, get/set) — The load magnitude vector P1. The values are given in the local coordinate system of the load (not in the current coordinate system).
- `P2` (Vector, get/set) — The load magnitude vector P2. The values are given in the local coordinate system of the load (not in the current coordinate system).
- `P3` (Vector, get/set) — The load magnitude vector P3. The values are given in the local coordinate system of the load (not in the current coordinate system).
- `P4` (Vector, get/set) — The load magnitude vector P4. This one cannot be set. The values are given in the local coordinate system of the load (not in the current coordinate system).
- `PartFilter` (String, get/set) — The part filter.
- `PartNames` (Load.LoadPartNamesEnum, get/set) — The part names.
- `Position1` (Point, get/set) — The first position point.
- `Position2` (Point, get/set) — The second position point.
- `Position3` (Point, get/set) — The third position point.
- `PrimaryAxisDirection` (Vector, get/set) — The vector for the primary axis direction.
- `Spanning` (Load.LoadSpanningEnum, get/set) — The load spanning.
- `Weight` (Double, get/set) — The weight.

## LoadArea.AreaLoadFormEnum (enum)

The area load form.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/38b0eec5-e459-c5fd-12d3-806d0256c95e)

### Values
- `LOAD_FORM_AREA_PARALLELOGRAM` = `1002` — The area load form is a parallelogram.
- `LOAD_FORM_AREA_TRIANGLE` = `1003` — The area load form is a triangle.

## LoadGroup (class)

The LoadGroup class implements grouping functionality related to all loads. Each load should be assigned to a load group. Each load group contains loads caused by the same action and to which you want to refer collectively. Tekla Structures assumes that all loads in a group Have the same partial safety and other combination factorsHave the same action directionOccur at the same time and all together You may create new load groups, modify existing ones and delete load groups via the .NET interface. You may also change the assigned load group of a load and list all the load groups found in the model via the ModelObjectSelector interface. The model always has at least one load group.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/c33bc83e-1690-718a-c3e9-0a588cbd1943)

### Constructors
- `public LoadGroup()` — Creates a new load group instance.

### Properties
- `Color` (LoadGroup.Colors, get/set) — The load group color. This variable indicates the color with which all loads that belong to this same group are drawn in Tekla Structures views.
- `Compatible` (Int32, get/set) — This variable is used to store information on which groups are compatible with each other in structural analysis load combination. Load combination is a process in which some simultaneously acting load groups are multiplied by their partial safety factors and combined with each other according to specific rules. To accurately combine loads which have the same load group type, you need to identify which load groups can occur at the same time, i.e. are compatible. This variable stores one integer, and all groups who have this same integer are known to be compatible with each other. Depending on the analysis needs, they may be calculated together, in smaller combinations, or separately.
- `Direction` (LoadGroup.LoadGroupDirection, get/set) — The load group direction.
- `GroupName` (String, get/set) — The load group name. The maximum size is 126 characters.
- `GroupType` (LoadGroup.LoadGroupType, get/set) — The load group type.
- `Identifier` (Identifier, get/set) — The identifier of the object.
- `Incompatible` (Int32, get/set) — This variable is used to store information on which groups exclude each other in structural analysis load combination. Load combination is a process in which some simultaneously acting load groups are multiplied by their partial safety factors and combined with each other according to specific rules. To accurately combine loads which have the same load group type, you need to identify which load groups exclude each other, i.e. are incompatible. This variable stores one integer, and all groups who have this same integer are known to be incompatible with each other.
- `IsUpToDate` (object, get) — Gets if the object does not have a modification which is not shared.
- `ModificationTime` (object, get) — Gets latest time of the object was modified or created.

## LoadGroup.Colors (enum)

The possible display colors for loads in a load group.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/6f2a89bd-8ba4-973c-a2f5-f7cef79ba412)

### Values
- `BLACK` = `0` — The black display color.
- `WHITE` = `1` — The white display color.
- `RED` = `2` — The red display color.
- `GREEN` = `3` — The green display color.
- `BLUE` = `4` — The blue display color.
- `CYAN` = `5` — The cyan display color.
- `YELLOW` = `6` — The yellow display color.
- `MAGENTA` = `7` — The magenta display color.

## LoadGroup.LoadGroupDirection (enum)

The possible directions.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/b0c729c6-cfde-f097-72c0-1ca150c956e9)

### Values
- `DIR_X` = `1` — The X direction.
- `DIR_Y` = `2` — The Y direction.
- `DIR_Z` = `3` — The Z direction.
- `DIR_NEG_X` = `4` — The negative X direction.
- `DIR_NEG_Y` = `5` — The negative Y direction.
- `DIR_NEG_Z` = `6` — The negative Z direction.

## LoadLine (class)

The LoadLine class defines a linearly-distributed force or torsion.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/6c2c4fcd-3944-0df3-0dfb-d042662860d1)

### Constructors
- `public LoadLine()` — Creates a new load line instance.

### Methods
#### `public int CompareTo(Object obj)`

Compares Identifiers of model objects.

**Parameters:**
- `obj` (System.Object) — The model object to be used in comparison.

**Returns:** `Int32` — 0 if object Identifiers are the same, 1 if given object Identifier is bigger, -1 otherwise.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/79cca356-0c8a-e8fb-463a-34ad4141bec7)

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/80bf0348-b651-3d4a-862b-49c85bc924ab)

### Properties
- `AutomaticPrimaryAxisWeight` (Boolean, get/set) — The automatic primary axis weight.
- `BoundingBoxDx` (Double, get/set) — The dimension of the bounding box in the X direction.
- `BoundingBoxDy` (Double, get/set) — The dimension of the bounding box in the Y direction.
- `BoundingBoxDz` (Double, get/set) — The dimension of the bounding box in the Z direction.
- `CreateFixedSupportConditionsAutomatically` (Boolean, get/set) — Indicates whether fixed support conditions are created automatically.
- `DistanceA` (Double, get/set) — The distance a (for shortening or dividing the length of a line load).
- `DistanceB` (Double, get/set) — The distance b (for shortening or dividing the length of a line load).
- `FatherId` (Identifier, get/set) — The identifier of the father object that the load is attached to.
- `Group` (LoadGroup, get/set) — The load group object for the load.
- `Identifier` (Identifier, get/set) — The identifier of the object.
- `IsUpToDate` (object, get) — Gets if the object does not have a modification which is not shared.
- `LoadAttachment` (Load.LoadAttachmentEnum, get/set) — The load attachment.
- `LoadDispersionAngle` (Double, get/set) — The load dispersion angle.
- `LoadForm` (LoadLine.LineLoadFormEnum, get/set) — The load form.
- `ModificationTime` (object, get) — Gets latest time of the object was modified or created.
- `P1` (Vector, get/set) — The load magnitude vector at the start position. The values are given in the local coordinate system of the load (not in the current coordinate system).
- `P2` (Vector, get/set) — The load magnitude vector at the end position. The values are given in the local coordinate system of the load (not in the current coordinate system).
- `PartFilter` (String, get/set) — The part filter.
- `PartNames` (Load.LoadPartNamesEnum, get/set) — The part names.
- `Position1` (Point, get/set) — The first input position point.
- `Position2` (Point, get/set) — The second input position point.
- `PrimaryAxisDirection` (Vector, get/set) — The vector for the primary axis direction.
- `Spanning` (Load.LoadSpanningEnum, get/set) — The load spanning.
- `Torsion1` (Double, get/set) — The first torsion.
- `Torsion2` (Double, get/set) — The second torsion.
- `Weight` (Double, get/set) — The weight.

## LoadLine.LineLoadFormEnum (enum)

The line load form.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/73f495f9-6e26-28a8-1ef6-82e1c7b2b808)

### Values
- `LOAD_FORM_LINE_1` = `1001` — The load magnitude is uniform across the loaded length.
- `LOAD_FORM_LINE_2` = `1002` — The load has different magnitudes at the ends of the loaded length. The magnitude changes linearly between the ends.
- `LOAD_FORM_LINE_3` = `1003` — The load magnitude changes linearly, from zero at the ends of the loaded length, to a fixed value in the middle of the loaded length.
- `LOAD_FORM_LINE_4` = `1004` — The load magnitude changes linearly, from zero at one end of the loaded length, through two (different) values, back to zero at the other end.

## LoadPoint (class)

The LoadPoint class defines a concentrated force or a bending moment.

### Properties
- `AutomaticPrimaryAxisWeight` (Boolean, get/set) — Property not supported in LoadTemperature class.
- `BoundingBoxDx` (Double, get/set) — The dimension of the bounding box in the X direction.
- `BoundingBoxDy` (Double, get/set) — The dimension of the bounding box in the Y direction.
- `BoundingBoxDz` (Double, get/set) — The dimension of the bounding box in the Z direction.
- `CreateFixedSupportConditionsAutomatically` (Boolean, get/set) — Property not supported in LoadTemperature class.
- `FatherId` (Identifier, get/set) — The identifier of the father object that the load is attached to.
- `Group` (LoadGroup, get/set) — The load group object for the load.
- `Identifier` (Identifier, get/set) — The identifier of the object.
- `InitialAxialElongation` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the initial axial elongation.
- `IsUpToDate` (object, get) — Gets if the object does not have a modification which is not shared.
- `LoadAttachment` (Load.LoadAttachmentEnum, get/set) — The load attachment.
- `LoadDispersionAngle` (Double, get/set) — Property not supported in LoadTemperature class.
- `ModificationTime` (object, get) — Gets latest time of the object was modified or created.
- `PartFilter` (String, get/set) — The part filter.
- `PartNames` (Load.LoadPartNamesEnum, get/set) — The part names.
- `Position1` (Point, get/set) — Gets or sets the first input position point.
- `Position2` (Point, get/set) — Gets or sets the second input position point.
- `PrimaryAxisDirection` (Vector, get/set) — Property not supported in LoadTemperature class.
- `Spanning` (Load.LoadSpanningEnum, get/set) — Property not supported in LoadTemperature class.
- `TemperatureChangeForAxialElongation` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the temperature change for axial elongation.
- `TemperatureDifferentialSideToSide` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the temperature differential from side to side.
- `TemperatureDifferentialTopToBottom` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the temperature differential from top to bottom.
- `Weight` (Double, get/set) — Property not supported in LoadTemperature class.

## LoadUniform (class)

The LoadUniform class defines a uniformly-distributed force bounded by a polygon.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/f2fcb15d-f861-c351-a847-901b47057015)

### Constructors
- `public LoadUniform()` — Creates a new load uniform instance.

### Methods
#### `public int CompareTo(Object obj)`

Compares Identifiers of model objects.

**Parameters:**
- `obj` (System.Object) — The model object to be used in comparison.

**Returns:** `Int32` — 0 if object Identifiers are the same, 1 if given object Identifier is bigger, -1 otherwise.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/79cca356-0c8a-e8fb-463a-34ad4141bec7)

Deletes the load uniform instance with the given ID from the model database.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True on success.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/d7d9ce37-2030-7445-e7ee-7df91162a114)

Check if Identifiers of model objects are same.

**Parameters:**
- `other` (Tekla.Structures.Model.ModelObject) — The model object to be used in comparison.

- `intPropertyNames` (System.Collections.Generic.List<String>) — The names of the int properties to be set.
- `intValues` (System.Collections.Generic.List<Int32>) — The new values for the int properties.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if the property was set successfully.

### Properties
- `AutomaticPrimaryAxisWeight` (Boolean, get/set) — The automatic primary axis weight.
- `BoundingBoxDx` (Double, get/set) — The dimension of the bounding box in the X direction.
- `BoundingBoxDy` (Double, get/set) — The dimension of the bounding box in the Y direction.
- `BoundingBoxDz` (Double, get/set) — The dimension of the bounding box in the Z direction.
- `CreateFixedSupportConditionsAutomatically` (Boolean, get/set) — Indicates whether fixed support conditions are created automatically.
- `DistanceA` (Double, get/set) — The distance a (for enlarging or reducing the loaded area).
- `FatherId` (Identifier, get/set) — The identifier of the father object that the load is attached to.
- `Group` (LoadGroup, get/set) — The load group object for the load.
- `Identifier` (Identifier, get/set) — The identifier of the object.
- `IsUpToDate` (object, get) — Gets if the object does not have a modification which is not shared.
- `LoadAttachment` (Load.LoadAttachmentEnum, get/set) — The load attachment.
- `LoadDispersionAngle` (Double, get/set) — The load dispersion angle.
- `ModificationTime` (object, get) — Gets latest time of the object was modified or created.
- `P1` (Vector, get/set) — The load magnitude vector. The values are given in the local coordinate system of the load (not in the current coordinate system).
- `PartFilter` (String, get/set) — The part filter.
- `PartNames` (Load.LoadPartNamesEnum, get/set) — The part names.
- `Polygon` (Polygon, get/set) — The position points for the uniform load.
- `PrimaryAxisDirection` (Vector, get/set) — The vector for the primary axis direction.
- `Spanning` (Load.LoadSpanningEnum, get/set) — The load spanning.
- `Weight` (Double, get/set) — The weight.

## LoftedPlate (class)

This class represents a lofted plate

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/a0265b09-ae24-4aa3-6d43-d03a19f8f923)
