### Properties
- `AssemblyNumber` (NumberingSeries, get/set) — Gets or sets the assembly number. Defines the numbering in the assembly sense.
- `BaseCurves` (List<ICurve>, get/set) — The curves that define the directrix lines of the lofted plate. A lofted plate is defined by at least to base curves (non empty if they are Polycurve(s)).
- `CastUnitType` (Part.CastUnitTypeEnum, get/set) — Gets or sets the cast unit type of the part.
- `Class` (String, get/set) — Gets or sets the class of the part.
- `DeformingData` (DeformingData, get/set) — Gets or sets the deforming data of the part.
- `FaceType` (LoftedPlate.LoftedPlateFaceTypeEnum, get/set) — Gets or sets what type of solid faces will be used to create lofted plate solid.
- `Finish` (String, get/set) — Gets or sets the finish of the part.
- `Identifier` (Identifier, get/set) — The identifier of the object.
- `IsUpToDate` (object, get) — Gets if the object does not have a modification which is not shared.
- `Material` (Material, get/set) — Gets or sets the material the part is made of.
- `ModificationTime` (object, get) — Gets latest time of the object was modified or created.
- `Name` (String, get/set) — Gets or sets the name of the part.
- `PartNumber` (NumberingSeries, get/set) — Gets or sets the part number. Defines the numbering in the part sense.
- `Position` (Position, get/set) — Gets or sets the part position. Defines the way the part is positioned in the model.
- `PourPhase` (Int32, get/set) — Gets or sets the pour phase of the part.
- `Profile` (Profile, get/set) — Gets or sets the profile of the part.

## LoftedPlate.LoftedPlateFaceTypeEnum (enum)

Lofted plate solid creation face types.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/f535a70c-d17d-cb0d-60a6-f472d5ec5664)

### Values
- `Perpendicular` = `0` — Faces are perpendicular to the thickness direction.
- `BoundedByCurvePlanes` = `1` — Faces following each curve plane.

## LoftedPlateOperationException (class)

This empty class serves as a base (marker class) for all the exception thrown in lofted plate operations

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/de853339-6173-37a9-4b73-fc8db446420f)

### Constructors
- `public LoftedPlateOperationException(string message)` — Constructs an instance of the class

## LogicalWeld (class)

The LogicalWeld class represents a group of welds in the model. A logical weld contains a list of children welds. The primary properties need to be the same in all the children welds.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/fc9878e2-e8b0-356f-5c7c-d06b71bd3057)

### Constructors
- `public LogicalWeld(BaseWeld MainWeld)` — Creates a new logical weld instance.

### Methods
#### `public bool AddWeld(BaseWeld Weld)`

Adds a weld to the logical weld.

**Parameters:**
- `Weld` (Tekla.Structures.Model.BaseWeld) — The weld to be added.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True on success.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/ef69a589-32c7-e352-0a9c-488305acb6f5)

Compares Identifiers of model objects.

### Properties
- `AdditionalSizeAbove` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the additional size above for combination welds.
- `AdditionalSizeBelow` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the additional size below for combination welds.
- `AngleAbove` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the angle above.
- `AngleBelow` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the angle below.
- `AroundWeld` (Boolean, get/set) — Gets or sets a value indicating whether the weld is an around weld (true) or an edge weld (false).
- `ConnectAssemblies` (Boolean, get/set) — Gets or sets a value indicating whether to connect a part or an assembly as a secondary part (false) or as a sub-assembly (true).
- `ContourAbove` (BaseWeld.WeldContourEnum, get/set) — Gets or sets the contour above.
- `ContourBelow` (BaseWeld.WeldContourEnum, get/set) — Gets or sets the contour below.
- `EffectiveThroatAbove` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the effective throat above.
- `EffectiveThroatBelow` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the effective throat below.
- `ElectrodeClassification` (BaseWeld.WeldElectrodeClassificationEnum, get/set) — Gets or sets the weld electrode classification.
- `ElectrodeCoefficient` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the electrode strength coefficient.
- `ElectrodeStrength` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the electrode strength.
- `FinishAbove` (BaseWeld.WeldFinishEnum, get/set) — Gets or sets the finish above.
- `FinishBelow` (BaseWeld.WeldFinishEnum, get/set) — Gets or sets the finish below.
- `Identifier` (Identifier, get/set) — The identifier of the object.
- `IncrementAmountAbove` (Int32, get/set) — Gets or sets the increment amount above.
- `IncrementAmountBelow` (Int32, get/set) — Gets or sets the increment amount below.
- `IntermittentType` (BaseWeld.WeldIntermittentTypeEnum, get/set) — Gets or sets the weld intermittent type.
- `IsUpToDate` (object, get) — Gets if the object does not have a modification which is not shared.
- `LengthAbove` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the length above.
- `LengthBelow` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the length below.
- `MainObject` (ModelObject, get/set) — Gets or sets the main part of the weld.
- `ModificationTime` (object, get) — Gets latest time of the object was modified or created.
- `NDTInspection` (BaseWeld.WeldNDTInspectionEnum, get/set) — Gets or sets the NDT inspection level.
- `PitchAbove` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the pitch above.
- `PitchBelow` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the pitch below.
- `Placement` (BaseWeld.WeldPlacementTypeEnum, get/set) — Gets or sets the weld placement.
- `PrefixAboveLine` (String, get/set) — Gets or sets the size prefix above the line.
- `PrefixBelowLine` (String, get/set) — Gets or sets the size prefix below the line.
- `Preparation` (BaseWeld.WeldPreparationTypeEnum, get/set) — Gets or sets the weld preparation.
- `ProcessType` (BaseWeld.WeldProcessTypeEnum, get/set) — Gets or sets the process type.
- `ReferenceText` (String, get/set) — Gets or sets the reference text.
- `RootFaceAbove` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the root face above.
- `RootFaceBelow` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the root face below.
- `RootOpeningAbove` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the root opening above.
- `RootOpeningBelow` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the root opening below.
- `SecondaryObject` (ModelObject, get/set) — Gets or sets the secondary part of the weld.
- `ShopWeld` (Boolean, get/set) — Gets or sets a value indicating whether the weld is a shop weld (true) or a site weld (false).
- `SizeAbove` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the size above.
- `SizeBelow` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the size below.
- `Standard` (String, get/set) — Gets or sets the weld detail/standard.
- `StitchWeld` (Boolean, get/set) — Gets or sets a value indicating whether the weld is stitched (true) or not stitched (false).
- `TypeAbove` (BaseWeld.WeldTypeEnum, get/set) — Gets or sets the type above.
- `TypeBelow` (BaseWeld.WeldTypeEnum, get/set) — Gets or sets the type below.
- `WeldNumber` (Int32, get) — Gets the weld number.
- `WeldNumberPrefix` (String, get/set) — Gets or sets the weld number prefix.

## Material (class)

The Material class represents a single material that parts can be made of.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/b76cd4d2-f863-0e66-174a-99dda6afc8d6)

### Constructors
- `public Material()` — Instantiates an empty material instance.

### Properties
- `MaterialString` (String, get/set) — Identifies the material in a string format.

## Model (class)

The Model class represents a single model open in Tekla Structures. Before interaction with the model, the user will have to create one instance of this class.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/01eab525-e3d6-22d8-b31a-e4c733e03d43)

### Constructors
- `public Model()` — Creates a "handle" to the currently open model.

### Methods
#### `public bool CommitChanges(string Message)`

Commits the changes made to the model database so far. One commit is something that a user can later on undo with the undo command. A commit also launches the drawing of the changed product model to the visible views. A plug-in should never call CommitChanges, since this would make undo very difficult for the user to do.

**Parameters:**
- `Message` (System.String) — The message to be saved with the commit.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True on success.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/6b589509-80c1-8a7d-4c6d-718ccf01b9d3)

#### `public bool CommitChanges()`

Commits the changes made to the model database so far. One commit is something that a user can later on undo with the undo command. A commit also launches the drawing of the changed product model to the visible views. A dependent plug-in should never call CommitChanges, since this would make undo very difficult for the user to do. However, non-dependent plug-ins require a separate commit if new objects are created.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True on success.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/06690b14-dafd-82b5-ee4c-51292a3e3055)

#### `public List<ModelObject> FetchModelObjects(List<Identifier> Ids, bool SelectInstances = true)`

Fetches a list of modelobjects based on given identifier list of objects, and optionally selects objects before returning them.

**Parameters:**
- `Ids` (System.Collections.Generic.List<Identifier>) — The identifier list to look for in the model.
- `SelectInstances` (System.Boolean) — Optional parameter whether object is selected, default = true.

**Returns:** `List<ModelObject>` — The model objects with the given identifiers skipping the objects which do not exists or object data loading failed for some reason.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/a1d2ba4a-7713-0fb2-5e0c-671708b98b58)

#### `public List<ModelObject> FetchModelObjects(List<string> Guids, bool SelectInstances = true)`

Fetches a list of modelobjects based on given guid list of objects, checks if guid is native or external (for reference model object) and optionally selects objects before returning them.

**Parameters:**
- `Guids` (System.Collections.Generic.List<String>) — The GUID list to look for in the model.
- `SelectInstances` (System.Boolean) — Optional parameter whether object is selected, default = true.

**Returns:** `List<ModelObject>` — The model objects with the given guids, or empty list if any of the objects do not exists or object data loading failed for some reason.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/fb4054d2-dfad-6bb6-c07d-0298ea5e1833)

#### `public ClashCheckHandler GetClashCheckHandler()`

Returns a new clash check handler.

**Returns:** `ClashCheckHandler` — The new clash check handler instance.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/e27b5954-d2a6-55e5-3b08-c0d3c8e8fba6)

#### `public bool GetConnectionStatus()`

Returns true if a proper connection to the Tekla Structures process has been established. Currently, there's no way to re-establish the connection.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True on success.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/aea8fbbf-cf8f-7128-f6e9-e3ef045497d4)

#### `public string GetGUIDByIdentifier(Identifier identifier)`

Returns the GUID of the given identifier instance.

**Parameters:**
- `identifier` (Tekla.Structures.Identifier) — The identifier to look for in the model.

**Returns:** `String` — The GUID of the identifier.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/01994be1-6039-ca3b-98fb-1deb2aeba040)

#### `public Identifier GetIdentifierByGUID(string guid)`

Returns an identifier instance that has the given GUID in the model.

**Parameters:**
- `guid` (System.String) — The GUID to look for in the model.

**Returns:** `Identifier` — The identifier of the GUID, or null if not found.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/3fb69e75-03a4-e79b-4f2d-8738407ffabe)

#### `public ModelInfo GetInfo()`

Returns information about the currently open model.

**Returns:** `ModelInfo` — A ModelInfo instance on success.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/de955ec1-7513-ad43-07ea-95d11ab3c34c)

#### `public ModelObjectSelector GetModelObjectSelector()`

Returns a ModelObjectSelector instance from which different kind of selections can be made.

**Returns:** `ModelObjectSelector` — A new ModelObjectSelector instance.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/fb9935eb-3299-ce0b-7415-c08eed8a8244)

#### `public PhaseCollection GetPhases()`

Returns information about the current model's phases.

**Returns:** `PhaseCollection` — A PhaseCollection instance on success.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/4bc73a5f-a7d0-72d1-1e9d-87587dd37139)

#### `public ProjectInfo GetProjectInfo()`

Returns information about the current model's project.

**Returns:** `ProjectInfo` — A ProjectInfo instance on success.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/dc119752-ab2b-b228-b92e-2e7d08a6f599)

#### `public List<double> GetReportPropertyDouble(IList<Identifier> identifiers, string property)`

Get a list of property values, where the property is of type double, for the given identifiers.

**Parameters:**
- `identifiers` (System.Collections.Generic.IList<Identifier>) — The list of identifiers to get the property for.
- `property` (System.String) — The name of the property to get.

**Returns:** `List<Double>` — The double values of the property for the given identifiers.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/0c8df735-b799-f799-b733-fd15cc8efd16)

#### `public List<int> GetReportPropertyInt(IList<Identifier> identifiers, string property)`

Get a list of property values, where the property is of type interger, for the given identifiers.

**Parameters:**
- `identifiers` (System.Collections.Generic.IList<Identifier>) — The list of identifiers to get the property for.
- `property` (System.String) — The name of the property to get.

**Returns:** `List<Int32>` — The integer values of the property for the given identifiers.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/a72cac03-20d1-216f-471f-7a8b491ba1f3)

#### `public List<string> GetReportPropertyStr(IList<Identifier> identifiers, string property)`

Get a list of property values, where the property is of type string, for the given identifiers.

**Parameters:**
- `identifiers` (System.Collections.Generic.IList<Identifier>) — The list of identifiers to get the property for.
- `property` (System.String) — The name of the property to get.

**Returns:** `List<String>` — The string values of the property for the given identifiers.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/3387b364-3010-d135-79c6-8f45d935e25f)

#### `public WorkPlaneHandler GetWorkPlaneHandler()`

Returns a new work plane handler for the model. The work plane handler can be used to manipulate the current work plane in the model.

**Returns:** `WorkPlaneHandler` — The new work plane handler instance.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/f0559338-2fb2-eb85-3986-9717b9637c73)

#### `public ModelObject SelectModelObject(Identifier ID)`

Takes as input an identifier to an object and then checks its type and instantiates and selects it before returning it.

**Parameters:**
- `ID` (Tekla.Structures.Identifier) — The identifier of the model object that is to be selected.

**Returns:** `ModelObject` — The selected model object with the given identifier, or null if object does not exists or object data loading failed for some reason.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/42921956-058f-e5b1-1765-02ad74c57f55)

## ModelHandler (class)

The ModelHandler class provides information about the currently open Tekla Structures model.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/4a2739ce-3c44-53ae-9246-2d6ce85d750b)

### Constructors
- `public ModelHandler()` — Creates a new ModelHandler instance.

### Methods
#### `public void Close()`

Closes current model.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/8f0e93e3-83f2-50b9-9c60-5a6ac54c5764)

#### `public bool CreateNewMultiUserModel(string ModelName, string ModelFolder, string ServerName)`

Creates a new multi-user model.

**Parameters:**
- `ModelName` (System.String) — The name for the new model.
- `ModelFolder` (System.String) — The folder path for the new model.
- `ServerName` (System.String) — The multi user server name.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True on success.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/26fdf9dc-2f34-5ea8-af1c-af891809822b)

#### `public bool CreateNewSingleUserModel(string ModelName, string ModelFolder, string TemplateNameOrTemplatePath = "")`

Creates a new single user model.

**Parameters:**
- `ModelName` (System.String) — The name for the new model.
- `ModelFolder` (System.String) — The folder path for the new model.
- `TemplateNameOrTemplatePath` (System.String) — The optional template name for the new model.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True on success.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/4a87383d-506b-847e-0259-27e1e9b1dfb0)

#### `public bool IsModelAutoSaved(string ModelFolder)`

Tells whether a model has auto saved information.

**Parameters:**
- `ModelFolder` (System.String) — The model folder path to be used.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if there is auto saved information.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/df868bbd-a070-1cc4-349f-e2ef21aa408c)

#### `public bool IsModelSaved()`

Tells whether current model has been saved.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if there are no unsaved changes in the model.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/c2a7a29e-4c4b-f75a-1116-d3c0e276fd25)

#### `public bool Open(string ModelFolder, bool OpenAutoSaved = false)`

Opens a new model to Tekla Structures without saving changes to current model.

**Parameters:**
- `ModelFolder` (System.String) — The model folder path to be used.
- `OpenAutoSaved` (System.Boolean) — Tells whether to open auto saved information or not. Optional parameter, default = false

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True on success.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/3c82285d-8ad8-bd71-cc74-874ea0ef0e16)

#### `public bool Save(string Comment = "", string User = "")`

Saves current model with comment and user information.

**Parameters:**
- `Comment` (System.String) — The comment for save operation, used only with multi-user models.
- `User` (System.String) — The user identification, used only with multi-user models.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True on success.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/c828c8fa-25b4-eb78-f9dc-0d465498e3d0)

#### `public bool Save(string Comment, string User, string Reason)`

Saves current model with comment and user information.

**Parameters:**
- `Comment` (System.String) — The comment for save operation, used only with multi-user models.
- `User` (System.String) — The user identification, used only with multi-user models.
- `Reason` (System.String) — The reason why this model save is being called. Used for identifying model save events.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True on success.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/7373ac01-ea88-2ffa-ddae-01a37ed87e2c)

## ModelInfo (class)

The ModelInfo class provides information about the currently open Tekla Structures model.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/cc2d593e-65f0-07c0-e4ab-17c60fe4f221)

### Properties
- `CurrentPhase` (Int32, get/set) — The number of the Tekla Structures model's current phase.
- `ModelName` (String, get/set) — The name of the Tekla Structures model.
- `ModelPath` (String, get/set) — The path to the Tekla Structures model.
- `NorthDirection` (Double, get/set) — The north direction of the current Tekla Structures model.
- `SharedModel` (Boolean, get) — The read-only property of the Tekla Structures model's sharing status.
- `SingleUserModel` (Boolean, get) — The read-only property if the Tekla Structures model is a single user model.

## ModelObject (class)

The ModelObject class is an abstract base class for all model objects, such as parts, bolts, welds and reinforcements.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/a90863bf-5ccc-e808-566a-a2ef79a9224d)

### Methods
#### `public int CompareTo(Object obj)`

Compares Identifiers of model objects.

**Parameters:**
- `obj` (System.Object) — The model object to be used in comparison.

**Returns:** `Int32` — 0 if object Identifiers are the same, 1 if given object Identifier is bigger, -1 otherwise.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/79cca356-0c8a-e8fb-463a-34ad4141bec7)

#### `public abstract bool Delete()`

Deletes the instance from the model database.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True on success.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/0b922776-ccee-4b5e-4a12-4e5306802e90)

#### `public bool Equals(ModelObject other)`

Check if Identifiers of model objects are same.

**Parameters:**
- `other` (Tekla.Structures.Model.ModelObject) — The model object to be used in comparison.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if object Identifiers are the same, false otherwise,

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/e4324265-2490-00de-4139-63a8f7acb492)

#### `public bool GetAllReportProperties(ArrayList stringNames, ArrayList doubleNames, ArrayList integerNames, ref Hashtable values)`

Retrieves all properties for the model object in one hashtable. Type for the returned value must be checked using type casting.

**Parameters:**
- `stringNames` (System.Collections.ArrayList) — An array list containing the names of the string properties.
- `doubleNames` (System.Collections.ArrayList) — An array list containing the names of the double properties.
- `integerNames` (System.Collections.ArrayList) — An array list containing the names of the integer properties.
- `values` (System.Collections.Hashtable.) — The return value, a hash table list that contains all the values that were retrieved successfully.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if at least one single property was retrieved successfully.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/0708f404-ce77-28e1-4ebe-fa906f68bff8)

#### `public bool GetAllUserProperties(ref Hashtable values)`

Retrieves all properties for the model object in one hashtable. Type for the returned value must be checked using type casting.

**Parameters:**
- `values` (System.Collections.Hashtable.) — The return value, a hash table that contains all the values that were retrieved successfully.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if at least one single property was retrieved successfully.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/63b853a0-0af5-af9c-3ce7-05299664d7f8)

Returns an enumerator of all the children model objects.

**Returns:** `ModelObjectEnumerator` — A model object enumerator on success, otherwise null.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/ce51ec40-310c-6067-a89f-f8d029aab747)

Returns the coordinate system for the given model object.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/8adfe9cd-f6e5-6474-1297-33c6270a7b0a)

#### `public string GetCustomObjectType()`

Gets custom object type name from an existing object.

**Returns:** `String` — Type name string, empty string if type is not set.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/d3be2ba3-8533-7165-f2d3-a5aa9df556b8)

#### `public bool GetDoubleReportProperties(ArrayList names, ref Hashtable values)`

Retrieves multiple double report properties for the model object. It is faster to fetch multiple properties at once.

**Parameters:**
- `names` (System.Collections.ArrayList) — An array list containing the names of the properties.
- `values` (System.Collections.Hashtable.) — The return value, a hash table that contains all the values that were retrieved successfully.

- `values` (System.Collections.Hashtable.) — The return value, a hash table that contains all the values that were retrieved successfully.

Gets a dynamic string property from the model object.

- `name` (System.String) — The name of the property to be set.
- `value` (System.String.) — The new value for the property.

Selects the model object instance from the model database.

#### `public bool SetCustomObjectType(string type)`

Sets a custom object type name for an existing object.

**Parameters:**
- `type` (System.String) — The custom obect type name to set to the model object.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True on success.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/3d6fb91b-48a9-35ca-d498-526514344089)

#### `public bool SetDynamicStringProperty(string name, string value)`

Sets a dynamic string property for the model object.

- `name` (System.String) — The name of the property to be set.
- `value` (System.String) — The new value for the property.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if the property was set successfully.

Sets a label for an object when a new instance is created, this method must be called before Insert. The label is used in plug-ins for identifying the changed object in modification.

**Parameters:**
- `label` (System.String) — The label to set to the model object.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True on success.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/3efcfc44-613a-e66b-9ebd-9f86df4f4036)

#### `public bool SetPhase(Phase phase)`

Sets the phase of the model object.

**Parameters:**
- `phase` (Tekla.Structures.Model.Phase) — The new phase. A phase with this phase's number must exist in the database.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True on success.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/ed80abcc-0ff7-d081-2229-e651f858a727)

#### `public bool SetUserProperties(List<string> stringPropertyNames, List<string> stringValues, List<string> doublePropertyNames, List<double> doubleValues, List<string> intPropertyNames, List<int> intValues)`

Sets multiple properties for the model object.

**Parameters:**
- `stringPropertyNames` (System.Collections.Generic.List<String>) — The names of the string properties to be set.
- `stringValues` (System.Collections.Generic.List<String>) — The new values for the string properties.
- `doublePropertyNames` (System.Collections.Generic.List<String>) — The names of the double properties to be set.
- `doubleValues` (System.Collections.Generic.List<Double>) — The new values for the double properties.
- `intPropertyNames` (System.Collections.Generic.List<String>) — The names of the int properties to be set.
- `intValues` (System.Collections.Generic.List<Int32>) — The new values for the int properties.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if the property was set successfully.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/8659da37-d571-f03f-c666-5c9fa3b113f5)

#### `public bool SetUserProperty(string name, double value)`

Sets a double property for the model object.

**Parameters:**
- `name` (System.String) — The name of the property to be set.
- `value` (System.Double) — The new value for the property.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if the property was set successfully.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/5c585b8b-5c2e-7b75-2242-758f17429396)

#### `public bool SetUserProperty(string name, int value)`

Sets an integer property for the model object.

**Parameters:**
- `name` (System.String) — The name of the property to be set.
- `value` (System.Int32) — The new value for the property.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if the property was set successfully.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/dd5b828d-3a87-bb58-5ac9-e1c3b4c2fe4b)

#### `public bool SetUserProperty(string name, string value)`

Sets a string property for the model object.

**Parameters:**
- `name` (System.String) — The name of the property to be set.
- `value` (System.String) — The new value for the property.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if the property was set successfully.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/80bf0348-b651-3d4a-862b-49c85bc924ab)

### Properties
- `Identifier` (Identifier, get/set) — The identifier of the object.
- `IsUpToDate` (object, get) — Gets if the object does not have a modification which is not shared.
- `ModificationTime` (object, get) — Gets latest time of the object was modified or created.

## ModelObject.ModelObjectEnum (enum)

All possible concrete model object subclasses are defined here. This enumeration can be used to fetch certain types of objects from the Tekla Structures model.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/40af6269-5d48-d7a0-3b34-95ef2f744c3e)

### Values
- `UNKNOWN` = `0` — The unknown model object.
- `BEAM` = `1` — The beam.
- `POLYBEAM` = `2` — The polybeam.
- `CONTOURPLATE` = `3` — The contour plate.
- `BOOLEANPART` = `4` — The boolean part.
- `FITTING` = `5` — The fitting.
- `CUTPLANE` = `6` — The cutplane.
- `SURFACE_TREATMENT` = `7` — The surface treatment.
- `WELD` = `8` — The weld.
- `ASSEMBLY` = `9` — The assembly.
- `SINGLEREBAR` = `10` — The single rebar.
- `REBARGROUP` = `11` — The rebar group.
- `REBARMESH` = `12` — The rebar mesh.
- `REBARSTRAND` = `13` — The rebar strand.
- `CONTROL_PLANE` = `14` — The control plane.
- `BOLT_ARRAY` = `15` — The bolt array.
- `BOLT_CIRCLE` = `16` — The bolt circle.
- `BOLT_XYLIST` = `17` — The bolt XY list.
- `LOAD_POINT` = `18` — The point load.
- `LOAD_LINE` = `19` — The line load.
- `LOAD_AREA` = `20` — The area load.
- `LOAD_UNIFORM` = `21` — The uniform load.
- `GRID` = `22` — The grid.
- `GRIDPLANE` = `23` — The grid plane.
- `CONNECTION` = `24` — The connection.
- `COMPONENT` = `25` — The component.
- `SEAM` = `26` — The seam.
- `DETAIL` = `27` — The detail.
- `REFERENCE_MODEL` = `28` — The reference model.
- `REBAR_SPLICE` = `29` — The rebar splice.
- `LOAD_GROUP` = `30` — The load group.
- `TASK` = `31` — The task.
- `TASK_DEPENDENCY` = `32` — The task dependency.
- `TASK_WORKTYPE` = `34` — The task worktype.
- `POLYGON_WELD` = `35` — The polygon weld.
- `LOGICAL_WELD` = `36` — The logical weld.
- `CIRCLEREBAR` = `37` — The circle rebar.
- `HIERARCHIC_DEFINITION` = `38` — The hierarchic definition.
- `HIERARCHIC_OBJECT` = `39` — The hierarchic object.
- `ANALYSIS_GEOMETRY` = `40` — The analysis geometry.
- `ANALYSIS_PART` = `41` — The analysis part.
- `REFERENCE_MODEL_OBJECT` = `42` — The reference model object.
- `CUSTOM_PART` = `43` — The custom part object.
- `CIRCLE_REBARGROUP` = `44` — The circle rebar group.
- `CURVED_REBARGROUP` = `45` — The curved rebar group.
- `EDGE_CHAMFER` = `46` — The edge chamfer.
- `POUR_OBJECT` = `47` — The pour object.
- `POUR_BREAK` = `48` — The pour break.
- `CONTROL_LINE` = `50` — The control line.
- `LOAD_TEMPERATURE` = `51` — The temperature load.
- `BREP` = `52` — The Brep part instance.
- `CONTROL_CIRCLE` = `53` — The control circle.
- `CONTROL_POINT` = `54` — The control point.
- `REBAR_SET` = `55` — The rebar set.
- `REBAR_SET_ADDITION` = `56` — The rebar set addition.
- `REBAR_PROPERTY_MODIFIER` = `57` — The rebar property modifier.
- `REBAR_END_DETAIL_MODIFIER` = `58` — The rebar end-detail modifier.
- `REBAR_SPLITTER` = `59` — The rebar splitter.
- `CONTROL_ARC` = `64` — Construction arc.
- `CONTROL_SPLINE` = `65` — Construction spline
- `CONTROL_POLYCURVE` = `66` — Construction polycurve
- `RADIAL_GRID` = `67` — Radial grid
- `GRID_CYLINDRICAL_SURFACE` = `68` — Cylindrical surfaces of radial grids
- `LOFTED_PLATE` = `69` — The lofted plate
- `LEG_SURFACE_OBJECT` = `70` — The leg surface object.
- `STOREY` = `71` — The storey object.
- `SPACE` = `72` — The building space object.
- `BUILDING` = `73` — The building space object.
- `BUILDING_SITE` = `74` — The site object.

## ModelObjectEnumerator (class)

The ModelObjectEnumerator class provides the means to iterate through model object instances in the current model.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/dfa49e10-8481-338b-9448-aa086d991eb4)

### Methods
#### `public IEnumerator GetEnumerator()`

Gets the enumerator.

**Returns:** `IEnumerator` — The enumerator.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/b9b8dfa0-1aff-df12-b15d-657934d702eb)

#### `public int GetSize()`

Returns the total amount of items.

**Returns:** `Int32` — The total amount of items.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/554f6b86-812b-dce3-6f18-238f1bdfba05)

#### `public bool MoveNext()`

Moves to the next item in the enumerator.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — False on failure.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/f77c087e-87ac-dad4-049a-4d8d6d340d4d)

#### `public void Reset()`

Resets the enumerator to the beginning.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/b62cffa6-b709-1ca1-e52f-64702d76aef6)

### Properties
- `AutoFetch` (Boolean, get/set) — Indicates that the objects are fetched from the model when the enumerator is created. Object information is therefore not anymore fetched when 'Current' item is asked from the enumerator. Warning: changing of TransformationPlane after creation of enumerator or during the enumeration requires a separate selection of object for refreshing the values. Property value is used for all enumerators in application
- `Current` (ModelObject, get) — The current model object instance active in the enumerator. The value is null if there are no more objects left.
- `SelectInstances` (Boolean, get/set) — Indicates that the instance Select() is called when the 'Current' item is asked from the enumerator. The user can set this to 'false' if no members are ever asked from the instance. This is the case when, for example, asking only a report property from the identifier. Warning: normally the user should not change this value.

## ModelObjectEnumerator.EnumeratorTypeEnum (enum)

The types of enumerator available.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/d68984b3-09aa-57bf-5ecb-e89993e7a61c)

### Values
- `ALL_SELECTED` = `1` — Selects all user selected objects from the model.
- `ALL_OBJECTS` = `2` — Selects all objects from the model.
- `MODELOBJECT_CHILDREN` = `3` — Selects children of specified type of given ModelObject
- `BY_FILTER_NAME` = `4` — Selects all the ModelObjects for a given filter name
- `CONNECTED_COMPONENTS` = `5` — Selects all connected components of given ModelObject
- `BY_BOUNDING_BOX` = `6` — Selects all objects intersecting with given bounding box
- `ALL_OBJECTS_WITH_TYPE` = `7` — All Objects with type given in type in the "subtype".
- `REFERENCE_MODEL_OBJECTS` = `8` — Selects sub objects of given ReferenceModel.
- `MODELOBJECT_FATHERS` = `9` — Selects fathers of specified type of given ModelObject (Tasks mainly)
- `CONNECTED_DEPENDENCIES` = `10` — Selects all connected dependencies of given ModelObject (Tasks mainly)
- `TASKS_OF_SELECTED_OBJECTS` = `11` — Selects all tasks of selected objects
- `MODIFIED_OBJECTS_AFTER_STAMP` = `12` — Selects all modified and created objects
- `FILTERED_OBJECTS_WITH_TYPE` = `13` — Filtered objects with type given in type in the "subtype".
- `DELETED_OBJECTS_AFTER_STAMP` = `14` — Selects all modified objects
- `ALL_PRESELECTED` = `15` — Selects all preselected objects.
- `BY_BOUNDING_BOX_AND_VIEW` = `16` — Selects all objects that intersect with the given bounding box and that are visible in the given view.
- `CONVERTED_FROM_REFERENCE_MODEL` = `17` — Selects objects that were converted from a given reference model (if they weren't unlinked)
- `ATTRIBUTE_MODIFIED_OBJECTS_AFTER_STAMP` = `18` — Selects all objects of which attributes (UDA) have been modified after the stamp.
- `NUMBERING_MODIFIED_OBJECTS_AFTER_STAMP` = `19` — Selects all objects of which numbering has been modified after the stamp.
- `CREATED_OBJECTS_AFTER_STAMP` = `20` — Selects all created objects
- `MODIFIED_OBJECTS_BY_FILTER_NAME` = `21` — Selects all objects which are new, modified, numbered or which phase has changed after the stamp and pass the filter of given name.
- `PHASE_MODIFIED_OBJECTS_AFTER_STAMP` = `22` — Selects all objects of which phase has been modified after the stamp.
- `OBJECTS_WITH_ANY_MODIFICATION_AFTER_STAMP` = `23` — Selects all objects with any type of modification or are created after the stamp.
- `BY_MODEL_OBJECT_TYPE` = `25` — Selects all model objects with the specified type.

## ModelObjectSelector (class)

The ModelObjectSelector class can be used to make different model object selections from the current model.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/50af1768-22fb-080e-f3d1-08b0da8199da)

### Methods
#### `public ModelObjectEnumerator GetAllObjects()`

Returns an enumerator of all the model objects in the current model.

**Returns:** `ModelObjectEnumerator` — A model object enumerator of all the model objects.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/10561eb7-f852-d0f1-a34e-8cab6c2e3459)

#### `public ModelObjectEnumerator GetAllObjectsWithType(Type[] TypeFilter)`

Returns an enumerator of all the model objects in the current model with the given base type.

**Parameters:**
- `TypeFilter` (.System.Type.) — The types of the objects to return.

**Returns:** `ModelObjectEnumerator` — A model object enumerator of all the model objects with the given types.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/71548129-b401-0376-634f-c3b2add9f38a)

#### `public ModelObjectEnumerator GetAllObjectsWithType(ModelObject.ModelObjectEnum Enum)`

Returns an enumerator of all the model objects in the current model with the given type.

**Parameters:**
- `Enum` (Tekla.Structures.Model.ModelObject.ModelObjectEnum) — The type of the objects to return.

**Returns:** `ModelObjectEnumerator` — A model object enumerator of all the model objects with the given type.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/d6c4b8b6-3636-3060-9458-a196720c6ba9)

#### `public ModelObjectEnumerator GetEnumerator()`

Returns an enumerator of all the model objects in the current model.

**Returns:** `ModelObjectEnumerator` — A model object enumerator of all the model objects.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/cef5b6c6-2855-343a-7ccd-3e3c16e3cf47)

#### `public ModelObjectEnumerator GetFilteredObjectsWithType(ModelObject.ModelObjectEnum Enum, string FilterName)`

Returns an enumerator of the model objects in the current model with the given type and selected by the filter.

**Parameters:**
- `Enum` (Tekla.Structures.Model.ModelObject.ModelObjectEnum) — The type of the objects to return.
- `FilterName` (System.String) — The name of an existing selection filter to apply.

**Returns:** `ModelObjectEnumerator` — A model object enumerator of the model objects with the given type and selected by the filter.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/1c569914-2d33-622e-1aae-8105c059ea09)

#### `public ModelObjectEnumerator GetObjectsByBoundingBox(Point MinPoint, Point MaxPoint)`

Returns an enumerator of the model objects in the current model colliding with the given geometrical bounding box. Note that this method uses approximate bounding boxes and thus is NOT EXACT, and may return objects not necessarily colliding with the given box but only being somewhere near to it.

**Parameters:**
- `MinPoint` (Tekla.Structures.Geometry3d.Point) — The minimum point of the bounding box.
- `MaxPoint` (Tekla.Structures.Geometry3d.Point) — The maximum point of the bounding box.

**Returns:** `ModelObjectEnumerator` — A model object enumerator of the model objects colliding with the given bounding box.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/0947a69e-18cb-ca41-cb95-c36c19b1c85e)

#### `public ModelObjectEnumerator GetObjectsByFilter(FilterExpression FilterExpression)`

Returns an enumerator of the model objects in the current model selected by the given selection filter definition.

**Parameters:**
- `FilterExpression` (Tekla.Structures.Filtering.FilterExpression) — The definition of a selection filter to apply.

**Returns:** `ModelObjectEnumerator` — A model object enumerator of the objects selected by the filter.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/84095767-c855-1698-b819-6f73c55e19b6)

#### `public ModelObjectEnumerator GetObjectsByFilterName(string FilterName)`

Returns an enumerator of the model objects in the current model selected by the given selection filter.

**Parameters:**
- `FilterName` (System.String) — The name of an existing selection filter to apply.

**Returns:** `ModelObjectEnumerator` — A model object enumerator of the objects selected by the filter.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/b51f9696-1097-13a6-7fac-f79264fabcbd)

## ModelSharingHandler (class)

Handles operations related to model sharing.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/60cc1fcf-a879-b4b3-10f8-cb27e1a52da4)

### Constructors
- `public ModelSharingHandler()` — Initializes a new instance of the ModelSharingHandler class.

### Methods
#### `public Task<ISharingResult> CreateBaselineAsync(string code, string comment, string releaseNextWriteOutComment = null)`

Make a new baseline and upload it to the service with the specified code and comment.

**Parameters:**
- `code` (System.String) — The code for the write-out operation.
- `comment` (System.String) — A comment describing the write-out.
- `releaseNextWriteOutComment` (System.String) — Optional. A comment for releasing the next write-out reservation. If provided, must not be empty.

**Returns:** `Task<ISharingResult>` — An awaitable task that returns an ISharingResult indicating the result of the making baseline operation.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/cb604e29-4672-1aa7-6063-7fedbdcf7510)

#### `public ISharingResult ExcludeAndOpen(string modelPath)`

Excludes the specified model from sharing and opens it. The currently opened model is closed.

**Parameters:**
- `modelPath` (System.String) — The path to the model to exclude from sharing and open.

**Returns:** `ISharingResult` — An ISharingResult indicating the result of the operation.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/252881cd-c926-f1dd-d6c0-43d250b09934)

#### `public Task<ISharingResult<ISharingLocationsInformation>> GetLocationsAsync()`

Gets the list of available locations for shared models.

**Returns:** `Task<ISharingResult<ISharingLocationsInformation>>` — An awaitable task that returns information about available locations.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/3ebe852a-f1d7-97ad-6015-3ed8cb534fcd)

#### `public Task<ISharingResult<IEnumerable<ISharedModel>>> GetModelsAsync()`

Gets the list of shared models available for the current user.

**Returns:** `Task<ISharingResult<IEnumerable<ISharedModel>>>` — An awaitable task that returns an ISharingResult containing a collection of ISharedModel.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/ad157052-8f0f-1a44-904e-6b445728cb7a)

#### `public ISharingEvents GetSharingEvents()`

Gets an instance of ISharingEvents to subscribe to sharing-related events.

**Returns:** `ISharingEvents` — An ISharingEvents instance for handling sharing events such as SharedModelStateChanged.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/2e80e39c-d769-e7f8-facf-1ac2af7f27dc)

#### `public Task<ISharingResult<IEnumerable<ISharingUpdate>>> GetUpdatesAsync(Guid? modelId = null)`

Get available updates for the model.

**Parameters:**
- `modelId` (System.Nullable<Guid>) — The model id. If not provided or empty, the currently opened model is used.

**Returns:** `Task<ISharingResult<IEnumerable<ISharingUpdate>>>` — An awaitable task that returns a list of udpates.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/2b0eb561-5a91-b03c-af2d-f81a8d335ef7)

#### `public Task<ISharingResult> InviteUserAsync(string userEmail, SharingRole role, bool sendEmail, string customEmailMessage, Guid? modelId = null)`

Invite sharing user.

**Parameters:**
- `userEmail` (System.String) — The user email.
- `role` (Tekla.Structures.Model.SharingRole) — The user role.
- `sendEmail` (System.Boolean) — If set, send an invitation email to the new user.
- `customEmailMessage` (System.String) — If non-empty, add the message to the invitation email. HTML markup can be used.
- `modelId` (System.Nullable<Guid>) — If set and nonempty, the model id to invite user to. If not set or empty, user is added to the currently opened model.

**Returns:** `Task<ISharingResult>` — Awaitable task with the operation result.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/51d37ad7-f0fa-b085-33d5-552b2883e1a8)

#### `public Task<ISharingResult> JoinAsync(Guid modelId, string joinFolder, Guid? targetUpdate = null)`

Joins a shared model using the specified model identifier and join folder.

**Parameters:**
- `modelId` (System.Guid) — The model identifier.
- `joinFolder` (System.String) — The folder to join to.
- `targetUpdate` (System.Nullable<Guid>) — The update to join to. If not set or empty, join to the latest update.

**Returns:** `Task<ISharingResult>` — 

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/fe2cf713-f044-cdd1-665e-ee6c6367e98b)

#### `public Task<ISharingResult> ReadInAsync(Guid? targetUpdate = null)`

Reads in updates to the shared model.

**Parameters:**
- `targetUpdate` (System.Nullable<Guid>) — The update to read in. If not set or empty, reads in to the latest update.

**Returns:** `Task<ISharingResult>` — An awaitable task that returns an ISharingResult indicating the result of the read-in operation.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/9ca10bdf-71e2-77b9-d825-de333c557e3d)

#### `public Task<ISharingResult> ReleaseNextWriteOutAsync(string comment)`

Releases a previously reserved next write-out for the current user with the specified comment.

**Parameters:**
- `comment` (System.String) — A comment describing the reason for the release. Must not be empty or whitespace.

**Returns:** `Task<ISharingResult>` — An awaitable task that returns an ISharingResult indicating the result of the release operation.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/3a3cc590-0fba-7993-d1c9-b29691708f7c)

#### `public Task<ISharingResult> RemoveModelAsync(Guid modelId)`

Removes the shared model from the system.

**Parameters:**
- `modelId` (System.Guid) — The identifier of the model to be removed.

**Returns:** `Task<ISharingResult>` — An awaitable task that returns an ISharingResult indicating the result of the remove operation.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/e3d1df04-d59c-eb1a-1758-f8e0852df753)

#### `public Task<ISharingResult> ReserveNextWriteOutAsync(string comment)`

Reserves the next write-out for the current user with the specified comment.

**Parameters:**
- `comment` (System.String) — A comment describing the reason for the reservation. Must not be empty or whitespace.

**Returns:** `Task<ISharingResult>` — An awaitable task that returns an ISharingResult indicating the result of the reserve operation.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/8033a381-f7ce-e0e8-28fd-27c59f00991b)

#### `public Task<ISharingResult<Guid>> StartSharingAsync(string code, string description, Guid? location = null)`

Starts sharing a model with the specified code, description, and location.

**Parameters:**
- `code` (System.String) — The sharing code for the model.
- `description` (System.String) — A description for the shared model.
- `location` (System.Nullable<Guid>) — The location of the shared model. If not provided or empty, the default location is used.

**Returns:** `Task<ISharingResult<Guid>>` — An awaitable task that returns an ISharingResult<T> containing the unique identifier of the shared model.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/673ada8b-5db9-e755-a708-faf0bd5da254)

#### `public Task<ISharingResult> WriteOutAsync(string code, string comment, string releaseNextWriteOutComment = null)`

Writes out changes to the service with the specified code and comment. Automatically release the next write-out reservation if there is one.

**Parameters:**
- `code` (System.String) — The code for the write-out operation.
- `comment` (System.String) — A comment describing the write-out.
- `releaseNextWriteOutComment` (System.String) — Optional. A comment for releasing the next write-out reservation. If provided, must not be empty.

**Returns:** `Task<ISharingResult>` — An awaitable task that returns an ISharingResult indicating the result of the write-out operation.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/3d421c71-06c9-187f-672c-b25e386b9e05)

## NullRulingException (class)

This class represent an exception thrown when a ruling of the lofted plate (i.e. the lines that swipe the plate surface between the base curves) would become zero because of a lofted plate operation

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/6a6c5f35-ba15-bf0c-ebff-7e205ead76fd)

### Constructors
- `public NullRulingException()` — Constructs an instance of the class

## NumberingSeries (class)

The NumberingSeries class describes how an object is to be numbered.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/8788e92a-0d78-4c24-a3cc-039b96c08789)

### Constructors
- `public NumberingSeries()` — Instantiates a default numbering.
- `public NumberingSeries(string Prefix, int Number)` — Instantiates a numbering series with the given arguments.

### Properties
- `Prefix` (String, get/set) — The prefix in numbering.
- `StartNumber` (Int32, get/set) — The start number in numbering.

## NumberingSeriesNullable (class)

The NumberingSeriesNullable class describes how an object is to be numbered.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/e2273fc8-03bf-259b-a74a-ae85efeca9e6)

### Constructors
- `public NumberingSeriesNullable()` — Initializes a new instance of the NumberingSeriesNullable class.
- `public NumberingSeriesNullable(string prefix, int number)` — Initializes a new instance of the NumberingSeriesNullable class with the given arguments.

### Properties
- `Prefix` (String, get/set) — Gets or sets the numbering prefix.
- `StartNumber` (Nullable<Int32>, get/set) — Gets or sets the numbering start number.

## Object (class)

The Object class is an abstract base class for all the objects Tekla Structures has.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/9e5dc86b-68fe-395d-5ad0-5d4ca084ab36)

### Properties
- `Identifier` (Identifier, get/set) — The identifier of the object.

## Offset (class)

The Offset class defines the offset value of start points and end points. The offset is the distance from the current work plane in the three global directions.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/288e8450-b840-d318-343e-9098448754b4)

### Constructors
- `public Offset()` — Instantiates an offset with zero distance.

### Properties
- `Dx` (Double, get/set) — The current object's Dx offset. This value is defined using the object's coordinate system.
- `Dy` (Double, get/set) — The current object's Dy offset. This value is defined using the current work plane.
- `Dz` (Double, get/set) — The current object's Dz offset. This value is defined using the current work plane.

## Part (class)

The Part class represents a part in the model. A part can be either a beam, a polybeam or a contour plate.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/ac720ea3-a9ae-235b-d627-c9c37585b6ec)

### Constructors
- `public Part()` — Initializes a new instance of the Part class with default attributes. The default values are set as follows: Position = new Position();Profile = new Profile();Material = new Material();DeformingData = new DeformingData();Name = "";Class = "";Finish = "";PartNumber = new NumberingSeries("P", 1);AssemblyNumber = new NumberingSeries("A", 1);CastUnitType = CastUnitTypeEnum.PRECAST;

### Methods
#### `public int CompareTo(Object obj)`

Compares Identifiers of model objects.

**Parameters:**
- `obj` (System.Object) — The model object to be used in comparison.

**Returns:** `Int32` — 0 if object Identifiers are the same, 1 if given object Identifier is bigger, -1 otherwise.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/79cca356-0c8a-e8fb-463a-34ad4141bec7)

#### `public bool CompareTo(Part partToCompare)`

Compares the instantiated part with another one.

**Remarks:** If the numbering hasn't been performed the function will also return false.

**Parameters:**
- `partToCompare` (Tekla.Structures.Model.Part) — The part to compare with.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if there are no differences, false otherwise.

Deletes the instance from the model database.

Check if Identifiers of model objects are same.

- `other` (Tekla.Structures.Model.ModelObject) — The model object to be used in comparison.

#### `public bool GetAllReportProperties(ArrayList stringNames, ArrayList doubleNames, ArrayList integerNames, ref Hashtable values)`

Retrieves all properties for the model object in one hashtable. Type for the returned value must be checked using type casting.

**Parameters:**

- `doubleNames` (System.Collections.ArrayList) — An array list containing the names of the double properties.
- `integerNames` (System.Collections.ArrayList) — An array list containing the names of the integer properties.
- `values` (System.Collections.Hashtable.) — The return value, a hash table list that contains all the values that were retrieved successfully.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if at least one single property was retrieved successfully.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/0708f404-ce77-28e1-4ebe-fa906f68bff8)

### Properties
- `AssemblyNumber` (NumberingSeries, get/set) — Gets or sets the assembly number. Defines the numbering in the assembly sense.
- `CastUnitType` (Part.CastUnitTypeEnum, get/set) — Gets or sets the cast unit type of the part.
- `Class` (String, get/set) — Gets or sets the class of the part.
- `DeformingData` (DeformingData, get/set) — Gets or sets the deforming data of the part.
- `Finish` (String, get/set) — Gets or sets the finish of the part.
- `Identifier` (Identifier, get/set) — The identifier of the object.
- `IsUpToDate` (object, get) — Gets if the object does not have a modification which is not shared.
- `Material` (Material, get/set) — Gets or sets the material the part is made of.
- `ModificationTime` (object, get) — Gets latest time of the object was modified or created.
- `Name` (String, get/set) — Gets or sets the name of the part.
- `PartNumber` (NumberingSeries, get/set) — Gets or sets the part number. Defines the numbering in the part sense.
- `Position` (Position, get/set) — Gets or sets the part position. Defines the way the part is positioned in the model.
- `PourPhase` (Int32, get/set) — Gets or sets the pour phase of the part.
- `Profile` (Profile, get/set) — Gets or sets the profile of the part.

## Part.CastUnitTypeEnum (enum)

The cast unit types.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/d8d763ec-4bed-04c2-a930-17d0fb53de92)

### Values
- `PRECAST` = `0` — The precast cast unit type.
- `CAST_IN_PLACE` = `1` — The cast in place cast unit type.

## Phase (class)

The Phase class defines a model object phase.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/2d4746c9-dc38-07d8-c644-b450af02d77d)

### Constructors
- `public Phase()` — Instantiates a new phase with the phase number 0.
- `public Phase(int PhaseNumber)` — Instantiates a new phase with the given phase number.
- `public Phase(int PhaseNumber, string PhaseName, string PhaseComment, int IsCurrentPhase)` — Instantiates a new phase with the given parameters.

### Methods
#### `public bool Delete()`

Deletes a phase. The current phase cannot be deleted.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True on success, false if failed (the attached objects probably caused the failure).

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/908ebf19-ac2e-5ea5-089d-8729f93c2569)

#### `public bool GetUserProperty(string Name, ref double Value)`

Retrieves a double property for the phase.

**Parameters:**
- `Name` (System.String) — The name of the property to be retrieved, in capital letters.
- `Value` (System.Double.) — The return value.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if the value was returned successfully.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/88f635f5-af5a-f8de-142d-1f5aafe13cda)

#### `public bool GetUserProperty(string Name, ref int Value)`

Retrieves an integer property for the phase.

**Parameters:**
- `Name` (System.String) — The name of the property to be retrieved, in capital letters.
- `Value` (System.Int32.) — The return value.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if the value was returned successfully.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/1637afd3-9740-d429-3636-4c665df77bd1)

#### `public bool GetUserProperty(string Name, ref string Value)`

Retrieves a string property for the phase.

**Parameters:**
- `Name` (System.String) — The name of the property to be retrieved, in capital letters.
- `Value` (System.String.) — The return value.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if the value was returned successfully.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/3e94df7a-eb8d-986c-afd6-dd42c8d5cf16)

#### `public bool Insert()`

Creates a new phase.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True on success, false if failed (the reason for the failure is probably that the phase number already exists).

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/3016a6b5-374c-abdc-a3c2-936850c22d67)

#### `public bool Modify()`

Modifies a phase.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True on success, false if failed (PhaseNumber should be higher than 0).

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/1ca533f6-1cc6-47bb-9537-4812b55e976d)

#### `public bool Select()`

Selects a phase.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True on success, false if failed (PhaseNumber should be higher than 0).

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/d107679d-0cab-291b-450e-82917ee51d77)

#### `public bool SetUserProperty(string Name, double Value)`

Sets a double property for the phase.

**Parameters:**
- `Name` (System.String) — The name of the property to be set, in capital letters.
- `Value` (System.Double) — The new value for the property.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if the property was set successfully.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/1b34bb94-be9d-b762-c6aa-c07f23a9aafe)

#### `public bool SetUserProperty(string Name, int Value)`

Sets an integer property for the phase.

**Parameters:**
- `Name` (System.String) — The name of the property to be set, in capital letters.
- `Value` (System.Int32) — The new value for the property.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if the property was set successfully.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/0fdc9fd6-07ce-feb9-4448-91f3279ec822)

#### `public bool SetUserProperty(string Name, string Value)`

Sets a string property for the phase.

**Parameters:**
- `Name` (System.String) — The name of the property to be set, in capital letters.
- `Value` (System.String) — The new value for the property.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if the property was set successfully.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/b6766490-3722-2bce-5ed3-f7c91182df8e)

### Properties
- `IsCurrentPhase` (Int32, get/set) — The value is 1 if the phase is the current phase, 0 otherwise.
- `PhaseComment` (String, get/set) — The phase comment.
- `PhaseName` (String, get/set) — The phase name.
- `PhaseNumber` (Int32, get/set) — The phase number.

## PhaseCollection (class)

The PhaseCollection class handles the collection of the model phases.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/8501ccf0-5464-8ee1-0155-52e30d1742a4)

### Methods
#### `public void CopyTo(Array Array, int Index)`

Copies the elements of the ICollection to an Array, starting at a particular Array index.

**Parameters:**
- `Array` (System.Array) — The one-dimensional Array that is the destination of the elements copied from the ICollection. The Array must have zero-based indexing.
- `Index` (System.Int32) — The zero-based index in the array at which copying begins.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/7a2ef0eb-1767-2b9a-13a9-1b9f88cafa46)

#### `public IEnumerator GetEnumerator()`

Returns an enumerator that iterates through a collection.

**Returns:** `IEnumerator` — An IEnumerator object that can be used to iterate through the collection.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/d9061c80-73fd-e533-e1c5-68d47d4873dc)

### Properties
- `Count` (Int32, get) — Gets the number of elements contained in the ICollection.
- `IsSynchronized` (Boolean, get) — Gets a value indicating whether access to the ICollection is synchronized (thread safe).
- `SyncRoot` (Object, get) — Gets an object that can be used to synchronize access to the ICollection.

## Plane (class)

The Plane class defines a plane in space using an origin, an X-axis and a Y-axis.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/37ad1799-48c1-2b90-a52c-fd0e84b4ec46)

### Constructors
- `public Plane()` — Initializes a new plane instance.

### Properties
- `AxisX` (Vector, get/set) — The X-axis of the plane.
- `AxisY` (Vector, get/set) — The Y-axis of the plane.
- `Origin` (Point, get/set) — The origin of the plane.

## PlateIntersectsWithIntersectionLineException (class)

The PlateIntersectsWithIntersectionLineException class represents an error when, plate polygon extends beyond intersection line.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/092874f5-d392-7d82-429f-f7a9167ca364)

### Constructors
- `public PlateIntersectsWithIntersectionLineException()` — Initializes a new instance of the PlateIntersectsWithIntersectionLineException class with "Plate polygon extends beyond intersection line" error message.

## PointCloud (class)

(No description provided in vendor docs for Tekla.Structures.Model.PointCloud.)

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/1b398fae-616f-2cfa-8940-23f4d30e11c5)

### Constructors
- `public PointCloud()` — Creates a new point cloud instance using the given parameters.

### Methods
#### `public bool Attach()`

Attach this point cloud to model. Can select only Potree type point clouds immediately after attach. Others need to be selected manually after attach has been completed. Use AttachComplete() to find out, if attach process has completed.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if successful, otherwise false.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/959ad250-6b61-2aa2-7fbb-d8517edebe11)

#### `public bool AttachComplete()`

Checks if the point cloud attach is complete. Needed when attaching non-Potree point cloud file, which needs conversion.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if successful, otherwise false.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/75b49d08-b9b4-82d7-15c4-006d7bb75c77)

#### `public bool Detach()`

Detach this point cloud from model.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if successful, otherwise false.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/d78949bd-39ae-6564-b318-5079c4f245cc)

#### `public static List<PointCloud> GetPointClouds()`

Utility function: Gets available point clouds.

**Returns:** `List<PointCloud>` — The available point clouds.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/1fea88c0-bef7-2351-4388-781f1da03780)

#### `public List<View> GetVisibleInViews()`

Gets the views in which this point cloud is visible.

**Returns:** `List<View>` — The available point clouds.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/ea50246a-4f69-ea97-6a67-c7734d15b73a)

#### `public bool Select()`

Select point cloud by Guid, by Name, by OriginalPath or by Url.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if successful, otherwise false.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/1cf3df03-e26a-0776-d572-596b4756c9a1)

#### `public bool SetVisibility(List<View> views, bool visible)`

Set point cloud visibility in given views.

**Parameters:**
- `views` (System.Collections.Generic.List<View>) — The views, in which the point clouds visibility is changed.
- `visible` (System.Boolean) — Visibility value: True for visible, false for hidden.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if successful to all given views, otherwise false.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/8f10f866-0f6e-a6ba-0e8a-9918e1ed65f8)

### Properties
- `BoundingBox` (AABB, get) — Gets or sets the bounding box.
- `Guid` (Guid, get/set) — Gets or sets the guid.
- `LocationBy` (Guid, get/set) — Gets or sets the location by value: Empty for model origin, base point guid for base point.
- `Name` (String, get/set) — Gets or sets the point clouds name. Abbreviated from the point cloud file name. Cannot be given by user.
- `OffsetX` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the offset X.
- `OffsetY` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the offset Y.
- `OffsetZ` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the offset Z.
- `OriginalPath` (String, get/set) — Gets or sets the point clouds path (where it originally located).
- `RotationZ` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the rotation Z.
- `Scale` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the scale.
- `TcPointCloudGuid` (String, get/set) — Gets or sets the Trimble Connect point cloud guid.
- `TcProjectId` (String, get/set) — Gets or sets the Trimble Connect project id.
- `Url` (String, get/set) — Gets or sets the point clouds URL without a file name.
- `UseAutoCreatedBasePoint` (Boolean, get/set) — Gets or sets a value indicating whether an auto-created base point should be used. If set to true, LocationBy value is ignored.

## PolyBeam (class)

The PolyBeam class represents a continuous beam with a contour as input.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/d879e653-76ab-ce1d-7b09-7abcdd6fa22e)

#### `public bool AddContourPoint(ContourPoint contourPoint)`

Adds a contour point to the polybeam.

- `contourPoint` (Tekla.Structures.Model.ContourPoint) — The contour point to be added to the polybeam.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True on success, false if there is no more space for another contour point.

### Properties
- `AssemblyNumber` (NumberingSeries, get/set) — Gets or sets the assembly number. Defines the numbering in the assembly sense.
- `CastUnitType` (Part.CastUnitTypeEnum, get/set) — Gets or sets the cast unit type of the part.
- `Class` (String, get/set) — Gets or sets the class of the part.
- `Contour` (Contour, get/set) — Gets or sets the contour for the polybeam.
- `DeformingData` (DeformingData, get/set) — Gets or sets the deforming data of the part.
- `EndLevelName` (String, get/set) — Gets or sets the end level name.
- `EndLevelOffset` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the end level offset.
- `Finish` (String, get/set) — Gets or sets the finish of the part.
- `Identifier` (Identifier, get/set) — The identifier of the object.
- `IsUpToDate` (object, get) — Gets if the object does not have a modification which is not shared.
- `Material` (Material, get/set) — Gets or sets the material the part is made of.
- `ModificationTime` (object, get) — Gets latest time of the object was modified or created.
- `Name` (String, get/set) — Gets or sets the name of the part.
- `PartNumber` (NumberingSeries, get/set) — Gets or sets the part number. Defines the numbering in the part sense.
- `Position` (Position, get/set) — Gets or sets the part position. Defines the way the part is positioned in the model.
- `PourPhase` (Int32, get/set) — Gets or sets the pour phase of the part.
- `Profile` (Profile, get/set) — Gets or sets the profile of the part.
- `StartLevelName` (String, get/set) — Gets or sets the start level name.
- `StartLevelOffset` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the start level offset.
- `Type` (PolyBeam.PolyBeamTypeEnum, get) — Gets the read only type of the polybeam. The default type for a new polybeam instance is beam.

## PolyBeam.PolyBeamTypeEnum (enum)

The polybeam types.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/252fe607-40e6-7326-862c-944f6c6e4513)

### Values
- `BEAM` = `0` — The polybeam.
- `PANEL` = `1` — The panel.
- `STRIP_FOOTING` = `2` — The strip footing.
- `COLUMN` = `3` — The column.

## Polygon (class)

The Polygon class represents a polygon object that has its corners at the given points.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/b08e87a3-a223-a38f-882e-91c412b7db5e)

### Constructors
- `public Polygon()` — Initializes a new polygon instance with empty attributes.

### Properties
- `Points` (ArrayList, get/set) — The corners of the polygon.

## PolygonNode (class)

The PolygonNode class represents flat geometry tree node.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/8b8b4b86-6354-8422-b25e-3cb648755976)

### Constructors
- `public PolygonNode(Contour contour, bool isAutomaticNode = false)` — Initializes a new instance of the PolygonNode class.

### Methods
#### `public void AcceptVisitor(IGeometryNodeVisitor visitor)`

Entry method for the visitor pattern

**Parameters:**
- `visitor` (Tekla.Structures.Model.IGeometryNodeVisitor)

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/ea91906f-dfb0-e192-f2d5-c912453e9568)

#### `public IGeometryNode Clone()`

Creates a new object that is a copy of the current instance.

**Returns:** `IGeometryNode` — A new object that is a copy of the current instance.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/947896a5-05f7-c3e7-e570-703dc743fdf1)

### Properties
- `Contour` (Contour, get) — Gets or sets the contour points of flat geometry.
- `IsAutomatic` (Boolean, get) — Gets a value indicating whether this geometry node was automatically generated (returns false if it was originally a user-defined part)

## PolygonWeld (class)

The PolygonWeld class represents a polygon weld in the model. A polygon weld has a main part and a secondary part.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/4b43dd4d-bd93-e2a8-7bf6-03cd05b834d6)

### Constructors
- `public PolygonWeld()` — Creates a new polygon weld instance.

### Methods
#### `public int CompareTo(Object obj)`

Compares Identifiers of model objects.

**Parameters:**
- `obj` (System.Object) — The model object to be used in comparison.

**Returns:** `Int32` — 0 if object Identifiers are the same, 1 if given object Identifier is bigger, -1 otherwise.

### Properties
- `AdditionalSizeAbove` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the additional size above for combination welds.
- `AdditionalSizeBelow` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the additional size below for combination welds.
- `AngleAbove` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the angle above.
- `AngleBelow` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the angle below.
- `AroundWeld` (Boolean, get/set) — Gets or sets a value indicating whether the weld is an around weld (true) or an edge weld (false).
- `ConnectAssemblies` (Boolean, get/set) — Gets or sets a value indicating whether to connect a part or an assembly as a secondary part (false) or as a sub-assembly (true).
- `ContourAbove` (BaseWeld.WeldContourEnum, get/set) — Gets or sets the contour above.
- `ContourBelow` (BaseWeld.WeldContourEnum, get/set) — Gets or sets the contour below.
- `EffectiveThroatAbove` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the effective throat above.
- `EffectiveThroatBelow` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the effective throat below.
- `ElectrodeClassification` (BaseWeld.WeldElectrodeClassificationEnum, get/set) — Gets or sets the weld electrode classification.
- `ElectrodeCoefficient` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the electrode strength coefficient.
- `ElectrodeStrength` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the electrode strength.
- `FinishAbove` (BaseWeld.WeldFinishEnum, get/set) — Gets or sets the finish above.
- `FinishBelow` (BaseWeld.WeldFinishEnum, get/set) — Gets or sets the finish below.
- `Identifier` (Identifier, get/set) — The identifier of the object.
- `IncrementAmountAbove` (Int32, get/set) — Gets or sets the increment amount above.
- `IncrementAmountBelow` (Int32, get/set) — Gets or sets the increment amount below.
- `IntermittentType` (BaseWeld.WeldIntermittentTypeEnum, get/set) — Gets or sets the weld intermittent type.
- `IsUpToDate` (object, get) — Gets if the object does not have a modification which is not shared.
- `LengthAbove` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the length above.
- `LengthBelow` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the length below.
- `MainObject` (ModelObject, get/set) — Gets or sets the main part of the weld.
- `ModificationTime` (object, get) — Gets latest time of the object was modified or created.
- `NDTInspection` (BaseWeld.WeldNDTInspectionEnum, get/set) — Gets or sets the NDT inspection level.
- `PitchAbove` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the pitch above.
- `PitchBelow` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the pitch below.
- `Placement` (BaseWeld.WeldPlacementTypeEnum, get/set) — Gets or sets the weld placement.
- `Polygon` (Polygon, get/set) — The polygon for the polygon weld.
- `PrefixAboveLine` (String, get/set) — Gets or sets the size prefix above the line.
- `PrefixBelowLine` (String, get/set) — Gets or sets the size prefix below the line.
- `Preparation` (BaseWeld.WeldPreparationTypeEnum, get/set) — Gets or sets the weld preparation.
- `ProcessType` (BaseWeld.WeldProcessTypeEnum, get/set) — Gets or sets the process type.
- `ReferenceText` (String, get/set) — Gets or sets the reference text.
- `RootFaceAbove` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the root face above.
- `RootFaceBelow` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the root face below.
- `RootOpeningAbove` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the root opening above.
- `RootOpeningBelow` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the root opening below.
- `SecondaryObject` (ModelObject, get/set) — Gets or sets the secondary part of the weld.
- `ShopWeld` (Boolean, get/set) — Gets or sets a value indicating whether the weld is a shop weld (true) or a site weld (false).
- `SizeAbove` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the size above.
- `SizeBelow` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the size below.
- `Standard` (String, get/set) — Gets or sets the weld detail/standard.
- `StitchWeld` (Boolean, get/set) — Gets or sets a value indicating whether the weld is stitched (true) or not stitched (false).
- `TypeAbove` (BaseWeld.WeldTypeEnum, get/set) — Gets or sets the type above.
- `TypeBelow` (BaseWeld.WeldTypeEnum, get/set) — Gets or sets the type below.
- `WeldNumber` (Int32, get) — Gets the weld number.
- `WeldNumberPrefix` (String, get/set) — Gets or sets the weld number prefix.

## Polymesh (class)

The Polygon class represents a polygon object that has its corners at the given points.

### Methods

- `fingerprint2` (System.String) — The input parameter2

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if fingerprints match, false otherwise

#### `public static bool Validate(FacetedBrep brep, Polymesh.PolymeshCheckerFlags checkCriteria, ref List<KeyValuePair<int, Polymesh.PolymeshHealthCheckEnum>> invalidInfo)`

Converts a CopyFacetedBrep to a polymesh and validates.

**Parameters:**
- `brep` (Tekla.Structures.Geometry3d.FacetedBrep) — The FacetedBrep to be converted and validated
- `checkCriteria` (Tekla.Structures.Model.Polymesh.PolymeshCheckerFlags) — The flags to indicate what to check
- `invalidInfo` (System.Collections.Generic.List<KeyValuePair<Int32,Polymesh.PolymeshHealthCheckEnum>>.) — The problematic faces' indices and their failed reasons, (-1, PolymeshUndefined) is for unknown reason for uncertain face. No inner loops checked further if outer loop found error.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if valid, false if error found

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/972f3ac2-c678-16b5-c9f6-110fb4d6a7cd)

### Properties
- `Brep` (FacetedBrep, get/set) — Gets or sets the polymesh that forms the object geometry.

## Polymesh.PolymeshCheckerFlags (enum)

Polymesh check flags, please refer to PolymeshHealthCheckEnum for the corresponding result values.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/9f06f9d1-a8fb-41f2-aa21-b962d632fcdb)

### Values
- `None` = `0` — Perform no check
- `LoopVertexUnique` = `2048` — To check that no two consecutive vertex index may be the same.
- `VerticesOnSameEdge` = `1` — To check outerloop vertices on same edge, distance tolerance 0.01.
- `OuterloopPlanarity` = `2` — To check outerloop's planarity, IN_PLANE_EPSILON 1.0e-3.
- `OuterloopSelfIntersection` = `4` — To check if outerloop intersects itself, EPSILON 1.E-4
- `InnerloopPlanarity` = `8` — To check innterloop's planarity, IN_PLANE_EPSILON 1.0e-3.
- `InnerloopSelfIntersection` = `16` — To check if innerloop intersects itself, EPSILON 1.E-4.
- `InnerloopInsideOuterloop` = `256` — To check if innerloop inside outerloop, EPSILON 1.E-4.
- `LoopNormalValidity` = `32` — The inner loop is oriented clockwise, the opposite of the outer loop (counterclockwise).
- `FaceEdgeOrientation` = `64` — To check face's edge orientation.
- `Multishellness` = `128` — To check are there more than one topologically connected components in the polymesh. This checks only through vertex indexes.
- `MultishellnessGeometrical` = `16384` — To check are there more than one geometrically connected components in the polymesh. Check with all vertex edge face position containment (includes partial edge touching cases). This check can be slow, the behavior is O(n^2).
- `VerticesOnSamePosition` = `512` — To check if there are any vertices that are in the same location, EPSILON 0.01.
- `UnusedVertices` = `1024` — To check if there are any unused vertices in the vertex array.
- `NonManifoldEdges` = `4096` — Are there non-manifold edges (indirect edge vertices are found for more than 2 faces).
- `NullFaces` = `8192` — To check if all vertices of the face are linearly dependent, or having less than 3 vertices, or smaller than allowed edges.
- `All` = `511` — To check all criteria.

## Polymesh.PolymeshHealthCheckEnum (enum)

Polymesh check error result codes. See the PolymeshCheckerFlags to see how to set the various checks.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/d3116a3b-bd01-49c1-ee54-56b2e9a2fb30)

### Values
- `PolymeshLevelError` = `-2` — The vertex array contains vertices that are geometrically equivalent or the polymesh contains unused vertices
- `PolymeshUndefined` = `-1` — Polymesh is null, the creation failed or unknown reasons.
- `PolymeshOk` = `0` — Valid polymesh
- `NumOfVerticesOuterloop` = `1` — Number of vertices in outer loop too small
- `NumOfVerticesInnerloop` = `2` — Number of vertices in innerloop too small
- `VertexArrayOuterloop` = `3` — Check of indices of vertices in outer loop failed
- `VertexArrayInnerloop` = `4` — Check of indices of vertices in the innerloop failed
- `InnerloopNotInsideOuterloop` = `5` — Inner loop is not inside the outer loop
- `VertexArrayForListOfEdges` = `6` — Check of indices of vertices for edge list failed
- `NonPlanarOuterloop` = `7` — Outer loop points are not in the same plane with each other
- `NonPlanarInnerloop` = `8` — Inner loop points are not in the same plane with each other or with outer loop
- `SelfIntersectingOuterloop` = `9` — Outer loop is self-intersecting
- `SelfIntersectingInnerloop` = `10` — Inner loop is self-intersecting
- `LoopNormalsInvalid` = `11` — Inner loop normal direction should be opposite to outer loop
- `FaceEdgesInvalidOrientation` = `12` — Two neighbor faces with common edge have wrong orientation
- `FaceEdgesPartlyInvalidOrientation` = `13` — Two neighbor faces with partly common edge have wrong orientation
- `MultishellPolymesh` = `14` — Polymesh consists topologically of multiple shells
- `MultishellPolymeshByGeometry` = `21` — Polymesh consists geometrically of multiple shells
- `NumberOfFaces` = `15` — Number of faces too small
- `VertexArrayUniqueness` = `16` — The vertex array contains vertices that are geometrically equivalent
- `VertexDuplicatesOnPolygon` = `17` — The same vertex index appears at least twice for a face
- `VertexCollinearOnPolygon` = `18` — The face has at least one vertex that is collinear with others on an edge
- `NonManifoldEdges` = `19` — The polymesh contains non-manifold edges
- `UnusedVertices` = `20` — The polymesh contains unused vertices
