#### `public RebarGeometry GetSingleRebar(int index, bool withHooks)`

Returns a single rebar inside the rebar group located by the given index. The indexing starts from the start point. The rebar represents a physical reinforcing bar and contains physical points in the 3D space of the bar. The method returns null on error, for example if given an erroneous index. The number of rebars in the group can be requested with GetNumberOfRebars().

**Parameters:**
- `index` (System.Int32) — The index of the rebar in the group.
- `withHooks` (System.Boolean) — A parameter to determine whether the hook points should be included in the rebar geometry or not.

**Returns:** `RebarGeometry` — The instance of a RebarGeometry object that contains the point list of the single physical reinforcing bar. Null if the given index was not valid.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/41181569-65f8-f4ba-7722-3cd890c7a3d4)

#### `public RebarGeometry GetSingleRebarWithoutClash(int index, bool withHooks)`

Returns a single rebar inside the rebar group located by the given index. The indexing starts from the start point. The rebar represents a physical reinforcing bar and contains physical, non-clashing points in the 3D space of the bar. The method returns null on error, for example if given an erroneous index. The number of rebars in the group can be requested with GetNumberOfRebars().

**Parameters:**
- `index` (System.Int32) — The index of the rebar in the group.
- `withHooks` (System.Boolean) — A parameter to determine whether the hook points should be included in the rebar geometry or not.

**Returns:** `RebarGeometry` — The instance of a RebarGeometry object that contains the point list of the single physical reinforcing bar. Null if the given index was not valid.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/8fd2e21a-3d2f-6211-92ba-9d6e652ac100)

### Properties
- `Class` (Int32, get/set) — Gets or sets the class of the reinforcement. The class is used to group reinforcements.
- `EndPoint` (Point, get/set) — The end point of the single strand (the start point and end point define the length of the strand).
- `EndPointOffsetType` (Reinforcement.RebarOffsetTypeEnum, get/set) — Gets or sets the type of the end point offset. The options are: OFFSET_TYPE_LEG_LENGTHOFFSET_TYPE_COVER_THICKNESS
- `EndPointOffsetValue` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the concrete cover thickness or leg length at the second end of the bar.
- `Father` (ModelObject, get/set) — Gets or sets the father object of the reinforcement; the model object instance to operate on.
- `FromPlaneOffset` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the offset value from the part surface.
- `Grade` (String, get/set) — Gets or sets the steel grade of the reinforcing bar. The grade indicates the strength of the steel used in reinforcing bars. It can also indicate other factors, such as the weldability or surface deformations of the bar.
- `Identifier` (Identifier, get/set) — The identifier of the object.
- `InputPointDeformingState` (DeformingType, get/set) — Gets or sets the reinforcement input point deforming state.
- `IsUpToDate` (object, get) — Gets if the object does not have a modification which is not shared.
- `ModificationTime` (object, get) — Gets latest time of the object was modified or created.
- `Name` (String, get/set) — Gets or sets the name of the reinforcement.
- `NumberingSeries` (NumberingSeries, get/set) — Gets or sets the numbering series of the reinforcement.
- `OnPlaneOffsets` (ArrayList, get) — Not supported.
- `Patterns` (ArrayList, get/set) — An array list of polygons that represents the patterns of the rebar strand. Give the strand positions in the same order as for the first pattern. The maximum number of patterns is 20.
- `PullPerStrand` (Double, get/set) — The pull per strand (N).
- `RadiusValues` (ArrayList, get/set) — Gets or sets the radius value(s) of the bends in the bar.
- `Size` (String, get/set) — The size of the reinforcement.
- `StartPoint` (Point, get/set) — The start point of the single strand (the start point and end point define the length of the strand).
- `StartPointOffsetType` (Reinforcement.RebarOffsetTypeEnum, get/set) — Gets or sets the type of the start point offset is either OFFSET_TYPE_LEG_LENGTH or OFFSET_TYPE_COVER_THICKNESS.
- `StartPointOffsetValue` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the concrete cover thickness or leg length at the first end of the bar.
- `Unbondings` (ArrayList, get/set) — An array list of StrandUnbondingData items.

## RebarThreadingDataNullable (class)

The RebarThreadingDataNullable class defines the threading properties at the end of a reinforcing bar affected by a RebarEndDetailModifier. If a threading property is null then that property will not be applied to the RebarSet generated reinforcing bars.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/57d87a1e-cd45-eccf-35f1-73a6e5fba633)

### Constructors
- `public RebarThreadingDataNullable()` — Initializes a new instance of the RebarThreadingDataNullable class.

### Properties
- `ExtraFabricationLength` (Nullable<Double>, get/set) — Gets or sets the extra fabrication length.
- `Length` (Nullable<Double>, get/set) — Gets or sets the threading length.
- `ThreadingType` (String, get/set) — Gets or sets the threading type. Null indicates that the threading type is not specified.

## ReferenceModel (class)

The ReferenceModel class contains methods related to reference models. A reference model is a reference of an external model.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/a1ca3d98-d440-e04c-ed85-901efed0470c)

### Constructors
- `public ReferenceModel()` — Initializes a new instance of the ReferenceModel class. The default values are as follows: Filename = string.Empty; ActiveFilePath = string.Empty; ProjectGUID = Guid.Empty; ModelGUID = Guid.Empty; VersionGUID = Guid.Empty; Position = new Point(); Scale = 1.0; Visibility = VisibilityEnum.VISIBLE; BasePointGuid = string.Empty;
- `public ReferenceModel(string filename, Point position, double scale)` — Initializes a new instance of the ReferenceModel class. Creates a new reference model instance using the given filename, position and scale.

### Methods
#### `public int CompareTo(Object obj)`

Compares Identifiers of model objects.

**Parameters:**
- `obj` (System.Object) — The model object to be used in comparison.

**Returns:** `Int32` — 0 if object Identifiers are the same, 1 if given object Identifier is bigger, -1 otherwise.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/79cca356-0c8a-e8fb-463a-34ad4141bec7)

Deletes the reference model with the given identifier.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True on success.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/532a3f22-e1de-20e4-93f1-034f6702119c)

Check if Identifiers of model objects are same.

**Parameters:**
- `other` (Tekla.Structures.Model.ModelObject) — The model object to be used in comparison.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if object Identifiers are the same, false otherwise,

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/db7b2238-5e56-7adb-e06a-cc91f71d21fe)

#### `public bool RefreshFile()`

Reload the reference file from the location specified by Filename. If the file has changed, a new revision will be created and taken into use. Note that if a new revision was successfully created, the ActiveFilePath is changed.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if a new revision was added, false if it was not. If false, the file can either be unchanged or there was an error.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/13291a3d-b362-4520-34c3-418563d3e06f)

#### `public bool RemoveRevision(ReferenceModel.Revision revision)`

Removes the given revision from the reference model.

**Parameters:**
- `revision` (Tekla.Structures.Model.ReferenceModel.Revision) — The reference model revision to be removed.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if removed successfully, otherwise false.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/8eff93f8-cfed-5f17-8767-00bd2fe1c375)

### Properties
- `ActiveFilePath` (String, get) — Gets the path to the local copy of the current revision of the reference model.
- `BasePointGuid` (Guid, get/set) — The GUID of the used base point. Empty if base point is not used.
- `Filename` (String, get/set) — The path to the original location of the reference file.
- `Identifier` (Identifier, get/set) — The identifier of the object.
- `IsUpToDate` (object, get) — Gets if the object does not have a modification which is not shared.
- `ModelGUID` (Guid, get/set) — The ModelGUID of the reference model in the Project Center.
- `ModificationTime` (object, get) — Gets latest time of the object was modified or created.
- `Position` (Point, get/set) — The position of the reference model.
- `ProjectGUID` (Guid, get/set) — The ProjectGUID of the reference model in the Project Center.
- `Rotation` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets value of rotation around Z axis (degrees).
- `Rotation3D` (Rotation3D, get/set) — Gets or sets value of 3d rotation.
- `Scale` (Double, get/set) — The scale of the reference model.
- `Title` (String, get/set) — The reference model name(title).
- `UseWorkplane` (Boolean, set) — Sets whether workplane is used.
- `VersionGUID` (Guid, get/set) — The VersionGUID of the reference model in the Project Center.
- `Visibility` (ReferenceModel.VisibilityEnum, get/set) — The visibility of the reference model in the model view.

## ReferenceModel.Revision (struct)

Reference model revision.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/2d3e9160-2d8e-2de7-7c4c-978d447e3d92)

### Constructors
- `public Revision(int referenceModelId, int id, DateTime time, string hash, string fileName, bool isCurrentRevision)` — Parameterized constructor.

### Properties
- `FileName` (String, get) — Gets the revision file name.
- `Hash` (String, get) — Gets the revision hash identifier.
- `Id` (Int32, get) — Gets the revision id.
- `IsCurrentRevision` (Boolean, get) — Gets the value indicating whether this revision is the current revision or not.
- `ReferenceModelId` (Int32, get) — Gets or sets the parent reference model id.
- `Time` (DateTime, get) — Gets the revision time.

## ReferenceModel.VisibilityEnum (enum)

The visibility type.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/2af7cd9d-3835-c661-a69c-a9f2cde183b7)

### Values
- `HIDDEN` = `0` — The reference model is invisible in the model.
- `VISIBLE` = `1` — The reference model is visible in the model.

## ReferenceModelObject (class)

The ReferenceModelObject class contains methods related to reference model objects. A reference model object is a subobject of a reference model.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/98ca59de-9a53-e939-e397-12158eb2f840)

### Constructors
- `public ReferenceModelObject()` — Creates a new reference model object instance.
- `public ReferenceModelObject(int ReferenceModelId, Identifier ID)` — Creates a new reference model object instance using the given parameters.

### Methods
#### `public int CompareTo(Object obj)`

Compares Identifiers of model objects.

**Parameters:**
- `obj` (System.Object) — The model object to be used in comparison.

**Returns:** `Int32` — 0 if object Identifiers are the same, 1 if given object Identifier is bigger, -1 otherwise.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/79cca356-0c8a-e8fb-463a-34ad4141bec7)

#### `public override bool Delete()`

At the moment reference objects cannot be deleted.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — An exception.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/e8ede2d8-73d3-e7bf-0348-a0ecda17896b)

#### `public bool Equals(ModelObject other)`

Check if Identifiers of model objects are same.

**Parameters:**
- `other` (Tekla.Structures.Model.ModelObject) — The model object to be used in comparison.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — true if object Identifiers are the same, false otherwise,

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/e4324265-2490-00de-4139-63a8f7acb492)

- `doubleNames` (System.Collections.ArrayList) — An array list containing the names of the double properties.
- `integerNames` (System.Collections.ArrayList) — An array list containing the names of the integer properties.

### Properties
- `Identifier` (Identifier, get/set) — The identifier of the object.
- `IsUpToDate` (object, get) — Gets if the object does not have a modification which is not shared.
- `ModificationTime` (object, get) — Gets latest time of the object was modified or created.

## Reinforcement (class)

The Reinforcement class represents a reinforcement in the model. A reinforcement can either be a mesh, a single rebar, a rebar group or a strand.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/c3d81ba0-8bd9-6e21-d36d-e650c6a0ef17)

### Methods
#### `public int CompareTo(Object obj)`

Compares Identifiers of model objects.

**Parameters:**
- `obj` (System.Object) — The model object to be used in comparison.

**Returns:** `Int32` — 0 if object Identifiers are the same, 1 if given object Identifier is bigger, -1 otherwise.

#### `public List<RebarComplexGeometry> GetRebarComplexGeometries(bool withHooks, bool withoutClashes, bool lengthAdjustments)`

Retrieves a list of physical reinforcing bars (of type RebarComplexGeometry). These objects contain physical curves in the 3D space of each reinforcing bar as shown in model view. RebarComplexGeometry only differs from RebarGeometry by containing a polycurve rather than a polyline.

**Parameters:**
- `withHooks` (System.Boolean) — A parameter to determine whether the hook points should be included in the rebar geometry or not.
- `withoutClashes` (System.Boolean) — A parameter to determine whether self-clash avoidance should be included in the rebar geometry or not.
- `lengthAdjustments` (System.Boolean) — A parameter to determine whether rounding and step tapering should be included in the rebar geometry or not.

**Returns:** `List<RebarComplexGeometry>` — A list of RebarComplexGeometrys.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/c796aafb-3520-fe06-12e3-a07a87c8f2f9)

#### `public List<RebarComplexGeometry> GetRebarComplexGeometries(bool withHooks, bool withoutClashes, bool lengthAdjustments, Reinforcement.RebarGeometrySimplificationTypeEnum simplified)`

Retrieves a list of physical reinforcing bars (of type RebarComplexGeometry). These objects contain physical curves in the 3D space of each reinforcing bar as shown in model view. RebarComplexGeometry only differs from RebarGeometry by containing a polycurve (of Arc and LineSegment objects) rather than a polyline. The polycurve only contains arcs if the "simplified" parameter is RATIONALIZED or FABRICATION. If "simplified" is NONE, any arcs will be returned as a series of line segments.

**Parameters:**
- `withHooks` (System.Boolean) — A parameter to determine whether the hook points should be included in the rebar geometry or not.
- `withoutClashes` (System.Boolean) — A parameter to determine whether self-clash avoidance should be included in the rebar geometry or not.
- `lengthAdjustments` (System.Boolean) — A parameter to determine whether rounding and step tapering should be included in the rebar geometry or not.
- `simplified` (Tekla.Structures.Model.Reinforcement.RebarGeometrySimplificationTypeEnum) — A parameter to determine whether the "simplified" rebar shape recognition geometry should be returned.

**Returns:** `List<RebarComplexGeometry>` — A list of RebarComplexGeometrys.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/84a3a38b-d7eb-97df-7fba-a34d13de6610)

#### `public ArrayList GetRebarGeometries(bool withHooks)`

Retrieves a list of physical reinforcing bars (of type RebarGeometry). These objects contain physical points in the 3D space of each reinforcing bar.

**Parameters:**
- `withHooks` (System.Boolean) — A parameter to determine whether the hook points should be included in the rebar geometry or not.

**Returns:** `ArrayList` — An array list that contains the RebarGeometry objects.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/75649a4c-6252-09a1-b509-cd815e718c0f)

#### `public ArrayList GetRebarGeometries(Reinforcement.RebarGeometryOptionEnum options)`

Retrieves a list of physical reinforcing bars (of type RebarGeometry). These objects contain physical points in the 3D space of each reinforcing bar.

**Parameters:**
- `options` (Tekla.Structures.Model.Reinforcement.RebarGeometryOptionEnum) — The options.

**Returns:** `ArrayList` — An array list that contains the RebarGeometry objects.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/bc45309d-fc4b-9bf9-c640-e71bb1ade771)

#### `public ArrayList GetRebarGeometriesWithoutClashes(bool withHooks)`

Retrieves a list of physical reinforcing bars (of type RebarGeometry). These objects contain physical points in the 3D space of each reinforcing bar as shown in model view. In case rebar polygon clashes with itself, physical points are moved to avoid clashing.

**Parameters:**
- `withHooks` (System.Boolean) — A parameter to determine whether the hook points should be included in the rebar geometry or not.

**Returns:** `ArrayList` — An array list that contains the RebarGeometry objects.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/470acf1f-1beb-f30a-a1c2-2fcab066ab4a)

#### `public RebarGeometry GetSingleRebar(int index, bool withHooks)`

Returns a single rebar inside the rebar group located by the given index. The indexing starts from the start point. The rebar represents a physical reinforcing bar and contains physical points in the 3D space of the bar. The method returns null on error, for example if given an erroneous index. The number of rebars in the group can be requested with GetNumberOfRebars().

**Parameters:**
- `index` (System.Int32) — The index of the rebar in the group.
- `withHooks` (System.Boolean) — A parameter to determine whether the hook points should be included in the rebar geometry or not.

**Returns:** `RebarGeometry` — The instance of a RebarGeometry object that contains the point list of the single physical reinforcing bar. Null if the given index was not valid.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/41181569-65f8-f4ba-7722-3cd890c7a3d4)

#### `public RebarGeometry GetSingleRebarWithoutClash(int index, bool withHooks)`

Returns a single rebar inside the rebar group located by the given index. The indexing starts from the start point. The rebar represents a physical reinforcing bar and contains physical, non-clashing points in the 3D space of the bar. The method returns null on error, for example if given an erroneous index. The number of rebars in the group can be requested with GetNumberOfRebars().

**Parameters:**
- `index` (System.Int32) — The index of the rebar in the group.
- `withHooks` (System.Boolean) — A parameter to determine whether the hook points should be included in the rebar geometry or not.

**Returns:** `RebarGeometry` — The instance of a RebarGeometry object that contains the point list of the single physical reinforcing bar. Null if the given index was not valid.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/8fd2e21a-3d2f-6211-92ba-9d6e652ac100)

### Properties
- `Class` (Int32, get/set) — Gets or sets the class of the reinforcement. The class is used to group reinforcements.
- `EndPointOffsetType` (Reinforcement.RebarOffsetTypeEnum, get/set) — Gets or sets the type of the end point offset. The options are: OFFSET_TYPE_LEG_LENGTHOFFSET_TYPE_COVER_THICKNESS
- `EndPointOffsetValue` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the concrete cover thickness or leg length at the second end of the bar.
- `Father` (ModelObject, get/set) — Gets or sets the father object of the reinforcement; the model object instance to operate on.
- `FromPlaneOffset` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the offset value from the part surface.
- `Grade` (String, get/set) — Gets or sets the steel grade of the reinforcing bar. The grade indicates the strength of the steel used in reinforcing bars. It can also indicate other factors, such as the weldability or surface deformations of the bar.
- `Identifier` (Identifier, get/set) — The identifier of the object.
- `InputPointDeformingState` (DeformingType, get/set) — Gets or sets the reinforcement input point deforming state.
- `IsUpToDate` (object, get) — Gets if the object does not have a modification which is not shared.
- `ModificationTime` (object, get) — Gets latest time of the object was modified or created.
- `Name` (String, get/set) — Gets or sets the name of the reinforcement.
- `NumberingSeries` (NumberingSeries, get/set) — Gets or sets the numbering series of the reinforcement.
- `OnPlaneOffsets` (ArrayList, get/set) — Gets or sets the double offset value for each leg on the same plane as the bar.
- `RadiusValues` (ArrayList, get/set) — Gets or sets the radius value(s) of the bends in the bar.
- `StartPointOffsetType` (Reinforcement.RebarOffsetTypeEnum, get/set) — Gets or sets the type of the start point offset is either OFFSET_TYPE_LEG_LENGTH or OFFSET_TYPE_COVER_THICKNESS.
- `StartPointOffsetValue` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the concrete cover thickness or leg length at the first end of the bar.

## Reinforcement.RebarGeometryOptionEnum (enum)

Options for the function GetRebarGeometries.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/7108e0fe-54a6-1bb5-2c14-3a461eff6bda)

### Values
- `NONE` = `0` — No hooks or clash avoidance or length adjustment.
- `HOOKS` = `1` — Hooks.
- `AVOID_CLASH` = `2` — In case rebar polygon clashes with itself, physical points are moved to avoid clashing.
- `LENGTH_ADJUSTMENTS` = `4` — Length adjustments

## Reinforcement.RebarGeometrySimplificationTypeEnum (enum)

The rebar geometry simplification type.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/9138fa19-1a9b-02bc-4710-a8856a76edc3)

### Values
- `NONE` = `0` — No simplification.
- `RATIONALIZED` = `1` — Rationalized geometry - includes segmented arcs converted to true arcs by Rebar Shape Recognition.
- `FABRICATION` = `2` — Fabrication geometry - includes arcs recognized as straight by Rebar Shape Recognition.

## Reinforcement.RebarOffsetTypeEnum (enum)

The different types of offsets.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/a0e850ad-1bda-6e5d-09b5-e64010191033)

### Values
- `OFFSET_TYPE_COVER_THICKNESS` = `0` — The concrete cover thickness.
- `OFFSET_TYPE_LEG_LENGTH` = `1` — The leg length.

## Seam (class)

The Seam class represents a seam. A seam is something that connects a main part to other parts along an edge.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/99d98d23-1040-9c9e-cab2-4d89c1e43f1e)

### Constructors
- `public Seam()` — Creates a new seam instance.

**Parameters:**
- `obj` (System.Object) — The model object to be used in comparison.

**Returns:** `Int32` — 0 if object Identifiers are the same, 1 if given object Identifier is bigger, -1 otherwise.

- `Secondaries` (System.Collections.ArrayList) — An array list containing all the secondary objects.

#### `public bool SetUserProperty(string name, string value)`

Sets a string property for the model object.

**Parameters:**
- `name` (System.String) — The name of the property to be set.
- `value` (System.String) — The new value for the property.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if the property was set successfully.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/80bf0348-b651-3d4a-862b-49c85bc924ab)

### Properties
- `AutoDirectionType` (AutoDirectionTypeEnum, get/set) — The auto direction type. Default value is AUTODIR_BASIC. Value should be AUTODIR_NA to set direction with UpVector property. Value should be AUTODIR_FROM_ATTRIBUTE_FILE to set direction with seam attribute file.
- `AutoPosition` (Boolean, get/set) — The auto position type. Defines that the seam will internally evaluate the final position more accurately. The input positions are still needed for reference.
- `Class` (Int32, get/set) — The class of the seam.
- `Code` (String, get/set) — The code of the seam. The code can be used to classify the seam. The code can be reported and shown in drawings. The maximum length of the string is 20 characters.
- `Identifier` (Identifier, get/set) — The identifier of the object.
- `IsUpToDate` (object, get) — Gets if the object does not have a modification which is not shared.
- `ModificationTime` (object, get) — Gets latest time of the object was modified or created.
- `Name` (String, get/set) — The name of the component. The name identifies custom components or plug-ins.
- `Number` (Int32, get/set) — The number of the component. A number greater than zero identifies system components, for custom components the number is CUSTOM_OBJECT_NUMBER, and for plug-ins the number is PLUGIN_OBJECT_NUMBER.
- `Status` (ConnectionStatusEnum, get) — The read only status of the seam. The status can be reported and shown in drawings. The color of the connection symbol in the model indicates the status of the seam.
- `UpVector` (Vector, get/set) — A vector indicating which direction is considered the up direction for the seam.

## SelfIntersectingSurfaceException (class)

This class represent an exception thrown when the an operation on a lofted plate has been determined to cause a self intersecting surface

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/9f7aca96-ef41-ae5f-d61a-43d8bc994304)

### Constructors
- `public SelfIntersectingSurfaceException()` — Constructs an instance of the class

## SharedModelStateEnum (enum)

Specifies the state of a shared model in the Tekla Structures sharing service.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/6dddf61a-52ec-0c34-6290-b241c53ad2c4)

### Values
- `OFFLINE` = `0` — Service is not connected, no information about what user can do is available.
- `UNLOCKED` = `1` — Service is unlocked and current model is shared. User can read in or write out depending on it the model is up-to-date.
- `WRITELOCK` = `2` — Service is reserved for writing to another user. Current user can read in but not write out.
- `FULLLOCK` = `3` — Service is reserved for investigation to another user. Current user cannot read in or write out.
- `NEEDJOIN` = `4` — Service has current packet removed. Current local model cannot be used for sharing, model need to be joined again.

## SharingResultCode (enum)

Represents the result code for a sharing operation.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/e74f760a-14ba-a630-1bf9-b9298c946280)

### Values
- `OK` = `0` — The operation completed successfully.
- `ANOTHER_OPERATION_IN_PROGRESS` = `1` — Another operation is already in progress.
- `CANCELLED` = `2` — The operation is cancelled by user.
- `WRITE_OUT_RESERVED` = `3` — Write out is reserved by a user who is not the current user.
- `READ_IN_REQUIRED_BEFORE_WRITE_OUT` = `4` — The service has versions newer than the last read-in. They should be read-in before writing out.
- `MODEL_DOES_NOT_EXIST` = `5` — A model with the specified id doen not exist on the service, or the current user is not invited to the model.
- `NO_PERMISSION` = `6` — The request operation is not permitted for the current user.
- `WRITEOUT_DATA_FOLDER_CREATION_FAILED` = `7` — The write out temporary folder could not be created.
- `WRITEOUT_ZIP_CREATION_FAILED` = `8` — The write out version packet could not be compressed.
- `JOIN_FAILED_FOLDER_EXISTS` = `9` — The requested join folder already exists.
- `SAVE_FAILED` = `10` — Saving the model failed because of reasons internal to Tekla Structures.
- `FILE_BUSY` = `11` — The operation failed because a file was opened in another program. If ErrorPath is set, it contains the file path.
- `MODEL_FOLDER_PATH_TOO_LONG` = `12` — The operation failed because the model folder path is too long. If ErrorPath is set, it contains the folder path, and PathLengthLimit contains the maximum allowed length.
- `TEMP_FOLDER_PATH_TOO_LONG` = `13` — The operation failed because the temporary folder path is too long. If ErrorPath is set, it contains the folder path, and PathLengthLimit contains the maximum allowed length.
- `FILE_PATH_TOO_LONG` = `14` — The operation failed because a file path is too long. If ErrorPath is set, it contains the file path, and PathLengthLimit contains the maximum allowed length.
- `FOLDER_CREATION_ERROR` = `15` — The operation failed because a folder could not be created. If ErrorPath is set, it contains the folder path.
- `FILE_COPY_ERROR` = `16` — The operation failed because a file could not be copied. If ErrorPath is set, it contains the file path.
- `DISK_FULL` = `17` — There is not enough disk space to complete the operation safely. Note that due to complexity of the operations, actual space requirements may be hard to predict, so the checks may make mistakes to either side.
- `UNPERMITTED_WRITE_OUT_CHANGES` = `18` — Unallowed model changes exist. The current user's role needs to be elevated or you need to join again to work with this model.
- `DATABASE_ERROR` = `19` — The operation failed because or error in database operations.
- `NO_PACKETS_AVAILABLE_TO_READ_IN` = `20` — Read in operation failed because the model is already up-to-date with latest service version.
- `MODEL_NOT_SHARED` = `21` — The operation failed because the current model is not shared.
- `CODEPAGE_UTF8` = `22` — The operation was stopped because the current system codepage is UTF-8, whic isn't supported in Tekla Structures.
- `LICENSE_CLOCK_SKEW` = `23` — The operation was stopped because the system clock is incorrect. Correct clock settings are imporrtant for proper licensing.
- `LICENSE_NOT_GRANTED` = `24` — The operation was stopped because the sharing license was not granted for the user's organisation.
- `LICENSE_QUOTA_EXCEEDED` = `25` — The operation was stopped because the sharing license has exceeded its allowed quota.
- `LICENSE_EXPIRED` = `26` — The operation was stopped because the license has expired.
- `LICENSE_DENIED` = `27` — The operation was stopped because the license was denied for the user.
- `SERVICE_CONNECTION_FAILED` = `28` — The operation failed because connection to a service or service operation failed.
- `ERROR` = `255` — The operation encountered an unspecified error. ErrorDetails and Notifications may contain more information.

## SharingResultState (enum)

Indicates a state of Tekla Structures and model after sharing operation.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/8e66d0fa-de5e-d8ac-f1df-6212dff803f8)

### Values
- `Undefined` = `0` — The underfined state. It is the default value which is also set when the code is not able to determine the current state.
- `Ready` = `1` — After a successfull operation or an error, model is still opened.
- `ModelReopened` = `2` — After an error, model is reopened to the last saved.
- `ModelClosed` = `3` — After an error, model is closed.
- `ProcessClosed` = `4` — After an error, model is closed and Structures process closes.

## SharingRole (enum)

Model sharing user roles.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/e1076cdb-18ce-ecd5-d61d-26dc647af00a)

### Values
- `None` = `0` — No role assigned.
- `AccessDenied` = `1` — Access denied to the model.
- `Viewer` = `10` — Model viewer role.
- `Editor` = `20` — Model editor role.
- `ProjectViewer` = `21` — Project viewer role.
- `Owner` = `30` — Model owner role.

## SharingUpdateType (enum)

Specifies the type of sharing update used in model sharing operations.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/c500ecb5-b84b-7a8c-3381-e1c241f93b9e)

### Values
- `Update` = `0` — Represents an update update.
- `Baseline` = `1` — Represents a baseline update.

#### `public List<RebarComplexGeometry> GetRebarComplexGeometries(bool withHooks, bool withoutClashes, bool lengthAdjustments)`

Retrieves a list of physical reinforcing bars (of type RebarComplexGeometry). These objects contain physical curves in the 3D space of each reinforcing bar as shown in model view. RebarComplexGeometry only differs from RebarGeometry by containing a polycurve rather than a polyline.

**Parameters:**
- `withHooks` (System.Boolean) — A parameter to determine whether the hook points should be included in the rebar geometry or not.
- `withoutClashes` (System.Boolean) — A parameter to determine whether self-clash avoidance should be included in the rebar geometry or not.
- `lengthAdjustments` (System.Boolean) — A parameter to determine whether rounding and step tapering should be included in the rebar geometry or not.

**Returns:** `List<RebarComplexGeometry>` — A list of RebarComplexGeometrys.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/c796aafb-3520-fe06-12e3-a07a87c8f2f9)

#### `public List<RebarComplexGeometry> GetRebarComplexGeometries(bool withHooks, bool withoutClashes, bool lengthAdjustments, Reinforcement.RebarGeometrySimplificationTypeEnum simplified)`

Retrieves a list of physical reinforcing bars (of type RebarComplexGeometry). These objects contain physical curves in the 3D space of each reinforcing bar as shown in model view. RebarComplexGeometry only differs from RebarGeometry by containing a polycurve (of Arc and LineSegment objects) rather than a polyline. The polycurve only contains arcs if the "simplified" parameter is RATIONALIZED or FABRICATION. If "simplified" is NONE, any arcs will be returned as a series of line segments.

**Parameters:**
- `withHooks` (System.Boolean) — A parameter to determine whether the hook points should be included in the rebar geometry or not.
- `withoutClashes` (System.Boolean) — A parameter to determine whether self-clash avoidance should be included in the rebar geometry or not.
- `lengthAdjustments` (System.Boolean) — A parameter to determine whether rounding and step tapering should be included in the rebar geometry or not.
- `simplified` (Tekla.Structures.Model.Reinforcement.RebarGeometrySimplificationTypeEnum) — A parameter to determine whether the "simplified" rebar shape recognition geometry should be returned.

**Returns:** `List<RebarComplexGeometry>` — A list of RebarComplexGeometrys.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/84a3a38b-d7eb-97df-7fba-a34d13de6610)

#### `public ArrayList GetRebarGeometries(bool withHooks)`

Retrieves a list of physical reinforcing bars (of type RebarGeometry). These objects contain physical points in the 3D space of each reinforcing bar.

**Parameters:**
- `withHooks` (System.Boolean) — A parameter to determine whether the hook points should be included in the rebar geometry or not.

**Returns:** `ArrayList` — An array list that contains the RebarGeometry objects.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/75649a4c-6252-09a1-b509-cd815e718c0f)

#### `public RebarGeometry GetSingleRebar(int index, bool withHooks)`

Returns a single rebar inside the rebar group located by the given index. The indexing starts from the start point. The rebar represents a physical reinforcing bar and contains physical points in the 3D space of the bar. The method returns null on error, for example if given an erroneous index. The number of rebars in the group can be requested with GetNumberOfRebars().

**Parameters:**
- `index` (System.Int32) — The index of the rebar in the group.
- `withHooks` (System.Boolean) — A parameter to determine whether the hook points should be included in the rebar geometry or not.

**Returns:** `RebarGeometry` — The instance of a RebarGeometry object that contains the point list of the single physical reinforcing bar. Null if the given index was not valid.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/41181569-65f8-f4ba-7722-3cd890c7a3d4)

#### `public RebarGeometry GetSingleRebarWithoutClash(int index, bool withHooks)`

Returns a single rebar inside the rebar group located by the given index. The indexing starts from the start point. The rebar represents a physical reinforcing bar and contains physical, non-clashing points in the 3D space of the bar. The method returns null on error, for example if given an erroneous index. The number of rebars in the group can be requested with GetNumberOfRebars().

**Parameters:**
- `index` (System.Int32) — The index of the rebar in the group.
- `withHooks` (System.Boolean) — A parameter to determine whether the hook points should be included in the rebar geometry or not.

**Returns:** `RebarGeometry` — The instance of a RebarGeometry object that contains the point list of the single physical reinforcing bar. Null if the given index was not valid.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/8fd2e21a-3d2f-6211-92ba-9d6e652ac100)

#### `public Solid GetSolid()`

Method for getting the solid information of the reinforcement.

**Returns:** `Solid` — The solid instance of the reinforcement.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/6717d119-97da-5554-f359-49ee55e65a63)

#### `public bool SetUserProperty(string name, int value)`

Sets an integer property for the model object.

**Parameters:**
- `name` (System.String) — The name of the property to be set.

### Properties
- `Class` (Int32, get/set) — Gets or sets the class of the reinforcement. The class is used to group reinforcements.
- `EndHook` (RebarHookData, get/set) — The hook at the end of the reinforcing bar.
- `EndPointOffsetType` (Reinforcement.RebarOffsetTypeEnum, get/set) — Gets or sets the type of the end point offset. The options are: OFFSET_TYPE_LEG_LENGTHOFFSET_TYPE_COVER_THICKNESS
- `EndPointOffsetValue` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the concrete cover thickness or leg length at the second end of the bar.
- `Father` (ModelObject, get/set) — Gets or sets the father object of the reinforcement; the model object instance to operate on.
- `FromPlaneOffset` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the offset value from the part surface.
- `Grade` (String, get/set) — Gets or sets the steel grade of the reinforcing bar. The grade indicates the strength of the steel used in reinforcing bars. It can also indicate other factors, such as the weldability or surface deformations of the bar.
- `Identifier` (Identifier, get/set) — The identifier of the object.
- `InputPointDeformingState` (DeformingType, get/set) — Gets or sets the reinforcement input point deforming state.
- `IsUpToDate` (object, get) — Gets if the object does not have a modification which is not shared.
- `ModificationTime` (object, get) — Gets latest time of the object was modified or created.
- `Name` (String, get/set) — Gets or sets the name of the reinforcement.
- `NumberingSeries` (NumberingSeries, get/set) — Gets or sets the numbering series of the reinforcement.
- `OnPlaneOffsets` (ArrayList, get/set) — Gets or sets the double offset value for each leg on the same plane as the bar.
- `Polygon` (Polygon, get/set) — The polygon of the reinforcing bar.
- `RadiusValues` (ArrayList, get/set) — Gets or sets the radius value(s) of the bends in the bar.
- `Size` (String, get/set) — The size of the reinforcement.
- `StartHook` (RebarHookData, get/set) — The hook at the beginning of the reinforcing bar.
- `StartPointOffsetType` (Reinforcement.RebarOffsetTypeEnum, get/set) — Gets or sets the type of the start point offset is either OFFSET_TYPE_LEG_LENGTH or OFFSET_TYPE_COVER_THICKNESS.
- `StartPointOffsetValue` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the concrete cover thickness or leg length at the first end of the bar.

## Solid (class)

The Solid class represents the physical object in the model created by a part instance. A solid instance can be used to query the actual geometry of the part and intersect that geometry with, for example, lines and planes.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/7d939b20-a22f-7370-299b-a5a9fee8e54e)

### Methods
#### `public IEnumerator GetAllIntersectionPoints(Point point1, Point point2, Point point3)`

Used to get all the intersection points between the solid and a plane. Does not arrange the points into polygons, thus a lot faster.

**Parameters:**
- `point1` (Tekla.Structures.Geometry3d.Point) — 1st of the 3 points defining a plane
- `point2` (Tekla.Structures.Geometry3d.Point) — 2nd of the 3 points defining a plane
- `point3` (Tekla.Structures.Geometry3d.Point) — 3rd of the 3 points defining a plane

**Returns:** `IEnumerator` — An IEnumerator containing the intersection points of type the Point

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/75e5f4f1-9b46-fce4-7cab-b7033817cc3e)

#### `public ShellEnumerator GetCutPart(Solid CuttingPart)`

Returns a shell enumerator for the solid th at results cutting this solid with the given solid.

**Parameters:**
- `CuttingPart` (Tekla.Structures.Model.Solid) — The solid to use for cutting.

**Returns:** `ShellEnumerator` — An shell enumerator to enumerate through the resulting solid's shells.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/6df0f96a-ce73-cec1-6320-f3b7ddeae3cf)

#### `public EdgeEnumerator GetEdgeEnumerator()`

Returns a new edge enumerator in the current plane.

**Returns:** `EdgeEnumerator` — An edge enumerator to enumerate through the solid's global edges.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/222fbb93-f13f-f7af-0d51-a8b0018fe12d)

#### `public FaceEnumerator GetFaceEnumerator()`

Returns a new face enumerator in the current plane.

**Returns:** `FaceEnumerator` — A face enumerator to enumerate through solid faces.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/a760c670-0718-ebd9-2535-86c4df113e2c)

#### `public ArrayList Intersect(LineSegment line)`

Returns a list of line - solid intersection points.

**Parameters:**
- `line` (Tekla.Structures.Geometry3d.LineSegment) — The intersection line to be used.

**Returns:** `ArrayList` — A list of intersection points.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/b60a7604-e5c4-cf74-77e0-34139eec5d9a)

#### `public ArrayList Intersect(Point point1, Point point2)`

Returns a list of line - solid intersection points.

**Parameters:**
- `point1` (Tekla.Structures.Geometry3d.Point) — The first end of the intersection line.
- `point2` (Tekla.Structures.Geometry3d.Point) — The second end of the intersection line.

**Returns:** `ArrayList` — A list of intersection points.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/829d834d-424a-034e-c903-9cccf1759304)

#### `[ObsoleteAttribute("Intersect is deprecated, please use IntersectAllFaces instead.")] public ArrayList Intersect(Point point1, Point point2, Point point3)`

Returns a list of plane - solid intersection points. The first item of the list contains a list of the outmost intersection polygon and the rest of the items (if there are any) inner polygons.

**Parameters:**
- `point1` (Tekla.Structures.Geometry3d.Point) — The first plane point.
- `point2` (Tekla.Structures.Geometry3d.Point) — The second plane point.
- `point3` (Tekla.Structures.Geometry3d.Point) — The third plane point.

**Returns:** `ArrayList` — An array list of intersection point lists.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/89e188c7-dedb-4439-db95-78e3a442bebf)

#### `public IEnumerator IntersectAllFaces(Point point1, Point point2, Point point3)`

Returns an enumerator for an array list of lists of plane - solid intersection points from all intersecting faces. The first item of one list contains points of the outmost intersection polygon and then the inner polygons (if there are any).

**Parameters:**
- `point1` (Tekla.Structures.Geometry3d.Point) — The first plane point.
- `point2` (Tekla.Structures.Geometry3d.Point) — The second plane point.
- `point3` (Tekla.Structures.Geometry3d.Point) — The third plane point.

**Returns:** `IEnumerator` — An enumerator for an array list of intersection point lists.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/223e9ea0-6f38-e3b0-f323-574838c399aa)

#### `public bool IsValid()`

Returns if the solid is valid.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — The validity of the solid.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/4b7bd339-892a-5872-8156-d761323779cf)

### Properties
- `MaximumPoint` (Point, get) — Gets the maximum axis-aligned point of the solid in the current plane.
- `MinimumPoint` (Point, get) — Gets the minimum axis-aligned point of the solid in the current plane.

## Solid.SolidCreationTypeEnum (enum)

The creation type of the solid.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/91495004-3272-05f8-52b1-cdc3d386ea19)

### Values
- `RAW` = `0` — The solid is created without any boolean operations.
- `FITTED` = `1` — The solid is created with fittings.
- `NORMAL` = `2` — The solid is created including all cutting and fitting operations.
- `HIGH_ACCURACY` = `3` — The solid is created as normal with an exact profile cross section.
- `PLANECUTTED` = `4` — The solid is created with fittings and cut planes.
- `NORMAL_WITHOUT_EDGECHAMFERS` = `5` — The solid is created including all cutting and fitting operations without edge chamfers.
- `NORMAL_WITHOUT_WELDPREPS` = `6` — The solid is created including all cutting and fitting operations without weld prepping.

## SpiralBeam (class)

A class for the spiral beam part.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/2b829f38-d9b1-d5ea-f9b5-68801bbfdcd3)

### Constructors
- `public SpiralBeam()` — Initializes a new instance of the SpiralBeam class.
- `public SpiralBeam(Point startPoint, Point rotationAxisPoint, double totalRise, double rotationAngle)` — Initializes a new instance of the SpiralBeam class with the given parameters. Rotation axis direction is current positive Z direction.
- `public SpiralBeam(Point startPoint, Point rotationAxisBasePoint, Point rotationAxisUpPoint, double totalRise, double rotationAngle)` — Initializes a new instance of the SpiralBeam class with the given parameters.
- `public SpiralBeam(Point startPoint, Point rotationAxisBasePoint, Point rotationAxisUpPoint, double totalRise, double rotationAngle, double twistAngleAtStart, double twistAngleAtEnd)` — Initializes a new instance of the SpiralBeam class with the given parameters.

### Methods

Compares Identifiers of model objects.

**Parameters:**
- `obj` (System.Object) — The model object to be used in comparison.

**Returns:** `Int32` — 0 if object Identifiers are the same, 1 if given object Identifier is bigger, -1 otherwise.

Compares the instantiated part with another one.

- `partToCompare` (Tekla.Structures.Model.Part) — The part to compare with.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if there are no differences, false otherwise.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/e8632c0d-89cf-9aab-6532-f62f9873067b)

### Properties
- `AssemblyNumber` (NumberingSeries, get/set) — Gets or sets the assembly number. Defines the numbering in the assembly sense.
- `CastUnitType` (Part.CastUnitTypeEnum, get/set) — Gets or sets the cast unit type of the part.
- `Class` (String, get/set) — Gets or sets the class of the part.
- `DeformingData` (DeformingData, get/set) — Gets or sets the deforming data of the part.
- `EndPoint` (Point, get) — Gets the spiral beam end point.
- `Finish` (String, get/set) — Gets or sets the finish of the part.
- `Identifier` (Identifier, get/set) — The identifier of the object.
- `IsUpToDate` (object, get) — Gets if the object does not have a modification which is not shared.
- `Material` (Material, get/set) — Gets or sets the material the part is made of.
- `ModificationTime` (object, get) — Gets latest time of the object was modified or created.
- `Name` (String, get/set) — Gets or sets the name of the part.
- `PartNumber` (NumberingSeries, get/set) — Gets or sets the part number. Defines the numbering in the part sense.
- `Position` (Position, get/set) — Gets or sets the part position. Defines the way the part is positioned in the model.
- `PourPhase` (Int32, get/set) — Gets or sets the pour phase of the part.
- `Profile` (Profile, get/set) — Gets or sets the profile of the part.
- `RotationAngle` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the rotation of the spiral beam in degrees. Each range of 360.0 degrees adds a full rotation.
- `RotationAxisBasePoint` (Point, get/set) — Gets or sets the first definition point of the spiral beam rotation axis.
- `RotationAxisDirection` (Vector, get) — Gets the direction of the spiral beam rotation axis.
- `RotationAxisUpPoint` (Point, get/set) — Gets or sets the second definition point of the spiral beam rotation axis.
- `RotationCenterPoint` (Point, get) — Gets the geometric rotation center point at the start of the spiral beam.
- `StartPoint` (Point, get/set) — Gets or sets the start point of the spiral beam.
- `TotalRise` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the difference between the spiral beam start and end point in the direction of the rotation axis.
- `TwistAngleEnd` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the twist angle in degrees at the end of the spiral beam.
- `TwistAngleStart` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the twist angle in degrees at the start of the spiral beam.

## SpiralBeam.ErrorStatus (enum)

Define possible statuses for geometry creation/modification commands.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/96dd1b8d-e2ec-e21a-a908-854a9748bd21)

### Values
- `DataMissing` = `0` — Status when some part data is missing.
- `DefinitionPointsTooClose` = `1` — Status when definition points are too close.
- `DefinitionPointsCannotBeAligned` = `2` — Status when definition points are on line with sweep direction.
- `ZeroTotalRiseWithMore360Degrees` = `3` — Status when total rise is zero and rotation angle more than 360 degrees.
- `RotationAngleIs0Degrees` = `4` — Status when rotation angle is 0 degrees.
- `InvalidGeometry` = `5` — Status when helix has invalid geometry.

## SpiralBeamDataException (class)

The SpiralBeamDataException class represents an error that occurred during the spiral beam creation or modification.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/031dfd58-7798-4ded-5a3a-3e35e02069d8)

### Constructors
- `public SpiralBeamDataException(SpiralBeam.ErrorStatus status, string message)` — Initializes a new instance of the SpiralBeamDataException class with spiral beam error message based on status.

## StrandUnbondingData (class)

The StrandUnbondingData class represents the unbonding of a rebar strand.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/b532c1be-5eec-7820-26ff-12e6061d7c05)

### Constructors
- `public StrandUnbondingData()` — Initializes a new strand unbonding data instance with empty attributes.

### Properties
- `FromEnd` (Double, get/set) — The unbonding from the end of the strand.
- `FromStart` (Double, get/set) — The unbonding from the start of the strand.
- `MiddleToEnd` (Double, get/set) — The unbonding from the middle of the strand to the end of the strand.
- `MiddleToStart` (Double, get/set) — The unbonding from the middle of the strand to the start of the strand.
- `StrandIndex` (Int32, get/set) — The index of the strand.

## SurfaceObject (class)

The SurfaceObject class represents a surface object in the model. Currently this class only support dynamic surfaces whose geometries are determined by the parts or pours they associate to. It is not allowed to modify the geometry of a dynamic surface on Open API. The polymesh setter of this class throws .

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/03dc457c-9884-1f10-6a99-390c401463b7)

### Constructors
- `public SurfaceObject()` — Initializes a new instance of the SurfaceObject class.

### Methods
#### `public int CompareTo(Object obj)`

Compares Identifiers of model objects.

**Parameters:**
- `obj` (System.Object) — The model object to be used in comparison.

**Returns:** `Int32` — 0 if object Identifiers are the same, 1 if given object Identifier is bigger, -1 otherwise.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/79cca356-0c8a-e8fb-463a-34ad4141bec7)

### Properties
- `Class` (String, get/set) — Gets or sets the class of the surface object.
- `CreateHoles` (Boolean, get/set) — Gets or sets a value indicating whether CreateHoles parameter of the surface object.
- `Father` (ModelObject, get/set) — Gets or sets the father object of the surface object. The father can be set only before the Insert(), otherwise exception is thrown.
- `Identifier` (Identifier, get/set) — The identifier of the object.
- `IsUpToDate` (object, get) — Gets if the object does not have a modification which is not shared.
- `ModificationTime` (object, get) — Gets latest time of the object was modified or created.
- `Name` (String, get/set) — Gets or sets the name of the surface object.
- `Polymesh` (FacetedBrep, get/set) — Gets or sets the surface geometry brep.
- `Type` (String, get/set) — Gets or sets the type of the surface object.

## SurfaceTreatment (class)

The SurfaceTreatment class defines a layer above a building element defining what a surface looks like. The surface could for example have a tiled look.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/6726c2c8-455a-71c3-8c81-3443c27018f9)

### Constructors
- `public SurfaceTreatment()` — Creates a new surface treatment instance.

**Parameters:**
- `obj` (System.Object) — The model object to be used in comparison.

**Returns:** `Int32` — 0 if object Identifiers are the same, 1 if given object Identifier is bigger, -1 otherwise.

### Properties
- `Class` (String, get/set) — The class of the surface treatment.
- `Color` (SurfaceTreatment.SurfaceColorEnum, get/set) — The color of the surfacing.
- `CutByFatherBooleans` (Boolean, get/set) — Defines if the part is cut by the father's cuts.
- `EndPoint` (Point, get/set) — Determines the direction the surfacing is filled in. The direction is from the start point towards the end point.
- `Father` (Part, get/set) — Defines the father part for the surface treatment: the part that the surface treatment is created on.
- `Identifier` (Identifier, get/set) — The identifier of the object.
- `IsUpToDate` (object, get) — Gets if the object does not have a modification which is not shared.
- `Material` (Material, get/set) — Defines the material for the surface treatment.
- `ModificationTime` (object, get) — Gets latest time of the object was modified or created.
- `Name` (String, get/set) — The name of the surface treatment.
- `Polygon` (Contour, get/set) — Defines the area for the surface treatment.
- `Position` (Position, get/set) — Defines the position for the surface treatment.
- `StartPoint` (Point, get/set) — Determines the origin of the surfacing. The surfacing is then filled out from the start point towards the end point.
- `Thickness` (Double, get/set) — Defines how thick the surface treatment is.
- `Type` (SurfaceTreatment.SurfaceTypeEnum, get/set) — The type of the surfacing.
- `TypeName` (String, get/set) — The name of the surface treatment type. The valid names are defined in the product_finishes.dat file.

## SurfaceTreatment.SurfaceColorEnum (enum)

The different surfacing colors.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/220357a1-749a-c75a-62cc-651b54fe5e1f)

### Values
- `WHITE` = `1` — The white surface color.
- `RED` = `2` — The red surface color.
- `GREEN` = `3` — The green surface color.
- `BLUE` = `4` — The blue surface color.
- `CYAN` = `5` — The cyan surface color.
- `YELLOW` = `6` — The yellow surface color.
- `MAGENTA` = `7` — The magenta surface color.

## SurfaceTreatment.SurfaceTypeEnum (enum)

The different surfacing types.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/3f55bbef-2e2a-3aac-7081-a9e9fd68a52e)

### Values
- `CONCRETE_FINISH` = `1` — The concrete finish surface type.
- `SPECIAL_MIX` = `2` — The special mix surface type.
- `TILE_SURFACE` = `3` — The tile surface type.
- `STEEL_FINISH` = `4` — The steel finish surface type.

## Task (class)

The Task class defines a single building site task. It may contain parts, assemblies or other tasks. Tasks may have a hierarchy between them i.e. there are other tasks as subtasks for a parent task. Tasks may also depend on each other, have resources assigned to them or have a single worktype.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/c36a21f1-71b0-e3ab-8db5-2f10414db8bb)

### Constructors
- `public Task()` — Creates a new task instance.
- `public Task(Identifier ID)` — Creates a task instance with a known identifier. Select the task after the creation.

### Methods
#### `public bool AddObjectsToTask(ArrayList ModelObjects)`

Adds objects to the task.

**Parameters:**
- `ModelObjects` (System.Collections.ArrayList) — An array list of model objects to be added to the task.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True on success.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/d2f3929b-a826-934f-1336-10d85ce949ef)

#### `public int CompareTo(Object obj)`

Compares Identifiers of model objects.

### Properties
- `ActualEndDate` (DateTime, get/set) — The actual end date of the task.
- `ActualStartDate` (DateTime, get/set) — The actual start date of the task.
- `ActualWorkAmount` (Double, get/set) — The amount of work already used for the task.
- `Completeness` (Int32, get/set) — The percentage of the completeness of the task on the scale from 0 to 100.
- `Critical` (Boolean, get/set) — The criticality of the task.
- `Description` (String, get/set) — A short textual description of the task.
- `Identifier` (Identifier, get/set) — The identifier of the object.
- `IsUpToDate` (object, get) — Gets if the object does not have a modification which is not shared.
- `Local` (Boolean, get/set) — The locality indicates if the task was created in Tekla Structures and Task Manager or imported.
- `ModificationTime` (object, get) — Gets latest time of the object was modified or created.
- `Name` (String, get/set) — The name of the task.
- `PlannedEndDate` (DateTime, get/set) — The planned end date of the task.
- `PlannedStartDate` (DateTime, get/set) — The planned start date of the task.
- `PlannedWorkAmount` (Double, get/set) — The amount of work planned to be used for the task.
- `Scenario` (HierarchicObject, get/set) — The scenario which the task belongs to.
- `Url` (String, get/set) — A link to material or data related to the task. The link can be a hyperlink or a file system link.

## TaskDependency (class)

The TaskDependency class represents a dependency between two task objects. Each task may depend on many other tasks, but between two specific tasks only one type of a dependency may exist. There are four types of dependencies: FINISH_TO_FINISH (FF)FINISH_TO_START (FS)START_TO_FINISH (SF)START_TO_START (SS)

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/2a999834-8d4f-42de-83fc-d66316056fc2)

### Constructors
- `public TaskDependency()` — Creates a new task dependency instance.
- `public TaskDependency(Task primary, Task secondary)` — Creates a new task dependency instance with the given primary and secondary tasks.

**Returns:** `Int32` — 0 if object Identifiers are the same, 1 if given object Identifier is bigger, -1 otherwise.

### Properties
- `DependencyType` (TaskDependency.DependencyTypeEnum, get/set) — The type of the task dependency.
- `Identifier` (Identifier, get/set) — The identifier of the object.
- `IsUpToDate` (object, get) — Gets if the object does not have a modification which is not shared.
- `Lag` (Int32, get/set) — The lag of the task dependency represented in whole hours.
- `Local` (Boolean, get/set) — The locality indicates if the dependency was created in Tekla Structures and Task Manager or imported.
- `ModificationTime` (object, get) — Gets latest time of the object was modified or created.
- `Primary` (Task, get/set) — The primary task of the task dependency.
- `Secondary` (Task, get/set) — The secondary task of the task dependency.

## TaskDependency.DependencyTypeEnum (enum)

The task dependency types.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/51995429-984b-e28d-1b25-adbb3a83fe37)

### Values
- `FINISH_TO_FINISH` = `0` — The task dependency type finish-to-finish.
- `FINISH_TO_START` = `1` — The task dependency type finish-to-start.
- `START_TO_FINISH` = `2` — The task dependency type start-to-finish.
- `START_TO_START` = `3` — The task dependency type start-to-start.

## TaskWorktype (class)

The TaskWorktype class defines a single worktype for a task object. Each task may belong to only one worktype.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/97bac05f-2188-ce24-1011-6e2e97cc8e3a)

### Constructors
- `public TaskWorktype()` — Creates a new task worktype instance.

- `Name` (String, get/set) — The name of the task worktype.

## TransformationPlane (class)

The TransformationPlane class describes a transformation from global model coordinates to local and back to global.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/324b1565-2e6e-8847-6c8b-88ca12e2151d)

### Constructors
- `public TransformationPlane()` — Creates a new identity transformation using the global coordinate system.
- `public TransformationPlane(CoordinateSystem CoordinateSystem)` — Creates a new transformation plane defined by the given coordinate system.
- `public TransformationPlane(Point Origo, Vector Xvector, Vector Yvector)` — Creates a new transformation plane defined by the given origin and two vectors.

### Methods
#### `public override string ToString()`

Returns a string representation of the matrices that transform to local and global coordinates.

**Returns:** `String` — A string representation of the matrices that transform to local and global coordinates.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/5a396a55-b509-6d5c-71cc-d4cd1861e3a6)

### Properties
- `TransformationMatrixToGlobal` (Matrix, get) — Gives a transformation matrix for converting local points in the transformation plane to global coordinates.
- `TransformationMatrixToLocal` (Matrix, get) — Gives a transformation matrix for converting points in the global plane to local coordinates in the transformation plane.

## UndefinedCurveDirectionException (class)

The UndefinedCurveDirectionException class represents an error when, cannot make inward/outward curve check.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/b4d572bd-333a-26f2-458c-d688d2ca0c4e)

### Constructors
- `public UndefinedCurveDirectionException()` — Initializes a new instance of the UndefinedCurveDirectionException class with "Cannot make inward/outward curve check" error message.

## UnknownLoftedPlateErrorException (class)

This class represent an exception thrown when an operation on a lofted plate caused an error of unknown origin

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/1b7dc228-870a-7be6-0bb6-cfc92a24ab32)

### Constructors
- `public UnknownLoftedPlateErrorException()` — Constructs an instance of the class

## UnsupportedChamferException (class)

The UnsupportedChamferException class represents an error when, plates containing unsupported chamfer information.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/7ab3fa5a-0958-525d-91a2-87eaf92f798d)

### Constructors
- `public UnsupportedChamferException()` — Initializes a new instance of the UnsupportedChamferException class with "Plates containing unsupported chamfer information" error message.

## Weld (class)

The Weld class represents a normal weld in the model. A normal weld has a main part and a secondary part.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/0acd5a34-eab5-0e9a-4869-d7f825f3a608)

### Constructors
- `public Weld()` — Creates a new weld instance.

### Methods
#### `public int CompareTo(Object obj)`

Compares Identifiers of model objects.

**Parameters:**
- `obj` (System.Object) — The model object to be used in comparison.

**Returns:** `Int32` — 0 if object Identifiers are the same, 1 if given object Identifier is bigger, -1 otherwise.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/79cca356-0c8a-e8fb-463a-34ad4141bec7)

#### `public override bool Insert()`

Inserts the weld into the model database. All the attributes must be set.

Modifies the existing weld in the model database to match the current one. The modification cannot be done if the weld is a part of a logical weld.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True on success.

### Properties
- `AdditionalSizeAbove` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the additional size above for combination welds.
- `AdditionalSizeBelow` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the additional size below for combination welds.
- `AngleAbove` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the angle above.
- `AngleBelow` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the angle below.
- `AroundWeld` (Boolean, get/set) — Gets or sets a value indicating whether the weld is an around weld (true) or an edge weld (false).
- `ConnectAssemblies` (Boolean, get/set) — Gets or sets a value indicating whether to connect a part or an assembly as a secondary part (false) or as a sub-assembly (true).
- `ContourAbove` (BaseWeld.WeldContourEnum, get/set) — Gets or sets the contour above.
- `ContourBelow` (BaseWeld.WeldContourEnum, get/set) — Gets or sets the contour below.
- `Direction` (Vector, get/set) — Defines the Weld edge search direction normalized to length 1000.0. Overrides Position if set.
- `EffectiveThroatAbove` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the effective throat above.
- `EffectiveThroatBelow` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the effective throat below.
- `ElectrodeClassification` (BaseWeld.WeldElectrodeClassificationEnum, get/set) — Gets or sets the weld electrode classification.
- `ElectrodeCoefficient` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the electrode strength coefficient.
- `ElectrodeStrength` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the electrode strength.
- `FinishAbove` (BaseWeld.WeldFinishEnum, get/set) — Gets or sets the finish above.
- `FinishBelow` (BaseWeld.WeldFinishEnum, get/set) — Gets or sets the finish below.
- `Identifier` (Identifier, get/set) — The identifier of the object.
- `IncrementAmountAbove` (Int32, get/set) — Gets or sets the increment amount above.
- `IncrementAmountBelow` (Int32, get/set) — Gets or sets the increment amount below.
- `IntermittentType` (BaseWeld.WeldIntermittentTypeEnum, get/set) — Gets or sets the weld intermittent type.
- `IsUpToDate` (object, get) — Gets if the object does not have a modification which is not shared.
- `LengthAbove` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the length above.
- `LengthBelow` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the length below.
- `MainObject` (ModelObject, get/set) — Gets or sets the main part of the weld.
- `ModificationTime` (object, get) — Gets latest time of the object was modified or created.
- `NDTInspection` (BaseWeld.WeldNDTInspectionEnum, get/set) — Gets or sets the NDT inspection level.
- `PitchAbove` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the pitch above.
- `PitchBelow` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the pitch below.
- `Placement` (BaseWeld.WeldPlacementTypeEnum, get/set) — Gets or sets the weld placement.
- `Position` (Weld.WeldPositionEnum, get/set) — Defines the position. Use of Direction is preferred over this.
- `PrefixAboveLine` (String, get/set) — Gets or sets the size prefix above the line.
- `PrefixBelowLine` (String, get/set) — Gets or sets the size prefix below the line.
- `Preparation` (BaseWeld.WeldPreparationTypeEnum, get/set) — Gets or sets the weld preparation.
- `ProcessType` (BaseWeld.WeldProcessTypeEnum, get/set) — Gets or sets the process type.
- `ReferenceText` (String, get/set) — Gets or sets the reference text.
- `RootFaceAbove` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the root face above.
- `RootFaceBelow` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the root face below.
- `RootOpeningAbove` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the root opening above.
- `RootOpeningBelow` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the root opening below.
- `SecondaryObject` (ModelObject, get/set) — Gets or sets the secondary part of the weld.
- `ShopWeld` (Boolean, get/set) — Gets or sets a value indicating whether the weld is a shop weld (true) or a site weld (false).
- `SizeAbove` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the size above.
- `SizeBelow` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the size below.
- `Standard` (String, get/set) — Gets or sets the weld detail/standard.
- `StitchWeld` (Boolean, get/set) — Gets or sets a value indicating whether the weld is stitched (true) or not stitched (false).
- `TypeAbove` (BaseWeld.WeldTypeEnum, get/set) — Gets or sets the type above.
- `TypeBelow` (BaseWeld.WeldTypeEnum, get/set) — Gets or sets the type below.
- `WeldNumber` (Int32, get) — Gets the weld number.
- `WeldNumberPrefix` (String, get/set) — Gets or sets the weld number prefix.

## Weld.WeldPositionEnum (enum)

The weld position.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/845f121d-e5ce-cfe1-1467-bd657060b631)

### Values
- `WELD_POSITION_PLUS_X` = `1` — The position +x.
- `WELD_POSITION_MINUS_X` = `2` — The position -x.
- `WELD_POSITION_PLUS_Y` = `3` — The position +y.
- `WELD_POSITION_MINUS_Y` = `4` — The position -y.
- `WELD_POSITION_PLUS_Z` = `5` — The position +z.
- `WELD_POSITION_MINUS_Z` = `6` — The position -z.

## WorkPlaneHandler (class)

The WorkPlaneHandler class contains methods for getting and setting the current transformation plane.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/ac187147-19d4-851c-7af5-19e217534818)

### Methods
#### `public TransformationPlane GetCurrentTransformationPlane()`

Gets the current transformation plane.

**Returns:** `TransformationPlane` — The current transformation plane.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/74206331-60ff-3061-ce71-526788aee687)

#### `public bool SetCurrentTransformationPlane(TransformationPlane TransformationPlane)`

Sets the current transformation plane.

**Remarks:** Please note that in order to see workplane change visible in the model, use the RedrawWorkplane() method of the ViewHandler class.

**Parameters:**
- `TransformationPlane` (Tekla.Structures.Model.TransformationPlane) — The transformation plane to be set as the current transformation plane.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — False on failure.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/47/e6f659a2-cc4a-9e35-fbde-78769210ab87)
